```
<path>/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/PDFAssembler.java</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
4: import java.io.File;
5: import java.io.IOException;
6: import java.util.List;
7: 
8: import org.apache.pdfbox.Loader;
9: import org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSName;
10: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;
11: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPage;
12: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPageContentStream;
13: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.PDRectangle;
14: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDType1Font;
15: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.Standard14Fonts;
16: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.CCITTFactory;
17: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.LosslessFactory;
18: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.PDImageXObject;
19: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.state.RenderingMode;
20: 
21: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;
22: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.TextBlock;
23: 
24: /**
25:  * Re-assembles a searchable PDF from per-page inputs:
26:  *   - Source PDF (copied for page dimensions / media boxes)
27:  *   - Cleaned background images
28:  *   - Binary foreground masks (CCITT G4 compressed stencil overlay)
29:  *   - OCR results (invisible text layer)
30:  *
31:  * MRC-like layout (per page):
32:  *   1. Background layer: cleaned page image (Lossless PNG for now).
33:  *   2. Foreground mask: CCITT G4 compressed binary mask, drawn as a
34:  *      PDF ImageMask stencil in black — reveals only the text pixels
35:  *      on top of the background.
36:  *   3. Text layer: invisible OCR text (RenderingMode.NEITHER = Tr 3),
37:  *      selectable and searchable but not visible.
38:  *
39:  * Rendering mode NEITHER makes text invisible on screen/print while
40:  * keeping it selectable and searchable.
41:  *
42:  * Coordinate transformation:
43:  *   Page images at 300 DPI → PDF user space at 72 DPI.
44:  *   scaleX = pageWidth_pts  / imageWidth_px
45:  *   scaleY = pageHeight_pts / imageHeight_px
46:  *   Y is flipped: image origin is top-left, PDF origin is bottom-left.
47:  */
48: public class PDFAssembler {
49: 
50:     private static final PDType1Font FONT = new PDType1Font(Standard14Fonts.FontName.HELVETICA);
51: 
52:     /**
53:      * Builds a searchable PDF from background images and OCR results.
54:      *
55:      * @param sourcePdf    Original PDF (for per-page media boxes).
56:      * @param backgrounds  Per-page cleaned background images.
57:      * @param ocrResults   Per-page OCR results.
58:      * @return A new PDDocument with searchable text overlaid on background images.
59:      */
60:     private final MetadataPreserver preserver = new MetadataPreserver();
61: 
62:     public PDDocument assemble(File sourcePdf,
63:                                List<BufferedImage> backgrounds,
64:                                List<PageResult> ocrResults) throws IOException {
65:         return assemble(sourcePdf, backgrounds, null, ocrResults);
66:     }
67: 
68:     /**
69:      * Builds a searchable PDF with MRC-like foreground mask overlay.
70:      *
71:      * When foregroundMasks is provided, each mask is CCITT G4 compressed
72:      * and drawn as a PDF ImageMask stencil in black on top of the background.
73:      * This reproduces the original text pixels at full sharpness, while
74:      * the background layer carries the cleaned (de-speckled) page image.
75:      *
76:      * @param sourcePdf       Original PDF (for per-page media boxes).
77:      * @param backgrounds     Per-page cleaned background images.
78:      * @param foregroundMasks Per-page binary foreground masks (TYPE_BYTE_BINARY,
79:      *                        black=text, white=background), or null to skip.
80:      * @param ocrResults      Per-page OCR results.
81:      * @return A new PDDocument with foreground mask overlay and searchable text.
82:      */
83:     public PDDocument assemble(File sourcePdf,
84:                                List<BufferedImage> backgrounds,
85:                                List<BufferedImage> foregroundMasks,
86:                                List<PageResult> ocrResults) throws IOException {
87:         PDDocument output = new PDDocument();
88:         List<PDPage> outPages = new java.util.ArrayList<>();
89: 
90:         try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(sourcePdf)) {
91:             int pageCount = source.getNumberOfPages();
92:             for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {
93:                 PDPage sourcePage = source.getPage(i);
94:                 PDRectangle mediaBox = sourcePage.getMediaBox();
95: 
96:                 BufferedImage bgImage = backgrounds.get(i);
97:                 PageResult ocr = ocrResults.get(i);
98: 
99:                 PDPage outPage = new PDPage(mediaBox);
100:                 output.addPage(outPage);
101:                 outPages.add(outPage);
102: 
103:                 try (PDPageContentStream cs = new PDPageContentStream(output, outPage)) {
104:                     float pageW = mediaBox.getWidth();
105:                     float pageH = mediaBox.getHeight();
106: 
107:                     // Layer 1: background image
108:                     PDImageXObject bgXObject = LosslessFactory.createFromImage(output, bgImage);
109:                     cs.drawImage(bgXObject, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);
110: 
111:                     // Layer 2: foreground mask as CCITT G4 stencil overlay
112:                     if (foregroundMasks != null && i < foregroundMasks.size()) {
113:                         BufferedImage fgMask = foregroundMasks.get(i);
114:                         BufferedImage inverted = invertBinaryImage(fgMask);
115:                         PDImageXObject fgCCITT = CCITTFactory.createFromImage(output, inverted);
116:                         fgCCITT.getCOSObject().setBoolean(COSName.IMAGE_MASK, true);
117:                         fgCCITT.getCOSObject().removeItem(COSName.COLORSPACE);
118:                         cs.setNonStrokingColor(0f, 0f, 0f);
119:                         cs.drawImage(fgCCITT, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);
120:                     }
121: 
122:                     // Layer 3: invisible OCR text
123:                     float scaleX = pageW / ocr.getWidth();
124:                     float scaleY = pageH / ocr.getHeight();
125:                     cs.beginText();
126:                     cs.setFont(FONT, 1);
127:                     cs.setRenderingMode(RenderingMode.NEITHER);
128: 
129:                     for (TextBlock tb : ocr.getTextBlocks()) {
130:                         float x = tb.getBbox().x * scaleX;
131:                         float y = pageH - (tb.getBbox().y + tb.getBbox().height) * scaleY;
132:                         float fontSize = Math.max(tb.getBbox().height * scaleY, 1);
133:                         cs.setFont(FONT, fontSize);
134:                         cs.newLineAtOffset(x, y);
135:                         cs.showText(tb.getWord());
136:                         cs.newLineAtOffset(-x, -y);
137:                     }
138:                     cs.endText();
139:                 }
140:             }
141: 
142:             // Copy metadata from source to output
143:             preserver.preserve(source, output, outPages);
144:         }
145: 
146:         return output;
147:     }
148: 
149:     private static BufferedImage invertBinaryImage(BufferedImage mask) {
150:         int w = mask.getWidth();
151:         int h = mask.getHeight();
152:         BufferedImage inverted = new BufferedImage(w, h, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);
153:         for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
154:             for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
155:                 int rgb = mask.getRGB(x, y);
156:                 inverted.setRGB(x, y, (rgb & 0xFFFFFF) == 0 ? 0xFFFFFFFF : 0xFF000000);
157:             }
158:         }
159:         return inverted;
160:     }
161: 
162: }

(End of file - total 162 lines)
</content>
```

```
<type>file</type>

1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
4: import java.io.File;
5: import java.io.IOException;
6: import java.util.ArrayList;
7: import java.util.List;
8: 
9: import org.apache.pdfbox.Loader;
10: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;
11: import org.apache.pdfbox.rendering.PDFRenderer;
12: 
13: /**
14:  * Renders each page of a PDF to a BufferedImage at 300 DPI
15:  * using PDFBox's PDFRenderer.
16:  *
17:  * 300 DPI is the standard scanning resolution for OCR:
18:  *   - US Letter (8.5"x11")  → 2550 × 3300 px
19:  *   - A4 (210×297 mm)        → 2480 × 3508 px
20:  *
21:  * The renderer uses PDFBox's built-in SFF (subpixel font finalizer)
22:  * for high-quality text rendering.
23:  *
24:  * Implements AutoCloseable so the underlying PDDocument is released.
25:  * Callers should use try-with-resources.
26:  */
27: public class PageExtractor implements AutoCloseable {
28: 
29:     private PDDocument document;
30: 
31:     /**
32:      * Loads a PDF and renders every page.
33:      *
34:      * @param pdf  Input PDF file.
35:      * @return List of BufferedImages, one per page, at 300 DPI.
36:      */
37:     public List<BufferedImage> extractPages(File pdf) throws IOException {
38:         document = Loader.loadPDF(pdf);
39:         PDFRenderer renderer = new PDFRenderer(document);
40:         int pageCount = document.getNumberOfPages();
41:         List<BufferedImage> pages = new ArrayList<>(pageCount);
42:         for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {
43:             BufferedImage img = renderer.renderImageWithDPI(i, 300);
44:             pages.add(img);
45:         }
46:         return pages;
47:     }
48: 
49:     @Override
50:     public void close() throws IOException {
51:         if (document != null) {
52:             document.close();
53:         }
54:     }
55: }

(End of file - total 55 lines)
</content>
```

```
<path>/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/MetadataPreserver.java</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.io.IOException;
4: import java.util.List;
5: 
6: import org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSArray;
7: import org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSDictionary;
8: import org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSName;
9: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;
10: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocumentInformation;
11: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPage;
12: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.PDMetadata;
13: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.documentinterchange.logicalstructure.PDMarkInfo;
14: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.annotation.PDAnnotation;
15: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.documentnavigation.outline.PDDocumentOutline;
16: 
17: /**
18:  * Copies metadata from a source PDF to an output PDF.
19:  *
20:  * Preserved elements:
21:  *   - PDDocumentInformation (title, author, subject, keywords, creator, producer)
22:  *   - PDDocumentOutline (bookmark tree) — COS-level deep copy
23:  *   - Per-page PDAnnotation lists — each annotation COS dictionary is duplicated
24:  *   - XML metadata stream (XMP)
25:  *
26:  * Limitations:
27:  *   - Annotation page references (e.g. link destinations) are not remapped.
28:  *   - Named destinations are preserved but may reference missing pages.
29:  *   - Outline entries pointing to specific pages are kept but not remapped
30:  *     (the output pages are in the same order, so page-number-based links
31:  *     generally still work).
32:  */
33: public class MetadataPreserver {
34: 
35:     /**
36:      * Copies all metadata from source to output.
37:      *
38:      * @param source      The original PDF.
39:      * @param output      The newly assembled PDF.
40:      * @param outputPages Output pages indexed to match source pages.
41:      */
42:     public void preserve(PDDocument source, PDDocument output, List<PDPage> outputPages) throws IOException {
43:         copyDocumentInfo(source, output);
44:         copyOutline(source, output);
45:         copyAnnotations(source, outputPages);
46:         copyXmlMetadata(source, output);
47:     }
48: 
49:     private void copyDocumentInfo(PDDocument source, PDDocument output) {
50:         PDDocumentInformation info = source.getDocumentInformation();
51:         if (info != null) {
52:             PDDocumentInformation dest = new PDDocumentInformation();
53:             copyIfSet(info::getTitle, dest::setTitle);
54:             copyIfSet(info::getAuthor, dest::setAuthor);
55:             copyIfSet(info::getSubject, dest::setSubject);
56:             copyIfSet(info::getKeywords, dest::setKeywords);
57:             copyIfSet(info::getCreator, dest::setCreator);
58:             copyIfSet(info::getProducer, dest::setProducer);
59:             output.setDocumentInformation(dest);
60:         }
61:     }
62: 
63:     private void copyIfSet(ThrowingSupplier<String> getter, ThrowingConsumer<String> setter) {
64:         try {
65:             String val = getter.get();
66:             if (val != null) setter.accept(val);
67:         } catch (Exception e) {
68:             // skip
69:         }
70:     }
71: 
72:     @FunctionalInterface
73:     private interface ThrowingSupplier<T> {
74:         T get() throws Exception;
75:     }
76: 
77:     @FunctionalInterface
78:     private interface ThrowingConsumer<T> {
79:         void accept(T t) throws Exception;
80:     }
81: 
82:     private void copyOutline(PDDocument source, PDDocument output) throws IOException {
83:         PDDocumentOutline srcOutline = source.getDocumentCatalog().getDocumentOutline();
84:         if (srcOutline == null) return;
85: 
86:         // Deep-copy the outline COS dictionary tree
87:         COSDictionary srcDict = srcOutline.getCOSObject();
88:         COSDictionary dstDict = deepCopyCOSDictionary(srcDict);
89:         PDDocumentOutline dstOutline = new PDDocumentOutline(dstDict);
90:         output.getDocumentCatalog().setDocumentOutline(dstOutline);
91:     }
92: 
93:     private void copyAnnotations(PDDocument source, List<PDPage> outputPages) throws IOException {
94:         int pageCount = Math.min(source.getNumberOfPages(), outputPages.size());
95:         for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {
96:             PDPage srcPage = source.getPage(i);
97:             PDPage dstPage = outputPages.get(i);
98: 
99:             List<PDAnnotation> annotations = srcPage.getAnnotations();
100:             if (annotations.isEmpty()) continue;
101: 
102:             COSArray dstAnnots = new COSArray();
103:             for (PDAnnotation ann : annotations) {
104:                 COSDictionary cloned = deepCopyCOSDictionary(ann.getCOSObject());
105:                 dstAnnots.add(cloned);
106:             }
107:             dstPage.getCOSObject().setItem(COSName.ANNOTS, dstAnnots);
108:         }
109:     }
110: 
111:     private void copyXmlMetadata(PDDocument source, PDDocument output) throws IOException {
112:         PDMetadata srcMeta = source.getDocumentCatalog().getMetadata();
113:         if (srcMeta == null) return;
114: 
115:         try (var in = srcMeta.createInputStream()) {
116:             byte[] data = in.readAllBytes();
117:             PDMetadata dstMeta = new PDMetadata(output);
118:             dstMeta.importXMPMetadata(data);
119:             output.getDocumentCatalog().setMetadata(dstMeta);
120:         } catch (Exception e) {
121:             // Non-critical — skip XML metadata copy on failure
122:         }
123:     }
124: 
125:     private static COSDictionary deepCopyCOSDictionary(COSDictionary original) {
126:         COSDictionary copy = new COSDictionary();
127:         for (var entry : original.entrySet()) {
128:             COSName key = entry.getKey();
129:             var value = entry.getValue();
130:             if (value instanceof COSDictionary dict) {
131:                 copy.setItem(key, deepCopyCOSDictionary(dict));
132:             } else if (value instanceof COSArray arr) {
133:                 copy.setItem(key, deepCopyCOSArray(arr));
134:             } else {
135:                 copy.setItem(key, value);
136:             }
137:         }
138:         return copy;
139:     }
140: 
141:     private static COSArray deepCopyCOSArray(COSArray original) {
142:         COSArray copy = new COSArray();
143:         for (var value : original) {
144:             if (value instanceof COSDictionary dict) {
145:                 copy.add(deepCopyCOSDictionary(dict));
146:             } else if (value instanceof COSArray arr) {
147:                 copy.add(deepCopyCOSArray(arr));
148:             } else {
149:                 copy.add(value);
150:             }
151:         }
152:         return copy;
153:     }
154: }

(End of file - total 154 lines)
```
```
</content>
```
```

<path>/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/util/SubprocessRunner.java</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.util;
2: 
3: import java.io.BufferedReader;
4: import java.io.File;
5: import java.io.IOException;
6: import java.io.InputStreamReader;
7: import java.util.ArrayList;
8: import java.util.List;
9: import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;
10: 
11: /**
12:  * Utility for running external processes and capturing stdout/stderr.
13:  *
14:  * Two convenience modes:
15:  *   run(executable, args...)      — blocks until completion (no timeout).
16:  *   run(executable, args, ms)     — kills the process after <ms> milliseconds.
17:  *
18:  * Stderr is NOT merged with stdout (redirectErrorStream = false) so
19:  * callers can distinguish normal output from error diagnostics.
20:  */
21: public class SubprocessRunner {
22: 
23:     /**
24:      * Executes a command with no timeout.
25:      *
26:      * @param executable  The binary to run.
27:      * @param args        Variable-length argument list.
28:      * @return Result containing exit code, stdout, and stderr.
29:      */
30:     public static Result run(File executable, String... args) throws IOException {
31:         return run(executable, List.of(args), -1);
32:     }
33: 
34:     /**
35:      * Executes a command with an optional timeout.
36:      *
37:      * @param executable  The binary to run.
38:      * @param args        Argument list.
39:      * @param timeoutMs   Max wall-clock time in milliseconds (&le;0 = no timeout).
40:      * @return Result containing exit code, stdout, and stderr.
41:      */
42:     public static Result run(File executable, List<String> args, long timeoutMs) throws IOException {
43:         List<String> command = new ArrayList<>();
44:         command.add(executable.getAbsolutePath());
45:         command.addAll(args);
46: 
47:         ProcessBuilder pb = new ProcessBuilder(command);
48:         pb.redirectErrorStream(false);
49: 
50:         Process process = pb.start();
51: 
52:         if (timeoutMs > 0) {
53:             try {
54:                 boolean finished = process.waitFor(timeoutMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);
55:                 if (!finished) {
56:                     process.destroyForcibly();
57:                     throw new IOException("Process timed out after " + timeoutMs + "ms: " + executable.getName());
58:                 }
59:             } catch (InterruptedException e) {
60:                 process.destroyForcibly();
61:                 Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
62:                 throw new IOException("Process was interrupted", e);
63:             }
64:         } else {
65:             try {
66:                 process.waitFor();
67:             } catch (InterruptedException e) {
68:                 process.destroyForcibly();
69:                 Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
70:                 throw new IOException("Process was interrupted", e);
71:             }
72:         }
73: 
74:         byte[] stdout = process.getInputStream().readAllBytes();
75:         byte[] stderr = process.getErrorStream().readAllBytes();
76:         int exitCode = process.exitValue();
77: 
78:         return new Result(exitCode, stdout, stderr);
79:     }
80: 
81:     /**
82:      * Holds the subprocess output.
83:      *
84:      * exitCode  Process exit status (0 = success).
85:      * stdout    Raw bytes written to standard output.
86:      * stderr    Raw bytes written to standard error.
87:      */
88:     public static class Result {
89:         private final int exitCode;
90:         private final byte[] stdout;
91:         private final byte[] stderr;
92: 
93:         public Result(int exitCode, byte[] stdout, byte[] stderr) {
94:             this.exitCode = exitCode;
95:             this.stdout = stdout;
96:             this.stderr = stderr;
97:         }
98: 
99:         public int getExitCode() { return exitCode; }
100:         public byte[] getStdout() { return stdout; }
101:         public byte[] getStderr() { return stderr; }
102:         public String getStdoutString() { return new String(stdout); }
103:         public String getStderrString() { return new String(stderr); }
104:     }
105: }
```

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.util;
2: 
3: import java.io.File;
4: import java.io.IOException;
5: import java.nio.file.Files;
6: import java.util.ArrayList;
7: import java.util.List;
8: 
9: /**
10:  * Tracks temporary files created during a pipeline run and provides
11:  * a single cleanup() call to delete them all.
12:  *
13:  * Each file is also registered with deleteOnExit() as a safety net
14:  * in case cleanup() is not called (e.g., JVM crash).
15:  */
16: public class TempFileManager {
17: 
18:     private final List<File> tempFiles = new ArrayList<>();
19: 
20:     /**
21:      * Creates a temp file and registers it for cleanup.
22:      *
23:      * @param prefix  File name prefix (e.g. "tfocr-").
24:      * @param suffix  File name suffix (e.g. ".png").
25:      * @return The created temporary File.
26:      */
27:     public File createTempFile(String prefix, String suffix) throws IOException {
28:         File f = Files.createTempFile(prefix, suffix).toFile();
29:         f.deleteOnExit();
30:         tempFiles.add(f);
31:         return f;
32:     }
33: 
34:     /**
35:      * Deletes all tracked temp files and clears the internal list.
36:      */
37:     public void cleanup() {
38:         for (File f : tempFiles) {
39:             if (f.exists()) f.delete();
40:         }
41:         tempFiles.clear();
42:     }
43: }

(End of file - total 43 lines)
</content>
```

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.util;
2: 
3: /**
4:  * Cross-platform detection utilities.
5:  *
6:  * Provides consistent enum values for OS and CPU architecture,
7:  * and resolves the project JDK location via the TFOCR_JAVA_HOME
8:  * environment variable (falling back to the running JVM's java.home).
9:  *
10:  * OS detection is based on the os.name system property:
11:  *   "win" in any case   → Os.WINDOWS
12:  *   "mac" in any case   → Os.MAC
13:  *   anything else       → Os.LINUX
14:  *
15:  * Architecture detection is based on os.arch:
16:  *   "aarch64" or "arm64" → Arch.AARCH64
17:  *   anything else         → Arch.X64
18:  */
19: public class PlatformUtils {
20: 
21:     public enum Os { WINDOWS, LINUX, MAC }
22: 
23:     public enum Arch { X64, AARCH64 }
24: 
25:     public static Os detectOs() {
26:         String raw = System.getProperty("os.name").toLowerCase();
27:         if (raw.contains("win")) return Os.WINDOWS;
28:         if (raw.contains("mac")) return Os.MAC;
29:         return Os.LINUX;
30:     }
31: 
32:     public static Arch detectArch() {
33:         String raw = System.getProperty("os.arch").toLowerCase();
34:         if (raw.contains("aarch64") || raw.contains("arm64")) return Arch.AARCH64;
35:         return Arch.X64;
36:     }
37: 
38:     /**
39:      * Returns the project-specific JDK home.
40:      *
41:      * Checks TFOCR_JAVA_HOME first (set by run.sh/bootstrap.sh),
42:      * then falls back to the JVM that is running this code.
43:      */
44:     public static String getProjectJavaHome() {
45:         String env = System.getenv("TFOCR_JAVA_HOME");
46:         if (env != null && !env.isEmpty()) return env;
47:         return System.getProperty("java.home");
48:     }
49: }
```

```
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.util;
2: 
3: import java.io.BufferedReader;
4: import java.io.File;
5: import java.io.IOException;
6: import java.io.InputStreamReader;
7: import java.util.ArrayList;
8: import java.util.List;
9: import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;
10: 
11: /**
12:  * Utility for running external processes and capturing stdout/stderr.
13:  *
14:  * Two convenience modes:
15:  *   run(executable, args...)      — blocks until completion (no timeout).
16:  *   run(executable, args, ms)     — kills the process after <ms> milliseconds.
17:  *
18:  * Stderr is NOT merged with stdout (redirectErrorStream = false) so
19:  * callers can distinguish normal output from error diagnostics.
20:  */
21: public class SubprocessRunner {
22: 
23:     /**
24:      * Executes a command with no timeout.
25:      *
26:      * @param executable  The binary to run.
27:      * @param args        Variable-length argument list.
28:      * @return Result containing exit code, stdout, and stderr.
29:      */
30:     public static Result run(File executable, String... args) throws IOException {
31:         return run(executable, List.of(args), -1);
32:     }
33: 
34:     /**
35:      * Executes a command with an optional timeout.
36:      *
37:      * @param executable  The binary to run.
38:      * @param args        Argument list.
39:      * @param timeoutMs   Max wall-clock time in milliseconds (&le;0 = no timeout).
40:      * @return Result containing exit code, stdout, and stderr.
41:      */
42:     public static Result run(File executable, List<String> args, long timeoutMs) throws IOException {
43:         List<String> command = new ArrayList<>();
44:         command.add(executable.getAbsolutePath());
45:         command.addAll(args);
46: 
47:         ProcessBuilder pb = new ProcessBuilder(command);
48:         pb.redirectErrorStream(false);
49: 
50:         Process process = pb.start();
51: 
52:         if (timeoutMs > 0) {
53:             try {
54:                 boolean finished = process.waitFor(timeoutMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);
55:                 if (!finished) {
56:                     process.destroyForcibly();
57:                     throw new IOException("Process timed out after " + timeoutMs + "ms: " + executable.getName());
58:                 }
59:             } catch (InterruptedException e) {
60:                 process.destroyForcibly();
61:                 Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
62:                 throw new IOException("Process was interrupted", e);
63:             }
64:         } else {
65:             try {
66:                 process.waitFor();
67:             } catch (InterruptedException e) {
68:                 process.destroyForcibly();
69:                 Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
70:                 throw new IOException("Process was interrupted", e);
71:             }
72:         }
73: 
74:         byte[] stdout = process.getInputStream().readAllBytes();
75:         byte[] stderr = process.getErrorStream().readAllBytes();
76:         int exitCode = process.exitValue();
77: 
78:         return new Result(exitCode, stdout, stderr);
79:     }
80: 
81:     /**
82:      * Holds the subprocess output.
83:      *
84:      * exitCode  Process exit status (0 = success).
85:      * stdout    Raw bytes written to standard output.
86:      * stderr    Raw bytes written to standard error.
87:      */
88:     public static class Result {
89:         private final int exitCode;
90:         private final byte[] stdout;
91:         private final byte[] stderr;
92: 
93:         public Result(int exitCode, byte[] stdout, byte[] stderr) {
94:             this.exitCode = exitCode;
95:             this.stdout = stdout;
96:             this.stderr = stderr;
97:         }
98: 
99:         public int getExitCode() { return exitCode; }
100:         public byte[] getStdout() { return stdout; }
101:         public byte[] getStderr() { return stderr; }
102:         public String getStdoutString() { return new String(stdout); }
103:         public String getStderrString() { return new String(stderr); }
104:     }
105: }

(End of file - total 105 lines)
</content>
```

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;
4: 
5: import java.io.File;
6: import java.io.IOException;
7: import java.util.ArrayList;
8: import java.util.List;
9: 
10: import org.apache.pdfbox.Loader;
11: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;
12: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPage;
13: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.documentnavigation.outline.PDDocumentOutline;
14: import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;
15: import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
16: 
17: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;
18: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.SegmentedImage;
19: 
20: class MetadataPreserverTest {
21: 
22:     static PageExtractor extractor;
23:     static ImageSegmenter segmenter;
24:     static OCREngine engine;
25:     static PDFAssembler assembler;
26:     static MetadataPreserver preserver;
27: 
28:     @BeforeAll
29:     static void setup() {
30:         extractor = new PageExtractor();
31:         segmenter = new ImageSegmenter();
32:         engine = new OCREngine();
33:         assembler = new PDFAssembler();
34:         preserver = new MetadataPreserver();
35:     }
36: 
37:     @Test
38:     void preserve_copiesDocumentInfo() throws IOException {
39:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf");
40:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
41:         var backgrounds = pages.stream()
42:                 .map(segmenter::segment)
43:                 .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)
44:                 .toList();
45:         var ocrResults = pages.stream()
46:                 .map(p -> { try { return engine.ocr(p); }
47:                     catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); } })
48:                 .toList();
49: 
50:         try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(input);
51:              PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {
52:             List<PDPage> outPages = new ArrayList<>();
53:             for (int i = 0; i < output.getNumberOfPages(); i++) {
54:                 outPages.add(output.getPage(i));
55:             }
56:             preserver.preserve(source, output, outPages);
57: 
58:             var srcInfo = source.getDocumentInformation();
59:             var dstInfo = output.getDocumentInformation();
60:             assertEquals(srcInfo.getTitle(), dstInfo.getTitle());
61:             assertEquals(srcInfo.getAuthor(), dstInfo.getAuthor());
62:             assertEquals(srcInfo.getSubject(), dstInfo.getSubject());
63:             assertEquals(srcInfo.getKeywords(), dstInfo.getKeywords());
64:         }
65:     }
66: 
67:     @Test
68:     void preserve_copiesOutline() throws IOException {
69:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/with-annotations.pdf");
70:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
71:         var backgrounds = pages.stream()
72:                 .map(segmenter::segment)
73:                 .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)
74:                 .toList();
75:         var ocrResults = pages.stream()
76:                 .map(p -> { try { return engine.ocr(p); }
77:                     catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); } })
78:                 .toList();
79: 
80:         try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(input);
81:              PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {
82:             List<PDPage> outPages = new ArrayList<>();
83:             for (int i = 0; i < output.getNumberOfPages(); i++) {
84:                 outPages.add(output.getPage(i));
85:             }
86:             preserver.preserve(source, output, outPages);
87: 
88:             // Verify outline was copied
89:             PDDocumentOutline srcOutline = source.getDocumentCatalog().getDocumentOutline();
90:             PDDocumentOutline dstOutline = output.getDocumentCatalog().getDocumentOutline();
91:             if (srcOutline != null) {
92:                 assertNotNull(dstOutline, "Outline should be copied when source has one");
93:                 int srcCount = 0;
94:                 for (var child : srcOutline.children()) srcCount++;
95:                 int dstCount = 0;
96:                 for (var child : dstOutline.children()) dstCount++;
97:                 assertEquals(srcCount, dstCount, "Outline item count should match");
98:             }
99:         }
100:     }
101: 
102:     @Test
103:     void preserve_copiesAnnotations() throws IOException {
104:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/with-annotations.pdf");
105:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
106:         var backgrounds = pages.stream()
107:                 .map(segmenter::segment)
108:                 .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)
109:                 .toList();
110:         var ocrResults = pages.stream()
111:                 .map(p -> { try { return engine.ocr(p); }
112:                     catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); } })
113:                 .toList();
114: 
115:         try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(input);
116:              PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {
117:             List<PDPage> outPages = new ArrayList<>();
118:             for (int i = 0; i < output.getNumberOfPages(); i++) {
119:                 outPages.add(output.getPage(i));
120:             }
121:             preserver.preserve(source, output, outPages);
122: 
123:             for (int i = 0; i < source.getNumberOfPages() && i < output.getNumberOfPages(); i++) {
124:                 int srcCount = source.getPage(i).getAnnotations().size();
125:                 int dstCount = output.getPage(i).getAnnotations().size();
126:                 assertEquals(srcCount, dstCount,
127:                         "Page " + (i + 1) + " annotation count should match");
128:             }
129:         }
130:     }
131: }
```

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;
4: 
5: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
6: import java.io.File;
7: import java.io.IOException;
8: 
9: import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;
10: import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
11: 
12: /**
13:  * Tests for OCREngine (Tesseract CLI subprocess) using test PDFs.
14:  *
15:  * Verifies word-level OCR output: non-null results, expected word presence,
16:  * positive confidence scores, bounding box validity, and correct behaviour
17:  * on blank pages (empty text block list).
18:  */
19: class OCREngineTest {
20: 
21:     static PageExtractor extractor;
22:     static OCREngine engine;
23: 
24:     @BeforeAll
25:     static void setup() {
26:         extractor = new PageExtractor();
27:         engine = new OCREngine();
28:     }
29: 
30:     @Test
31:     void ocr_simpleText_returnsWordBlocks() throws IOException {
32:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf"));
33:         var result = engine.ocr(pages.get(0));
34:         assertNotNull(result);
35:         assertNotNull(result.getTextBlocks());
36:         assertFalse(result.getTextBlocks().isEmpty());
37:     }
38: 
39:     @Test
40:     void ocr_simpleText_containsExpectedWord() throws IOException {
41:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf"));
42:         var result = engine.ocr(pages.get(0));
43:         var words = result.getTextBlocks().stream()
44:                 .map(tb -> tb.getWord().toLowerCase())
45:                 .toList();
46:         assertTrue(words.contains("quick"),
47:                 () -> "Expected 'quick' in OCR output but got: " + words);
48:     }
49: 
50:     @Test
51:     void ocr_simpleText_allBlocksHavePositiveConfidence() throws IOException {
52:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf"));
53:         var result = engine.ocr(pages.get(0));
54:         for (var tb : result.getTextBlocks()) {
55:             assertTrue(tb.getConfidence() >= 0,
56:                     () -> "Negative confidence for word: " + tb.getWord());
57:         }
58:     }
59: 
60:     @Test
61:     void ocr_blankPage_returnsEmptyBlocks() throws IOException {
62:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/blank.pdf"));
63:         var result = engine.ocr(pages.get(0));
64:         assertNotNull(result);
65:         assertTrue(result.getTextBlocks().isEmpty());
66:     }
67: 
68:     @Test
69:     void ocr_blankPage_returnsCorrectPageNumber() throws IOException {
70:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/blank.pdf"));
71:         var result = engine.ocr(pages.get(0));
72:         assertEquals(1, result.getPageNumber());
73:     }
74: 
75:     @Test
76:     void ocr_simpleText_blocksHaveBoundingBoxes() throws IOException {
77:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf"));
78:         var result = engine.ocr(pages.get(0));
79:         for (var tb : result.getTextBlocks()) {
80:             assertNotNull(tb.getBbox());
81:             assertTrue(tb.getBbox().width > 0);
82:             assertTrue(tb.getBbox().height > 0);
83:         }
84:     }
85: }
```

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;
4: 
5: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
6: import java.io.File;
7: import java.io.IOException;
8: 
9: import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;
10: import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
11: 
12: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.SegmentedImage;
13: 
14: /**
15:  * Tests for ImageSegmenter (pure-Java binarization pipeline).
16:  *
17:  * Verifies that segment() returns non-null outputs, preserves dimensions,
18:  * produces a binary foreground mask (TYPE_BYTE_BINARY), correctly identifies
19:  * text vs blank pages, and inpaints foreground pixels in the cleaned background.
20:  */
21: class ImageSegmenterTest {
22: 
23:     static PageExtractor extractor;
24:     static ImageSegmenter segmenter;
25: 
26:     @BeforeAll
27:     static void setup() {
28:         extractor = new PageExtractor();
29:         segmenter = new ImageSegmenter();
30:     }
31: 
32:     @Test
33:     void segment_blankPage_returnsNonNull() throws IOException {
34:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/blank.pdf"));
35:         SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(pages.get(0));
36:         assertNotNull(result);
37:         assertNotNull(result.getForegroundMask());
38:         assertNotNull(result.getCleanedBackground());
39:     }
40: 
41:     @Test
42:     void segment_preservesDimensions() throws IOException {
43:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf"));
44:         BufferedImage page = pages.get(0);
45:         SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(page);
46:         assertEquals(page.getWidth(), result.getForegroundMask().getWidth());
47:         assertEquals(page.getHeight(), result.getForegroundMask().getHeight());
48:         assertEquals(page.getWidth(), result.getCleanedBackground().getWidth());
49:         assertEquals(page.getHeight(), result.getCleanedBackground().getHeight());
50:     }
51: 
52:     @Test
53:     void segment_foregroundMaskIsBinary() throws IOException {
54:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf"));
55:         SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(pages.get(0));
56:         BufferedImage mask = result.getForegroundMask();
57:         assertEquals(BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY, mask.getType());
58:     }
59: 
60:     @Test
61:     void segment_blankPage_hasLittleForeground() throws IOException {
62:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/blank.pdf"));
63:         SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(pages.get(0));
64:         BufferedImage mask = result.getForegroundMask();
65:         int width = mask.getWidth();
66:         int height = mask.getHeight();
67:         int blackPixels = 0;
68:         for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {
69:             for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
70:                 if ((mask.getRGB(x, y) & 0xFFFFFF) == 0) {
71:                     blackPixels++;
72:                 }
73:             }
74:         }
75:         int total = width * height;
76:         assertTrue((double) blackPixels / total < 0.05,
77:                 "Blank page should have <5% foreground pixels, got " +
78:                 (100.0 * blackPixels / total) + "%");
79:     }
80: 
81:     @Test
82:     void segment_simpleText_hasForegroundPixels() throws IOException {
83:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf"));
84:         SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(pages.get(0));
85:         BufferedImage mask = result.getForegroundMask();
86:         int width = mask.getWidth();
87:         int height = mask.getHeight();
88:         int blackPixels = 0;
89:         for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {
90:             for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
91:                 if ((mask.getRGB(x, y) & 0xFFFFFF) == 0) {
92:                     blackPixels++;
93:                 }
94:             }
95:         }
96:         int total = width * height;
97:         assertTrue((double) blackPixels / total > 0.001,
98:                 "Text page should have >0.1% foreground pixels, got " +
99:                 (100.0 * blackPixels / total) + "%");
100:     }
101: 
102:     @Test
103:     void segment_cleanedBackground_reducesTextNoise() throws IOException {
104:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf"));
105:         BufferedImage page = pages.get(0);
106:         SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(page);
107:         BufferedImage cleaned = result.getCleanedBackground();
108: 
109:         // Compare cleaned vs original: text areas should be filled in
110:         BufferedImage mask = result.getForegroundMask();
111:         int width = page.getWidth();
112:         int height = page.getHeight();
113:         int textPixels = 0;
114:         int differentPixels = 0;
115:         for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {
116:             for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
117:                 if ((mask.getRGB(x, y) & 0xFFFFFF) == 0) {
118:                     textPixels++;
119:                     int origRgb = page.getRGB(x, y);
120:                     int cleanRgb = cleaned.getRGB(x, y);
121:                     if (origRgb != cleanRgb) {
122:                         differentPixels++;
123:                     }
124:                 }
125:             }
126:         }
127:         // Most foreground pixels should differ (text filled in)
128:         assertTrue(textPixels > 0);
129:         assertTrue((double) differentPixels / textPixels > 0.5,
130:                 "Most foreground pixels should differ between original and cleaned");
131:     }
132: }

(End of file - total 132 lines)
</content>
```

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;
4: 
5: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
6: import java.io.IOException;
7: 
8: import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;
9: import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
10: 
11: /**
12:  * Tests for JBIG2Compressor with synthetic binary images.
13:  *
14:  * Since jbig2enc is not available in this environment, all tests exercise
15:  * the CCITT G4 fallback path.  Verifies non-null outputs, dimension preservation,
16:  * and correct behaviour for blank, all-white, and mixed foreground images.
17:  */
18: class JBIG2CompressorTest {
19: 
20:     static JBIG2Compressor compressor;
21: 
22:     @BeforeAll
23:     static void setup() {
24:         compressor = new JBIG2Compressor();
25:     }
26: 
27:     @Test
28:     void compress_blankImage_returnsNonNull() throws IOException {
29:         BufferedImage img = new BufferedImage(100, 100, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);
30:         JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result = compressor.compress(img);
31:         assertNotNull(result);
32:         assertNotNull(result.getData());
33:         assertTrue(result.getData().length > 0);
34:     }
35: 
36:     @Test
37:     void compress_preservesDimensions() throws IOException {
38:         BufferedImage img = new BufferedImage(200, 300, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);
39:         JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result = compressor.compress(img);
40:         assertEquals(200, result.getWidth());
41:         assertEquals(300, result.getHeight());
42:     }
43: 
44:     @Test
45:     void compress_allWhite_returnsNonNull() throws IOException {
46:         BufferedImage img = new BufferedImage(100, 100, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);
47:         // Fill with white (background)
48:         for (int y = 0; y < 100; y++) {
49:             for (int x = 0; x < 100; x++) {
50:                 img.setRGB(x, y, 0xFFFFFFFF);
51:             }
52:         }
53:         JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result = compressor.compress(img);
54:         assertNotNull(result);
55:         assertTrue(result.getData().length > 0);
56:     }
57: 
58:     @Test
59:     void compress_withForegroundPixels_returnsNonNull() throws IOException {
60:         BufferedImage img = new BufferedImage(100, 100, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);
61:         // Fill with white
62:         for (int y = 0; y < 100; y++) {
63:             for (int x = 0; x < 100; x++) {
64:                 img.setRGB(x, y, 0xFFFFFFFF);
65:             }
66:         }
67:         // Add some black pixels (foreground)
68:         for (int x = 10; x < 90; x++) {
69:             img.setRGB(x, 50, 0xFF000000);
70:         }
71:         JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result = compressor.compress(img);
72:         assertNotNull(result);
73:         assertTrue(result.getData().length > 0);
74:     }
75: 
76:     @Test
77:     void compress_fallbackIsNotJbig2() throws IOException {
78:         BufferedImage img = new BufferedImage(50, 50, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);
79:         System.clearProperty("tfocr.native.dir");
80:         JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result = compressor.compress(img);
81:         assertNotNull(result);
82:         assertFalse(result.isJbig2(), "Should fallback when jbig2enc unavailable");
83:     }
84: }
```

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;

3: import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;

13: /**
14:  * Tests for PageExtractor using the generated test PDFs in tests/test-files/.

17:  * image type (TYPE_INT_RGB), and resource cleanup via AutoCloseable.

20: 
21:     @Test

41:         List<BufferedImage> pages = extractor.extractPages(pdf);

45:         assertTrue(Math.abs(pages.get(0).getHeight() - 3300) <= 1);
```

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;
4: 
5: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
6: import java.io.File;
7: import java.io.IOException;
8: import java.util.List;
9: 
10: import org.apache.pdfbox.Loader;
11: import org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSName;
12: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;
13: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPage;
14: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.PDRectangle;
15: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.PDImageXObject;
16: import org.apache.pdfbox.text.PDFTextStripper;
17: import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;
18: import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
19: 
20: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;
21: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.SegmentedImage;
22: 
23: /**
24:  * Integration tests for PDFAssembler that run the full pipeline
25:  * (PageExtractor → ImageSegmenter → OCREngine → PDFAssembler).
26:  *
27:  * Verifies: output PDF creation, correct page count for multi-page inputs,
28:  * text extractability (searchable PDF), and correct page dimensions
29:  * (US Letter = 612×792 pt).
30:  */
31: class PDFAssemblerTest {
32: 
33:     static PageExtractor extractor;
34:     static ImageSegmenter segmenter;
35:     static OCREngine engine;
36:     static PDFAssembler assembler;
37: 
38:     @BeforeAll
39:     static void setup() {
40:         extractor = new PageExtractor();
41:         segmenter = new ImageSegmenter();
42:         engine = new OCREngine();
43:         assembler = new PDFAssembler();
44:     }
45: 
46:     @Test
47:     void assemble_simpleText_createsSearchablePdf() throws IOException {
48:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf");
49:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
50:         assertFalse(pages.isEmpty());
51: 
52:         // Segment and OCR each page
53:         var backgrounds = pages.stream()
54:                 .map(segmenter::segment)
55:                 .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)
56:                 .toList();
57:         var ocrResults = pages.stream()
58:                 .map(p -> {
59:                     try { return engine.ocr(p); }
60:                     catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
61:                 })
62:                 .toList();
63: 
64:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {
65:             assertNotNull(output);
66:             assertEquals(1, output.getNumberOfPages());
67: 
68:             // Verify text is extractable (searchable)
69:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
70:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
71:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("quick"),
72:                     "Output PDF should contain 'quick' but got: " + text);
73:         }
74:     }
75: 
76:     @Test
77:     void assemble_multiPage_preservesPageCount() throws IOException {
78:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/multi-page.pdf");
79:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
80:         assertEquals(3, pages.size());
81: 
82:         var backgrounds = pages.stream()
83:                 .map(segmenter::segment)
84:                 .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)
85:                 .toList();
86:         var ocrResults = pages.stream()
87:                 .map(p -> {
88:                     try { return engine.ocr(p); }
89:                     catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
90:                 })
91:                 .toList();
92: 
93:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {
94:             assertEquals(3, output.getNumberOfPages());
95:         }
96:     }
97: 
98:     @Test
99:     void assemble_multiPage_eachPageHasText() throws IOException {
100:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/multi-page.pdf");
101:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
102:         var backgrounds = pages.stream()
103:                 .map(segmenter::segment)
104:                 .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)
105:                 .toList();
106:         var ocrResults = pages.stream()
107:                 .map(p -> {
108:                     try { return engine.ocr(p); }
109:                     catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
110:                 })
111:                 .toList();
112: 
113:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {
114:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
115:             for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
116:                 stripper.setStartPage(i + 1);
117:                 stripper.setEndPage(i + 1);
118:                 String text = stripper.getText(output);
119:                 assertFalse(text.trim().isEmpty(),
120:                         "Page " + (i + 1) + " should have extractable text");
121:             }
122:         }
123:     }
124: 
125:     @Test
126:     void assemble_blankPage_createsPdfWithCorrectDimensions() throws IOException {
127:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/blank.pdf");
128:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
129:         var backgrounds = pages.stream()
130:                 .map(segmenter::segment)
131:                 .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)
132:                 .toList();
133:         var ocrResults = pages.stream()
134:                 .map(p -> {
135:                     try { return engine.ocr(p); }
136:                     catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
137:                 })
138:                 .toList();
139: 
140:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {
141:             assertEquals(1, output.getNumberOfPages());
142:             var mediaBox = output.getPage(0).getMediaBox();
143:             assertEquals(612.0f, mediaBox.getWidth(), 0.01);
144:             assertEquals(792.0f, mediaBox.getHeight(), 0.01);
145:         }
146:     }
147: 
148:     @Test
149:     void assemble_withForegroundMask_usesCcittStencil() throws IOException {
150:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf");
151:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
152:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
153:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
154:         var foregroundMasks = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getForegroundMask).toList();
155:         var ocrResults = pages.stream()
156:                 .map(p -> {
157:                     try { return engine.ocr(p); }
158:                     catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
159:                 })
160:                 .toList();
161: 
162:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults)) {
163:             assertNotNull(output);
164:             assertEquals(1, output.getNumberOfPages());
165: 
166:             // Verify text is still searchable
167:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
168:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
169:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("quick"),
170:                     "Output PDF should contain 'quick' but got: " + text);
171: 
172:             // Verify page resources include a CCITT-encoded image (stencil mask)
173:             PDPage page = output.getPage(0);
174:             var resources = page.getResources();
175:             boolean hasStencil = false;
176:             for (var name : resources.getXObjectNames()) {
177:                 try {
178:                     var xObj = resources.getXObject(name);
179:                     if (xObj instanceof PDImageXObject img) {
180:                         if (img.getCOSObject().getBoolean(COSName.IMAGE_MASK, false)) {
181:                             hasStencil = true;
182:                             break;
183:                         }
184:                     }
185:                 } catch (IOException e) {
186:                     // skip
187:                 }
188:             }
189:             assertTrue(hasStencil, "Output PDF should contain an ImageMask stencil");
190:         }
191:     }
192: }
```

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;
4: 
5: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
6: import java.io.File;
7: import java.io.IOException;
8: import java.util.ArrayList;
9: import java.util.List;
10: 
11: import org.apache.pdfbox.Loader;
12: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;
13: import org.apache.pdfbox.text.PDFTextStripper;
14: import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;
15: import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
16: 
17: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;
18: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.SegmentedImage;
19: 
20: /**
21:  * End-to-end integration tests running the full pipeline
22:  * (PageExtractor → ImageSegmenter → OCREngine → PDFAssembler)
23:  * on all sample PDFs and verifying the output.
24:  */
25: class PipelineIntegrationTest {
26: 
27:     static PageExtractor extractor;
28:     static ImageSegmenter segmenter;
29:     static OCREngine engine;
30:     static PDFAssembler assembler;
31: 
32:     @BeforeAll
33:     static void setup() {
34:         extractor = new PageExtractor();
35:         segmenter = new ImageSegmenter();
36:         engine = new OCREngine();
37:         assembler = new PDFAssembler();
38:     }
39: 
40:     @Test
41:     void fullPipeline_simpleText_outputIsSearchable() throws IOException {
42:         runPipeline("simple-text.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
43:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
44:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
45:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("quick"),
46:                     "Output should contain 'quick' from the sample text");
47:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("brown"),
48:                     "Output should contain 'brown' from the sample text");
49:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("fox"),
50:                     "Output should contain 'fox' from the sample text");
51:         });
52:     }
53: 
54:     @Test
55:     void fullPipeline_blankPage_outputHasNoSearchableText() throws IOException {
56:         runPipeline("blank.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
57:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
58:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
59:             assertTrue(text.trim().isEmpty(),
60:                     "Blank page should have no searchable text, but got: '" + text + "'");
61:         });
62:     }
63: 
64:     @Test
65:     void fullPipeline_multiPage_preservesPageCount() throws IOException {
66:         runPipeline("multi-page.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
67:             assertEquals(3, output.getNumberOfPages(),
68:                     "Three-page input should produce three-page output");
69:         });
70:     }
71: 
72:     @Test
73:     void fullPipeline_multiPage_eachPageHasText() throws IOException {
74:         runPipeline("multi-page.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
75:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
76:             for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
77:                 stripper.setStartPage(i + 1);
78:                 stripper.setEndPage(i + 1);
79:                 String text = stripper.getText(output);
80:                 assertFalse(text.trim().isEmpty(),
81:                         "Page " + (i + 1) + " should have extractable text");
82:             }
83:         });
84:     }
85: 
86:     @Test
87:     void fullPipeline_withAnnotations_preservesMetadata() throws IOException {
88:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/with-annotations.pdf");
89:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
90:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
91:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
92:         var foregroundMasks = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getForegroundMask).toList();
93:         var ocrResults = processOcr(pages);
94: 
95:         try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(input);
96:              PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults)) {
97: 
98:             // Verify document info preserved
99:             var srcInfo = source.getDocumentInformation();
100:             var dstInfo = output.getDocumentInformation();
101:             assertEquals(srcInfo.getTitle(), dstInfo.getTitle());
102:             assertEquals(srcInfo.getAuthor(), dstInfo.getAuthor());
103: 
104:             // Verify outline preserved if source has one
105:             var srcOutline = source.getDocumentCatalog().getDocumentOutline();
106:             if (srcOutline != null) {
107:                 var dstOutline = output.getDocumentCatalog().getDocumentOutline();
108:                 assertNotNull(dstOutline);
109:             }
110:         }
111:     }
112: 
113:     @Test
114:     void fullPipeline_twoColumn_outputIsSearchable() throws IOException {
115:         runPipeline("two-column.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
116:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
117:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
118:             assertFalse(text.trim().isEmpty(), "Two-column PDF should have searchable text");
119:             // Should contain content from both columns
120:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("lorem") || text.toLowerCase().contains("ipsum"),
121:                     "Two-column output should contain sample text");
122:         });
123:     }
124: 
125:     @Test
126:     void fullPipeline_simpleText_withForegroundMask() throws IOException {
127:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf");
128:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
129:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
130:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
131:         var foregroundMasks = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getForegroundMask).toList();
132:         var ocrResults = processOcr(pages);
133: 
134:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults)) {
135:             assertNotNull(output);
136:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
137:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
138:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("quick"));
139:         }
140:     }
141: 
142:     @Test
143:     void fullPipeline_outputFileSizeIsReasonable() throws IOException {
144:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf");
145:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
146:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
147:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
148:         var foregroundMasks = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getForegroundMask).toList();
149:         var ocrResults = processOcr(pages);
150: 
151:         File tempOutput = File.createTempFile("tfocr-integration-", ".pdf");
152:         tempOutput.deleteOnExit();
153:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults)) {
154:             output.save(tempOutput);
155:         }
156:         // Output should be reasonably sized (not trivially tiny, not enormous)
157:         long size = tempOutput.length();
158:         assertTrue(size > 1000, "Output PDF should be at least 1KB, was " + size + " bytes");
159:         // At 300 DPI for US Letter, even compressed, expect > 10KB
160:         assertTrue(size < 100_000_000, "Output PDF should be under 100MB, was " + size + " bytes");
161:     }
162: 
163:     // ── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
164: 
165:     @FunctionalInterface
166:     private interface OutputValidator {
167:         void validate(PDDocument output, File sourceFile) throws IOException;
168:     }
169: 
170:     private void runPipeline(String testPdfName, OutputValidator validator) throws IOException {
171:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/" + testPdfName);
172:         assertTrue(input.exists(), "Test PDF not found: " + input);
173: 
174:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
175:         assertNotNull(pages);
176:         assertFalse(pages.isEmpty());
177: 
178:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
179:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
180:         var ocrResults = processOcr(pages);
181: 
182:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {
183:             assertNotNull(output);
184:             validator.validate(output, input);
185:         }
186:     }
187: 
188:     private List<PageResult> processOcr(List<BufferedImage> pages) {
189:         return pages.stream().map(p -> {
190:             try { return engine.ocr(p); }
191:             catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
192:         }).toList();
193:     }
194: }

(End of file - total 194 lines)
</content>
```

### Summary: Most Impactful Candidate Values for Externalization

These are values that would most benefit from being extracted to configuration (application.properties, system properties, CLI args, or constants):

| Priority | Value | File:Line | Why Configurable |
|----------|-------|-----------|------------------|
| HIGH | `/usr/share/tesseract-ocr/5/tessdata` | OCREngine.java:47 | Linux-specific fallback; fails on other distros/OS |
| HIGH | `300` (DPI) | PageExtractor.java:43 | DPI should be user-selectable for quality/speed tradeoff |
| HIGH | `tesseract` (CLI name) | OCREngine.java:64 | Binary may be at different path or named differently |
| HIGH | `eng` (language) | OCREngine.java:67 | User must select their document's language |
| HIGH | `1` (PSM) | OCREngine.java:68 | Different docs need different segmentation modes |
| HIGH | `64` (tile size) | ImageSegmenter.java:25 | Performance/accuracy tradeoff; should be adjustable |
| HIGH | `3` (inpaint radius) | ImageSegmenter.java:46 | Depends on DPI and font size |
| HIGH | `0.95` (percentile) | ImageSegmenter.java:129 | May need tuning for different page qualities |
| HIGH | `-p -s` (jbig2enc flags) | JBIG2Compressor.java:110 | Users may want to adjust compression parameters |
| HIGH | `./native` / `./tessdata` (defaults) | TrulyFreeOCR.java:34,37 | Installation-dependent paths |
| HIGH | JVM args in build.gradle.kts:38-41 | build.gradle.kts | Hardcoded Linux paths for native libraries |
| MED | `2550`/`3300` (page dims) | PageExtractorTest.java:44-45 | US Letter specific; A4 would be 2480x3508 |
| MED | `612.0f`/`792.0f` (media box) | PDFAssemblerTest.java:143 | US Letter specific |
| MED | `255.0` (clip) | ImageSegmenter.java:152 | Could be dynamic for non-8-bit images |
| MED | `5` (TSV level) | OCREngine.java:123 | Tesseract output format column constant |
| MED | `0.299, 0.587, 0.114` (luma) | ImageSegmenter.java:77 | Well-known standard, but alternative coefficients exist (BT.709) |
| LOW | `tfocr-`, `.png`, `.tsv`, `.sym`, `.jbig2` | Various | Temp file naming convention |
| LOW | `HELVETICA` (font) | PDFAssembler.java:50 | Standard PDF font; rarely changed |
| LOW | `0xFFFFFF`, `0xFF000000` (bitmasks) | Various | ARGB bit manipulation constants |

```
63:         SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(pages.get(0));
64:         BufferedImage mask = result.getForegroundMask();

102:     @Test
103:     void segment_cleanedBackground_reducesTextNoise() throws IOException {
104:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf"));
105:         BufferedImage page = pages.get(0);
106:         SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(page);
107:         BufferedImage cleaned = result.getCleanedBackground();
108: 
109:         // Compare cleaned vs original: text areas should be filled in
110:         BufferedImage mask = result.getForegroundMask();
111:         int width = page.getWidth();
112:         int height = page.getHeight();
113:         int textPixels = 0;
114:         int differentPixels = 0;
115:         for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {
116:             for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
117:                 if ((mask.getRGB(x, y) & 0xFFFFFF) == 0) {
118:                     textPixels++;
119:                     int origRgb = page.getRGB(x, y);
120:                     int cleanRgb = cleaned.getRGB(x, y);
121:                     if (origRgb != cleanRgb) {
122:                         differentPixels++;
123:                     }
124:                 }
125:             }
126:         }
127:         // Most foreground pixels should differ (text filled in)
128:         assertTrue(textPixels > 0);
129:         assertTrue((double) differentPixels / textPixels > 0.5,
130:                 "Most foreground pixels should differ between original and cleaned");
131:     }
132: }

(End of file - total 132 lines)
</content>
```

```
23: /**
24:  * Integration tests for PDFAssembler that run the full pipeline
25:  * (PageExtractor → ImageSegmenter → OCREngine → PDFAssembler).
26:  *
27:  * Verifies: output PDF creation, correct page count for multi-page inputs,
28:  * text extractability (searchable PDF), and correct page dimensions
29:  * (US Letter = 612×792 pt).
30:  */
31: class PDFAssemblerTest {
32: 
33:     static PageExtractor extractor;
34:     static ImageSegmenter segmenter;
35:     static OCREngine engine;

41:         segmenter = new ImageSegmenter();

45: 
46:     @Test
47:     void assemble_simpleText_createsSearchablePdf() throws IOException {

51:

53:         var backgrounds = pages.stream()
54:                 .map(segmenter::segment)

56:                 .toList();

58:                 .map(p -> {
59:                     try { return engine.ocr(p); }
60:                     catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
61:                 })
62:                 .toList();
63: 
64:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {
65:             assertNotNull(output);
66:             assertEquals(1, output.getNumberOfPages());
67: 
68:             // Verify text is extractable (searchable)
69:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
70:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
71:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("quick"),
72:                     "Output PDF should contain 'quick' but got: " + text);
73:         }
74:     }
75: 
76:     @Test
77:     void assemble_multiPage_preservesPageCount() throws IOException {

82:         var backgrounds = pages.stream()

88:                     try { return engine.ocr(p); }

90:                 })
91:                 .toList();
92: 
93:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {
94:             assertEquals(3, output.getNumberOfPages());
95:         }
96:     }

171: 
172:             // Verify page resources include a CCITT-encoded image (stencil mask)
173:             PDPage page = output.getPage(0);

176:             for (var name : resources.getXObjectNames()) {
177:                 try {
178:                     var xObj = resources.getXObject(name);
179:                     if (xObj instanceof PDImageXObject img) {
180:                         if (img.getCOSObject().getBoolean(COSName.IMAGE_MASK, false)) {
181:                             hasStencil = true;
182:                             break;
183:                         }
184:                     }
185:                 } catch (IOException e) {
186:                     // skip
187:                 }
188:             }
189:             assertTrue(hasStencil, "Output PDF should contain an ImageMask stencil");
190:         }
191:     }
192: }
```

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;
4: 
5: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
6: import java.io.File;
7: import java.io.IOException;
8: import java.util.ArrayList;

10: 
11: import org.apache.pdfbox.Loader;
12: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;
13: import org.apache.pdfbox.text.PDFTextStripper;
14: import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;
```
