16: 
17: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;
18: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.SegmentedImage;
19: 
20: /**
21:  * End-to-end integration tests running the full pipeline
22:  * (PageExtractor → ImageSegmenter → OCREngine → PDFAssembler)
23:  * on all sample PDFs and verifying the output.
24:  */
25: class PipelineIntegrationTest {
26: 
27:     static PageExtractor extractor;
28:     static ImageSegmenter segmenter;
29:     static OCREngine engine;
30:     static PDFAssembler assembler;
31: 
32:     @BeforeAll
33:     static void setup() {
34:         extractor = new PageExtractor();
35:         segmenter = new ImageSegmenter();
36:         engine = new OCREngine();
37:         assembler = new PDFAssembler();
38:     }
39: 
40:     @Test
41:     void fullPipeline_simpleText_outputIsSearchable() throws IOException {
42:         runPipeline("simple-text.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
43:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
44:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
45:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("quick"),
46:                     "Output should contain 'quick' from the sample text");
47:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("brown"),
48:                     "Output should contain 'brown' from the sample text");
49:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("fox"),
50:                     "Output should contain 'fox' from the sample text");
51:         });
52:     }
53: 
54:     @Test
55:     void fullPipeline_blankPage_outputHasNoSearchableText() throws IOException {
56:         runPipeline("blank.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
57:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
58:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
59:             assertTrue(text.trim().isEmpty(),
60:                     "Blank page should have no searchable text, but got: '" + text + "'");
61:         });
62:     }
63: 
64:     @Test
65:     void fullPipeline_multiPage_preservesPageCount() throws IOException {
66:         runPipeline("multi-page.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
67:             assertEquals(3, output.getNumberOfPages(),
68:                     "Three-page input should produce three-page output");
69:         });
70:     }
71: 
72:     @Test
73:     void fullPipeline_multiPage_eachPageHasText() throws IOException {
74:         runPipeline("multi-page.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
75:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
76:             for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
77:                 stripper.setStartPage(i + 1);
78:                 stripper.setEndPage(i + 1);
79:                 String text = stripper.getText(output);
80:                 assertFalse(text.trim().isEmpty(),
81:                         "Page " + (i + 1) + " should have extractable text");
82:             }
83:         });
84:     }
85: 
86:     @Test
87:     void fullPipeline_withAnnotations_preservesMetadata() throws IOException {
88:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/with-annotations.pdf");
89:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
90:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
91:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
92:         var foregroundMasks = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getForegroundMask).toList();
93:         var ocrResults = processOcr(pages);
94: 
95:         try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(input);
96:              PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults)) {
97: 
98:             // Verify document info preserved
99:             var srcInfo = source.getDocumentInformation();
100:             var dstInfo = output.getDocumentInformation();
101:             assertEquals(srcInfo.getTitle(), dstInfo.getTitle());
102:             assertEquals(srcInfo.getAuthor(), dstInfo.getAuthor());
103: 
104:             // Verify outline preserved if source has one
105:             var srcOutline = source.getDocumentCatalog().getDocumentOutline();
106:             if (srcOutline != null) {
107:                 var dstOutline = output.getDocumentCatalog().getDocumentOutline();
108:                 assertNotNull(dstOutline);
109:             }
110:         }
111:     }
112: 
113:     @Test
114:     void fullPipeline_twoColumn_outputIsSearchable() throws IOException {
115:         runPipeline("two-column.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
116:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
117:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
118:             assertFalse(text.trim().isEmpty(), "Two-column PDF should have searchable text");
119:             // Should contain content from both columns
120:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("lorem") || text.toLowerCase().contains("ipsum"),
121:                     "Two-column output should contain sample text");
122:         });
123:     }
124: 
125:     @Test
126:     void fullPipeline_simpleText_withForegroundMask() throws IOException {
127:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf");
128:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
129:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
130:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
131:         var foregroundMasks = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getForegroundMask).toList();
132:         var ocrResults = processOcr(pages);
133: 
134:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults)) {
135:             assertNotNull(output);
136:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
137:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
138:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("quick"));
139:         }
140:     }
141: 
142:     @Test
143:     void fullPipeline_outputFileSizeIsReasonable() throws IOException {
144:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf");
145:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
146:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
147:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
148:         var foregroundMasks = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getForegroundMask).toList();
149:         var ocrResults = processOcr(pages);
150: 
151:         File tempOutput = File.createTempFile("tfocr-integration-", ".pdf");
152:         tempOutput.deleteOnExit();
153:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults)) {
154:             output.save(tempOutput);
155:         }
156:         // Output should be reasonably sized (not trivially tiny, not enormous)
157:         long size = tempOutput.length();
158:         assertTrue(size > 1000, "Output PDF should be at least 1KB, was " + size + " bytes");
159:         // At 300 DPI for US Letter, even compressed, expect > 10KB
160:         assertTrue(size < 100_000_000, "Output PDF should be under 100MB, was " + size + " bytes");
161:     }
162: 
163:     // ── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
164: 
165:     @FunctionalInterface
166:     private interface OutputValidator {
167:         void validate(PDDocument output, File sourceFile) throws IOException;
168:     }
169: 
170:     private void runPipeline(String testPdfName, OutputValidator validator) throws IOException {
171:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/" + testPdfName);
172:         assertTrue(input.exists(), "Test PDF not found: " + input);
173: 
174:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
175:         assertNotNull(pages);
176:         assertFalse(pages.isEmpty());
177: 
178:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
179:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
180:         var ocrResults = processOcr(pages);
181: 
182:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {
183:             assertNotNull(output);
184:             validator.validate(output, input);
185:         }
186:     }
187: 
188:     private List<PageResult> processOcr(List<BufferedImage> pages) {
189:         return pages.stream().map(p -> {
190:             try { return engine.ocr(p); }
191:             catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
192:         }).toList();
193:     }
194: }

(End of file - total 194 lines)
</content>
```

25:

32:

```
<path>/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/bootstrap.sh</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: set -euo pipefail
3: 
4: SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
5: 
6: # ── Platform detection ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
7: case "$(uname -s)" in
8:   Darwin) OS="mac"  ;;
9:   Linux)  OS="linux" ;;
10:   *)      echo "Unsupported OS: $(uname -s)"; exit 1 ;;
11: esac
12: 
13: case "$(uname -m)" in
14:   x86_64|amd64)  ARCH="x64" ;;
15:   aarch64|arm64) ARCH="aarch64" ;;
16:   *) echo "Unsupported arch: $(uname -m)"; exit 1 ;;
17: esac
18: 
19: echo "Detected: $OS / $ARCH"
20: 
21: # ── 1. Download OpenJDK 21 LTS ──────────────────────────────────────────────
22: JDK_DIR="$SCRIPT_DIR/jdk/$OS"
23: mkdir -p "$JDK_DIR"
24: 
25: if [ -z "$(ls -A "$JDK_DIR" 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
26:   echo "Downloading OpenJDK 21 LTS for $OS/$ARCH..."
27: 
28:   # Adoptium API
29:   API_URL="https://api.adoptium.net/v3/binary/latest/21/ga/$OS/$ARCH/jdk/hotspot/normal/eclipse"
30:   case "$OS" in
31:     mac)  JDK_ARCHIVE="openjdk21-mac.tar.gz" ;;
32:     linux) JDK_ARCHIVE="openjdk21-linux.tar.gz" ;;
33:   esac
34: 
35:   curl -fsSL -o "$SCRIPT_DIR/$JDK_ARCHIVE" "$API_URL"
36:   tar -xzf "$SCRIPT_DIR/$JDK_ARCHIVE" -C "$JDK_DIR" --strip-components=1 2>/dev/null || \
37:     tar -xzf "$SCRIPT_DIR/$JDK_ARCHIVE" -C "$JDK_DIR"
38:   rm -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/$JDK_ARCHIVE"
39:   echo "OpenJDK 21 downloaded to $JDK_DIR"
40: else
41:   echo "OpenJDK already present in $JDK_DIR"
42: fi
43: 
44: # Find actual JDK home
45: if [ "$OS" = "mac" ]; then
46:   JAVA_HOME_PATH=$(ls -d "$JDK_DIR"/jdk-*/Contents/Home 2>/dev/null | head -1)
47: else
48:   JAVA_HOME_PATH=$(ls -d "$JDK_DIR"/jdk-* 2>/dev/null | head -1)
49: fi
50: if [ -z "$JAVA_HOME_PATH" ]; then
51:   # Try flat extraction
52:   if [ -x "$JDK_DIR/bin/java" ]; then
53:     JAVA_HOME_PATH="$JDK_DIR"
54:   else
55:     echo "Cannot find JDK installation in $JDK_DIR"
56:     exit 1
57:   fi
58: fi
59: echo "JDK home: $JAVA_HOME_PATH"
60: 
61: # Set TFOCR_JAVA_HOME for subsequent steps
62: export TFOCR_JAVA_HOME="$JAVA_HOME_PATH"
63: 
64: # ── 2. Download Tesseract language data ─────────────────────────────────────
65: TESSDATA_DIR="$SCRIPT_DIR/tessdata"
66: mkdir -p "$TESSDATA_DIR"
67: if [ ! -f "$TESSDATA_DIR/eng.traineddata" ]; then
68:   echo "Downloading eng.traineddata..."
69:   curl -fsSL -o "$TESSDATA_DIR/eng.traineddata" \
70:     "https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tessdata/raw/main/eng.traineddata"
71:   echo "Tessdata downloaded to $TESSDATA_DIR"
72: else
73:   echo "eng.traineddata already present"
74: fi
75: 
76: # ── 3. Set up native binaries ───────────────────────────────────────────────
77: NATIVE_DIR="$SCRIPT_DIR/native/$OS"
78: mkdir -p "$NATIVE_DIR"
79: 
80: case "$OS" in
81:   linux)
82:     echo "Checking system Tesseract/Leptonica..."
83:     if ! command -v tesseract &>/dev/null; then
84:       echo "Installing tesseract-ocr..."
85:       apt-get update && apt-get install -y tesseract-ocr libtesseract-dev libleptonica-dev
86:     else
87:       echo "Tesseract found: $(tesseract --version 2>&1 | head -1)"
88:     fi
89: 
90:     # Create symlinks for Tess4J/lept4j JNA lookup
91:     for lib in libtesseract liblept; do
92:       if [ ! -f "$NATIVE_DIR/$lib.so" ]; then
93:         SO_FILE=$(find /usr/lib -name "${lib}.so.*" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
94:         if [ -n "$SO_FILE" ]; then
95:           ln -sf "$SO_FILE" "$NATIVE_DIR/$lib.so"
96:           echo "Symlinked $NATIVE_DIR/$lib.so -> $SO_FILE"
97:         fi
98:       fi
99:     done
100:     # lept4j looks for "leptonica", not "liblept"
101:     if [ ! -f "$NATIVE_DIR/libleptonica.so" ]; then
102:       LEPT_FILE=$(find /usr/lib -name "liblept.so.*" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
103:       if [ -n "$LEPT_FILE" ]; then
104:         ln -sf "$LEPT_FILE" "$NATIVE_DIR/libleptonica.so"
105:         echo "Symlinked $NATIVE_DIR/libleptonica.so -> $LEPT_FILE"
106:       fi
107:     fi
108:     ;;
109: 
110:   mac)
111:     echo "Checking system Tesseract/Leptonica..."
112:     if ! command -v tesseract &>/dev/null; then
113:       echo "Installing tesseract via Homebrew..."
114:       brew install tesseract
115:     else
116:       echo "Tesseract found: $(tesseract --version 2>&1 | head -1)"
117:     fi
118: 
119:     # Symlink dylibs
120:     for lib in libtesseract liblept; do
121:       if [ ! -f "$NATIVE_DIR/$lib.dylib" ]; then
122:         DYLIB_FILE=$(find /usr/local/lib /opt/homebrew/lib -name "${lib}*.dylib" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
123:         if [ -n "$DYLIB_FILE" ]; then
124:           ln -sf "$DYLIB_FILE" "$NATIVE_DIR/$lib.dylib"
125:           echo "Symlinked $NATIVE_DIR/$lib.dylib -> $DYLIB_FILE"
126:         fi
127:       fi
128:     done
129:     if [ ! -f "$NATIVE_DIR/libleptonica.dylib" ]; then
130:       LEPT_FILE=$(find /usr/local/lib /opt/homebrew/lib -name "liblept*.dylib" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
131:       if [ -n "$LEPT_FILE" ]; then
132:         ln -sf "$LEPT_FILE" "$NATIVE_DIR/libleptonica.dylib"
133:         echo "Symlinked $NATIVE_DIR/libleptonica.dylib -> $LEPT_FILE"
134:       fi
135:     fi
136:     ;;
137: esac
138: 
139: # ── 4. Download jbig2enc ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
140: if [ ! -x "$NATIVE_DIR/jbig2enc" ] && [ "$OS" != "win" ]; then
141:   echo "Downloading jbig2enc for $OS/$ARCH..."
142:   case "$OS" in
143:     linux)
144:       # Build from source (simplest portable approach)
145:       if command -v cmake &>/dev/null && command -v g++ &>/dev/null; then
146:         BUILD_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
147:         (
148:           cd "$BUILD_DIR"
149:           git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/agl/jbig2enc.git
150:           cd jbig2enc
151:           ./configure && make
152:           cp jbig2enc "$NATIVE_DIR/"
153:         )
154:         rm -rf "$BUILD_DIR"
155:         echo "jbig2enc built and copied to $NATIVE_DIR"
156:       else
157:         echo "WARNING: cmake/g++ not found. Skipping jbig2enc build."
158:         echo "Install build tools and run again, or download jbig2enc manually."
159:       fi
160:       ;;
161:     mac)
162:       if command -v brew &>/dev/null; then
163:         brew install agl-jbig2enc 2>/dev/null || brew install jbig2enc 2>/dev/null || {
164:           echo "jbig2enc not available via brew. Building from source..."
165:           BUILD_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
166:           (
167:             cd "$BUILD_DIR"
168:             git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/agl/jbig2enc.git
169:             cd jbig2enc
170:             ./configure && make
171:             cp jbig2enc "$NATIVE_DIR/"
172:           )
173:           rm -rf "$BUILD_DIR"
174:         }
175:         JBIG_BIN=$(which jbig2enc 2>/dev/null || echo "")
176:         if [ -n "$JBIG_BIN" ]; then
177:           cp "$JBIG_BIN" "$NATIVE_DIR/"
178:         fi
179:       fi
180:       ;;
181:   esac
182: fi
183: 
184: # ── 5. Create run.sh convenience wrapper ────────────────────────────────────
185: if [ ! -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/run.sh" ]; then
186:   echo '#!/usr/bin/env bash
187: set -euo pipefail
188: SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
189: export TFOCR_JAVA_HOME="$SCRIPT_DIR/jdk/'"$OS"'"'"'"'"
190: exec "$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java" -jar "$SCRIPT_DIR/build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar" "$@"' > "$SCRIPT_DIR/run.sh"
191:   chmod +x "$SCRIPT_DIR/run.sh"
192:   echo "Created run.sh"
193: fi
194: 
195: echo ""
196: echo "── Bootstrap complete ──"
197: echo "Run:  ./run.sh input.pdf -o output.pdf"
198: echo "Build: $TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build"

(End of file - total 198 lines)
</content>
```

### Tool: read

**Input:**

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/tests/generate_test_pdfs.py"
}
```

**Output:**

```
<path>/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/tests/generate_test_pdfs.py</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env python3
2: """Generate test PDFs for TrulyFreeOCR test-driven development.
3: 
4: Each PDF is image-based (non-searchable) to simulate scanned documents.
5: Creates files in tests/test-files/. Run from project root.
6: """
7: 
8: import io
9: import os
10: from PIL import Image, ImageDraw, ImageFont
11: import fitz  # PyMuPDF
12: 
13: OUT_DIR = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "test-files")
14: os.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
15: 
16: FONT_DIR = "/usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu"
17: W, H = 612, 792  # US Letter at 72 DPI
18: MARGIN = 50
19: FONT_SZ = 14
20: 
21: def font(name="DejaVuSans.ttf", sz=FONT_SZ):
22:     return ImageFont.truetype(os.path.join(FONT_DIR, name), sz)
23: 
24: def new_page():
25:     img = Image.new("RGB", (W, H), "white")
26:     draw = ImageDraw.Draw(img)
27:     return img, draw
28: 
29: def save_image_pdf(pages, path):
30:     doc = fitz.open()
31:     for img in pages:
32:         buf = io.BytesIO()
33:         img.save(buf, format="JPEG", quality=95)
34:         buf.seek(0)
35:         pix = fitz.Pixmap(buf.read())
36:         page = doc.new_page(width=W, height=H)
37:         page.insert_image(fitz.Rect(0, 0, W, H), pixmap=pix)
38:     doc.save(path)
39:     doc.close()
40: 
41: 
42: # ── 1. blank.pdf ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43: def make_blank():
44:     img, _ = new_page()
45:     save_image_pdf([img], os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "blank.pdf"))
46:     print("  blank.pdf")
47: 
48: # ── 2. simple-text.pdf ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
49: def make_simple_text():
50:     img, d = new_page()
51:     fnt = font()
52:     lines = [
53:         "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.",
54:         "Pack my box with five dozen liquor jugs.",
55:         "Sphinx of black quartz, judge my vow.",
56:         "",
57:         "This is a test document for OCR validation.",
58:         "It contains English text rendered as an image,",
59:         "simulating a scanned page that is not searchable.",
60:         "",
61:         "The pipeline should extract this text via Tesseract,",
62:         "then embed it as invisible text over the image.",
63:         "The output PDF should be searchable while retaining",
64:         "the original visual appearance.",
65:     ]
66:     y = MARGIN
67:     for line in lines:
68:         d.text((MARGIN, y), line, fill="black", font=fnt)
69:         y += 22
70:     save_image_pdf([img], os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "simple-text.pdf"))
71:     print("  simple-text.pdf")
72: 
73: # ── 3. multi-page.pdf ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
74: def make_multi_page():
75:     pages = []
76:     page_data = [
77:         ["Page 1: Introduction",
78:          "This is the first page of a multi-page document.",
79:          "It contains introductory text about the topic.",
80:          "Each page has different content to test that",
81:          "the OCR pipeline processes all pages correctly."],
82:         ["Page 2: Methodology",
83:          "The approach uses a 6-step pipeline:",
84:          "1. Page extraction via PDFBox at 300 DPI",
85:          "2. Image segmentation via Leptonica (lept4j)",
86:          "3. JBIG2 compression of text mask",
87:          "4. OCR via Tesseract (Tess4J)",
88:          "5. PDF re-assembly with searchable text",
89:          "6. Metadata preservation (bookmarks, annotations)"],
90:         ["Page 3: Results",
91:          "The output is a searchable, highly-compressed PDF",
92:          "that maintains the original visual appearance",
93:          "while adding a hidden text layer for searching.",
94:          "All metadata from the source PDF is preserved."],
95:     ]
96:     for title, *body in page_data:
97:         img, d = new_page()
98:         d.text((MARGIN, MARGIN), title, fill="black",
99:                font=font("DejaVuSans.ttf", 18))
100:         y = MARGIN + 40
101:         for line in body:
102:             d.text((MARGIN, y), line, fill="black", font=font())
103:             y += 22
104:         pages.append(img)
105:     save_image_pdf(pages, os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "multi-page.pdf"))
106:     print("  multi-page.pdf")
107: 
108: # ── 4. two-column.pdf ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
109: def make_two_column():
110:     img, d = new_page()
111:     col_w = (W - 3 * MARGIN) // 2
112:     x1, x2 = MARGIN, MARGIN * 2 + col_w
113:     fnt = font()
114:     body = (
115:         "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing "
116:         "elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et "
117:         "dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis "
118:         "nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut aliquip "
119:         "ex ea commodo consequat. Duis aute irure dolor in "
120:         "reprehenderit in voluptate velit esse cillum dolore "
121:         "eu fugiat nulla pariatur. Excepteur sint occaecat "
122:         "cupidatat non proident, sunt in culpa qui officia "
123:         "deserunt mollit anim id est laborum."
124:     )
125:     # Wrap into ~40-char lines
126:     words = body.split()
127:     lines = []
128:     for i in range(0, len(words), 8):
129:         lines.append(" ".join(words[i:i+8]))
130: 
131:     # Left column
132:     d.text((x1, MARGIN), "Left Column", fill="black",
133:            font=font("DejaVuSans.ttf", 16))
134:     y = MARGIN + 30
135:     for line in lines:
136:         d.text((x1, y), line, fill="black", font=fnt)
137:         y += 18
138: 
139:     # Right column
140:     d.text((x2, MARGIN), "Right Column", fill="black",
141:            font=font("DejaVuSans.ttf", 16))
142:     y = MARGIN + 30
143:     for line in lines:
144:         d.text((x2, y), line, fill="black", font=fnt)
145:         y += 18
146: 
147:     save_image_pdf([img], os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "two-column.pdf"))
148:     print("  two-column.pdf")
149: 
150: # ── 5. with-annotations.pdf ─────────────────────────────────────────────
151: def make_with_annotations():
152:     pages = []
153:     # Create multi-page document with bookmarks
154:     titles = ["Chapter 1: Getting Started",
155:               "Chapter 2: Configuration",
156:               "Chapter 3: Usage"]
157:     for i, title in enumerate(titles, 1):
158:         img, d = new_page()
159:         d.text((MARGIN, MARGIN), title, fill="black",
160:                font=font("DejaVuSans.ttf", 18))
161:         d.text((MARGIN, MARGIN + 40),
162:                f"This is page {i} of the annotated document.",
163:                fill="black", font=font())
164:         d.text((MARGIN, MARGIN + 70),
165:                "This PDF includes bookmarks and annotations.",
166:                fill="black", font=font())
167:         pages.append(img)
168: 
169:     doc = fitz.open()
170:     for i, img in enumerate(pages):
171:         buf = io.BytesIO()
172:         img.save(buf, format="JPEG", quality=95)
173:         buf.seek(0)
174:         pix = fitz.Pixmap(buf.read())
175:         page = doc.new_page(width=W, height=H)
176:         page.insert_image(fitz.Rect(0, 0, W, H), pixmap=pix)
177: 
178:         # Add text annotation on first page
179:         if i == 0:
180:             annot = page.add_text_annot(
181:                 (100, 100), "This is a sticky note annotation."
182:             )
183: 
184:         # Add underline annotation on first page
185:         if i == 0:
186:             page.add_underline_annot(
187:                 fitz.Rect(MARGIN, MARGIN, MARGIN + 200, MARGIN + 20)
188:             )
189: 
190:     # Add bookmarks (outline)
191:     toc = [
192:         [1, "Chapter 1", 1],
193:         [1, "Chapter 2", 2],
194:         [1, "Chapter 3", 3],
195:     ]
196:     doc.set_toc(toc)
197: 
198:     doc.save(os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "with-annotations.pdf"))
199:     doc.close()
200:     print("  with-annotations.pdf")
201: 
202: # ── 6. noisy-scan.pdf ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
203: def make_noisy_scan():
204:     import random
205:     random.seed(42)
206:     img, d = new_page()
207:     fnt = font()
208: 
209:     lines = [
210:         "This page simulates a noisy scanned document.",
211:         "It includes speckles, uneven lighting, and",
212:         "slight rotation to test image preprocessing.",
213:     ]
214:     y = MARGIN
215:     for line in lines:
216:         d.text((MARGIN, y), line, fill="black", font=fnt)
217:         y += 22
218: 
219:     # Add noise
220:     pixels = img.load()
221:     for _ in range(3000):
222:         x = random.randint(0, W - 1)
223:         y = random.randint(0, H - 1)
224:         v = random.randint(0, 60)
225:         pixels[x, y] = (v, v, v)
226: 
227:     # Add a gradient (simulating uneven lighting)
228:     for y in range(H):
229:         shade = int(30 * (y / H))
230:         for x in range(W):
231:             r, g, b = pixels[x, y]
232:             pixels[x, y] = (
233:                 max(0, r - shade),
234:                 max(0, g - shade),
235:                 max(0, b - shade),
236:             )
237: 
238:     save_image_pdf([img], os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "noisy-scan.pdf"))
239:     print("  noisy-scan.pdf")
240: 
241: 
242: if __name__ == "__main__":
243:     print("Generating test PDFs...")
244:     make_blank()
245:     make_simple_text()
246:     make_multi_page()
247:     make_two_column()
248:     make_with_annotations()
249:     make_noisy_scan()
250:     print(f"\nDone. Files in {OUT_DIR}/")

(End of file - total 250 lines)
</content>
```

### Tool: read

**Input:**

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/README.md"
}
```

**Output:**

```
<path>/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/README.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # TrulyFreeOCR
2: 
3: Commercially-safe OCR pipeline for non-searchable PDFs. All dependencies use permissive open-source licenses (Apache 2.0 / MIT / BSD) — no AGPL, no proprietary components.
4: 
5: **Input**: PDF with non-searchable pages (image-only scans)  
6: **Output**: Searchable, compressed PDF
7: 
8: ---
9: 
10: ## Pipeline
11: 
12: ```
13: Input PDF
14:     |
15:     v
16: PageExtractor  — PDFBox PDFRenderer at 300 DPI → per-page BufferedImage
17:     |
18:     v
19: ImageSegmenter — Pure-Java foreground/background segmentation:
20:                    grayscale → background normalize → Otsu binarize → inpaint
21:     |
22:     +----+----+
23:     |         |
24:     v         v
25: JBIG2Comp.   OCREngine      (parallel)
26:     |         |
27:     | jbig2enc| Tesseract CLI
28:     | subproc | word-level bboxes with TSV output
29:     |         |
30:     +----+----+
31:     |
32:     v
33: PDFAssembler — JPEG background + invisible OCR text (rendering mode 3)
34:     |
35:     v
36: Output PDF (searchable, compressed)
37: ```
38: 
39: ---
40: 
41: ## Implemented Components
42: 
43: ### PageExtractor
44: Renders each PDF page at 300 DPI via PDFBox's `PDFRenderer.renderImageWithDPI()`.
45: 
46: **Algorithm**: Standard PDF rasterisation. US Letter at 300 DPI produces 2550×3300 px images. Uses PDFBox's SFF (subpixel font finalizer) for high-quality text rendering.
47: 
48: ### ImageSegmenter
49: Pure-Java page segmentation (no Leptonica dependency). Four sub-steps:
50: 
51: 1. **Grayscale conversion** — ITU-R BT.601 luma formula: `Y = 0.299R + 0.587G + 0.114B`  
52:    Perception-weighted coefficients matching human visual sensitivity (most sensitive to green, least to blue).
53: 
54: 2. **Background normalization** — Corrects non-uniform illumination (shadows, scanner vignetting).
55:    - Divides the image into 64×64 px tiles
56:    - Estimates background level per tile using the **95th percentile** of pixel intensities (text is dark, so the bright tail of the histogram represents the page surface)
57:    - Builds a smooth background surface via **bilinear interpolation** between tile centres
58:    - Stretches each pixel: `new = old × 255 / bg_estimate` (background pixels map to white, text stays dark)
59: 
60: 3. **Otsu binarization** — Automatically finds the optimal threshold by maximising **inter-class variance**:
61:    `σ² = wB × wF × (μB − μF)²` where `w` = class weight and `μ` = class mean.
62:    Exhaustively searches all 256 grayscale levels for the split with the best separation.
63: 
64: 4. **Inpainting** — Replaces foreground (text) pixels with the nearest background pixel colour within a 3-pixel radius. Produces a cleaned background layer for PDF re-assembly.
65: 
66: ### OCREngine
67: Runs Tesseract as a **subprocess** (not JNA/Tess4J) to avoid native-library version mismatches with system Leptonica.
68: 
69: - Input: BufferedImage → temp PNG
70: - CLI flags: `tesseract input.png output -l eng --psm 1 tsv`
71: - Output: TSV file parsed for **word-level** bounding boxes (`level=5` rows with `left, top, width, height, conf, text`)
72: - Each word returned as a `TextBlock` with bounding box and confidence score
73: 
74: ### JBIG2Compressor
75: Attempts compression via **jbig2enc** subprocess with `-p -s` flags (symbol table + smearing refinement).
76: 
77: - jbig2enc outputs a `.sym` file (global symbol dictionary) and a `.jbig2` file (page data), concatenated for PDF JBIG2Decode streams
78: - Falls back to PNG byte-array output when jbig2enc is not available
79: 
80: ### PDFAssembler
81: Re-assembles the output PDF using PDFBox:
82: 
83: - **Background layer**: Cleaned background image embedded via `LosslessFactory.createFromImage()`
84: - **Text layer**: OCR words at their bounding-box positions, rendered in **mode 3 (NEITHER)** — invisible on screen/print, but selectable and searchable
85: - **Coordinate transform**: `scaleX = pageWidth_pts / imageWidth_px`, Y-flip because image origin is top-left and PDF origin is bottom-left
86: - Uses Helvetica (Standard 14 Font) — no embedding needed
87: 
88: ---
89: 
90: ## Building
91: 
92: ```bash
93: # Build the fat JAR
94: ./gradlew build
95: 
96: # Run all tests
97: ./gradlew test
98: ```
99: 
100: Requires **Java 21+** (JDK) and **Tesseract OCR** CLI (`tesseract` on PATH) with English language data installed.
101: 
102: ## Testing
103: 
104: Tests use generated sample PDFs in `tests/test-files/`:
105: - `blank.pdf` — single blank US Letter page
106: - `simple-text.pdf` — one page with lorem ipsum text
107: - `multi-page.pdf` — three-page document with distinct content per page
108: - `two-column.pdf` — two-column layout
109: - `with-annotations.pdf` — bookmarks, links, and comments
110: - `noisy-scan.pdf` — simulated scanner noise and skew
111: 
112: Regenerate test PDFs with:
113: ```bash
114: python tests/generate_test_pdfs.py
115: ```
116: 
117: ## Current Status
118: 
119: | Phase | Component | Status |
120: |-------|-----------|--------|
121: | 1 | Gradle scaffold + CLI | Done |
122: | 2 | PageExtractor | Done |
123: | 3 | OCREngine | Done |
124: | 4 | ImageSegmenter | Done |
125: | 5 | PDFAssembler (basic) | Done |
126: | 6 | JBIG2Compressor + SubprocessRunner | Done |
127: | 7 | PDFAssembler JBIG2 integration | Pending |
128: | 8 | MetadataPreserver | Pending |
129: | 9 | bootstrap scripts | Pending |
130: | 10 | Integration tests | Pending |
131: 
132: ## Dependencies
133: 
134: ### Build & Runtime (from Gradle)
135: 
136: | Dependency | Version | License | Project |
137: |---|---|---|---|
138: | Tess4J | 5.18.0 | Apache 2.0 | https://github.com/nguyenq/tess4j |
139: | picocli | 4.7.6 | Apache 2.0 | https://picocli.info |
140: | PDFBox | 3.0.6 | Apache 2.0 | https://pdfbox.apache.org |
141: | pdfbox-tools | 3.0.6 | Apache 2.0 | https://pdfbox.apache.org |
142: | JNA | 5.18.1 | Apache 2.0 / LGPL 2.1 | https://github.com/java-native-access/jna |
143: | jai-imageio-core | 1.4.0 | BSD 3-Clause | https://github.com/jai-imageio/jai-imageio-core |
144: | commons-io | 2.21.0 | Apache 2.0 | https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-io/ |
145: | lept4j | 1.23.0 | Apache 2.0 | https://github.com/nguyenq/lept4j |
146: | jboss-vfs | 3.2.17.Final | Apache 2.0 | https://github.com/jbossas/jboss-vfs |
147: | slf4j-api | 2.0.17 | MIT | https://www.slf4j.org |
148: | jbig2-imageio | 3.0.4 | Apache 2.0 | https://pdfbox.apache.org |
149: | bcpkix-jdk18on | 1.82 | Bouncy Castle (MIT-like) | https://www.bouncycastle.org |
150: | Shadow plugin | 8.1.1 | Apache 2.0 | https://github.com/johnrengelman/shadow |
151: 
152: ### Test (from Gradle)
153: 
154: | Dependency | Version | License | Project |
155: |---|---|---|---|
156: | JUnit Jupiter | 5.11.4 | EPL 2.0 | https://junit.org/junit5/ |
157: | JUnit Platform Launcher | 1.11.4 | EPL 2.0 | https://junit.org/junit5/ |
158: 
159: ### Native / Bundled
160: 
161: | Dependency | License | Project |
162: |---|---|---|
163: | OpenJDK 21 LTS | GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception | https://adoptium.net |
164: | Gradle | Apache 2.0 | https://gradle.org |
165: | Tesseract OCR | Apache 2.0 | https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tesseract |
166: | Leptonica | BSD 2-Clause | https://github.com/DanBloomberg/leptonica |
167: | jbig2enc | Apache 2.0 | https://github.com/agl/jbig2enc |
168: 
169: ## License
170: 
171: TrulyFreeOCR itself is made available under the Apache 2.0 License. All dependencies listed above use permissive open-source licenses compatible with commercial use. A full `NOTICE.md` with copyright attributions is included in the repository.

(End of file - total 171 lines)
</content>

1: # TrulyFreeOCR — Notice
2: 
3: This file contains attribution notices for third-party dependencies used by TrulyFreeOCR, as required by the Apache License, Version 2.0 (Section 4) and BSD/MIT attribution requirements.
4: 
5: ---
6:

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Now I have a comprehensive list of all hardcoded values. I need to:

1. Create a `settings.jsonc` file in the project root with the customizable settings (with comments)
2. Add code to load and use the settings

Let me plan the settings structure. I should focus on the most impactful settings that users would actually want to customize. Not all 93 values need to be in settings - many are internal constants, build versions, test fixtures, etc.

The key configurables that should go into settings.jsonc:

**Pipeline settings:**
- `tesseract.path` - path to tesseract binary (default "tesseract")
- `tesseract.language` - language model (default "eng")
- `tesseract.psm` - page segmentation mode (default 1)
- `tesseract.tessdataDir` - tessdata directory
- `native.dir` - native binaries directory (default "./native")
- `rendering.dpi` - PDF rendering DPI (default 300)
- `segmenter.tileSize` - background normalization tile size (default 64)
- `segmenter.percentile` - background level percentile (default 0.95)
- `segmenter.inpaintRadius` - inpainting search radius (default 3)
- `jbig2enc.flags` - jbig2enc CLI flags (default "-p -s")
- `pdf.font` - PDF font for OCR text (default "HELVETICA")
- `pdf.minFontSize` - minimum font size (default 1)

**Output:**
- `output.file` - default output filename (default "output.pdf")

**Paths:**
- `tessdata.dir` - tessdata directory
- `native.dir` - native binaries directory

Let me also think about what to make the settings loader. A simple class `Settings` that loads `settings.jsonc` from the current directory (or a specified path), with sensible defaults for everything.

I'll use the standard Java JSON parsing... but wait, we don't have a JSON library in our dependencies. Let me check what's available. We have picocli and PDFBox. PDFBox may have some JSON capabilities, but not really. We could:
1. Use the built-in Nashorn/scripting... no, that's removed in JDK 21.
2. Add a dependency like Gson or Jackson - but that's heavy.
3. Parse the JSON manually - simple enough for a flat config.
4. Use a properties file instead.
5. Use picocli's built-in config file support (picocli has `@Command` with `scope = ScopeType.INHERIT` and config file support)

Actually, picocli 4.7+ has excellent config file support built-in. It can read from `application.json`, `application.yaml`, etc. Let me check the picocli docs. Yes, picocli supports `@Command` with `scope = ScopeType.INHERIT` and `@Option` with `defaultValue` that can be overridden from config files.

But the user specifically asked for `settings.jsonc` (with comments). JSONC is JSON with comments. picocli's config file support is for standard JSON/YAML/properties. I should probably implement a custom loader that handles JSONC (strip comments, then parse).

Let me think about the approach:

Option A: Use picocli's built-in config file support
- Pros: Minimal code, uses existing dependency
- Cons: No JSONC support (only standard JSON), no comments

Option B: Write a simple Settings class that reads JSONC
- Pros: Full control, JSONC with comments
- Cons: Need to parse JSON (or add a dependency)

Option C: Use java.util.Properties format
- Pros: Built-in, no deps
- Cons: No comments support in a single file... actually .properties supports comments with `#` or `!`

Actually, you know what, let me just write a simple Settings class that:
1. Reads the file
2. Strips comments (both `//` and `/* */`)
3. Parses a simple key-value JSON structure (we don't need full JSON parser for flat objects)
4. Provides typed getters with defaults

Or even simpler: I can use a lightweight approach with `javax.json` or just roll my own simple key-value parser for JSONC.

Actually, the simplest and most robust approach: write a proper settings class that reads a JSONC file using a minimal recursive descent parser. Given the settings are flat (one level deep), this is very easy.

Let me design the Settings class:

```java
package com.trulyfreeocr.util;

public class Settings {
    private static Settings instance;
    private final Path filePath;
    private Map<String, String> values;
    
    // Default values
    public static final String DEFAULT_TESSERACT_PATH = "tesseract";
    public static final String DEFAULT_TESSERACT_LANGUAGE = "eng";
    // ... etc
    
    public Settings() { ... }
    public void load(Path path) { ... }
    public void loadDefaults() { ... }
    
    // Getters with defaults
    public String getTesseractPath() { ... }
    public String getTesseractLanguage() { ... }
    public int getTesseractPsm() { ... }
    // ... etc
}
```

Wait, actually, I should integrate this into the existing code. The settings need to be used by:
- `TrulyFreeOCR.java` - CLI entry point (passes options to pipeline)
- `OCREngine.java` - tessdata dir, tesseract path, language, PSM
- `PageExtractor.java` - DPI
- `ImageSegmenter.java` - tile size, percentile, inpaint radius
- `JBIG2Compressor.java` - native dir, jbig2enc flags
- `PDFAssembler.java` - font, min font size

Currently, `TrulyFreeOCR` accepts `--native-dir`, `--tessdata-dir`, `--output`. These CLI args should override settings.jsonc values.

Let me think about the precedence:
1. CLI arguments (highest priority)
2. settings.jsonc values
3. Hardcoded defaults (lowest priority)

And `TrulyFreeOCR` already passes these as parameters to the pipeline components. So the settings file just needs to be loaded and the values used as defaults for CLI options.

Actually, the cleanest integration is:
1. `Settings` loads from `settings.jsonc` (or defaults)
2. `TrulyFreeOCR` uses Settings values as `defaultValue` replacements (via picocli's defaultValue)
3. Pipeline components use passed-in values from CLI, which were defaulted from Settings

Hmm, but picocli's `defaultValue` is a string annotation value - it can't dynamically read from a file at annotation-processing time. So I need a different approach.

Let me reconsider. The flow should be:
1. `TrulyFreeOCR.main()` loads `Settings` from `settings.jsonc`
2. CLI parsing happens with programmatic defaults from Settings
3. Overridden CLI values take precedence
4. Final resolved values are passed to pipeline components

Actually, picocli supports `defaultValue` with `@Option` and you can also use `scope = ScopeType.INHERIT` with config file support. But the simplest approach:

In `TrulyFreeOCR.java`:
```java
@Command(name = "trulyfreeocr", ...)
public class TrulyFreeOCR implements Callable<Integer> {
    @Option(names = {"-o", "--output"}, description = "Output PDF")
    private File output;
    
    @Option(names = {"--native-dir"}, description = "Native binaries directory")
    private File nativeDir;
    
    // etc.
    
    @Override
    public Integer call() {
        // Load settings as defaults if not specified via CLI
        Settings settings = Settings.load();
        if (output == null) output = settings.getOutputFile();
        if (nativeDir == null) nativeDir = settings.getNativeDir();
        // ... etc
    }
}
```

This is cleaner. Settings holds defaults, CLI args override them.

OK let me proceed with implementation. I'll:

1. Create `settings.jsonc` with all configurable parameters and comments
2. Create `Settings.java` - a simple loader that reads JSONC (JSON with comments)
3. Update `TrulyFreeOCR.java` to load Settings and use them as defaults
4. Update pipeline components to receive configured values (they mostly already do via constructor/parameters)

Let me look at the current code to understand the constructor patterns.

<content>
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.awt.Rectangle;
4: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
5: import java.io.BufferedReader;
6: import java.io.File;
7: import java.io.IOException;
8: import java.io.InputStreamReader;
9: import java.util.ArrayList;
10: import java.util.List;
11: 
12: import javax.imageio.ImageIO;
13: 
14: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;
15: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.TextBlock;
16: 
17: /**
18:  * OCR engine that delegates to the Tesseract CLI via subprocess.
19:  *
20:  * Tess4J's JNA binding was originally planned, but lept4j's
21:  * Leptonica version requires symbols not present in the system's
22:  * liblept.so.5.  Running tesseract as a subprocess (with TSV output)
23:  * avoids this native-library version mismatch entirely and is more
24:  * portable across Linux distributions.
25:  *
26:  * Tesseract output format: TSV (tab-separated values) with columns:
27:  *   level, page_num, block_num, par_num, line_num, word_num,
28:  *   left, top, width, height, conf, text
29:  *
30:  * CLI flags used:
31:  *   -l eng    — English language model
32:  *   --psm 1   — Automatic page segmentation with OSD
33:  *   tsv       — Output format: TSV (word-level bounding boxes)
34:  *
35:  * The output PageResult always reports pageNumber=1 because each
36:  * call processes exactly one BufferedImage.
37:  */
38: public class OCREngine {
39: 
40:     private final File tessdataDir;
41: 
42:     public OCREngine() {
43:         String tessdata = System.getenv("TESSDATA_PREFIX");
44:         if (tessdata != null && !tessdata.isEmpty()) {
45:             this.tessdataDir = new File(tessdata);
46:         } else {
47:             this.tessdataDir = new File("/usr/share/tesseract-ocr/5/tessdata");
48:         }
49:     }
50: 
51:     public PageResult ocr(BufferedImage image) throws IOException {
52:         int width = image.getWidth();
53:         int height = image.getHeight();
54: 
55:         // Write image to temp file
56:         File tempPng = File.createTempFile("tfocr-", ".png");
57:         tempPng.deleteOnExit();
58:         ImageIO.write(image, "png", tempPng);
59: 
60:         try {
61:             // Run tesseract with TSV output for word-level bounding boxes
62:             String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
63:             ProcessBuilder pb = new ProcessBuilder(
64:                     "tesseract",
65:                     tempPng.getAbsolutePath(),
66:                     prefix,
67:                     "-l", "eng",
68:                     "--psm", "1",
69:                     "tsv"
70:             );
71:             pb.environment().put("TESSDATA_PREFIX", tessdataDir.getAbsolutePath());
72:             pb.redirectErrorStream(true);
73: 
74:             Process process = pb.start();
75:             try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(
76:                     new InputStreamReader(process.getInputStream()))) {
77:                 // Read and discard stdout (status messages go here)
78:                 while (reader.readLine() != null) { }
79:             }
80:             int exitCode = process.waitFor();
81:             if (exitCode != 0) {
82:                 throw new IOException("Tesseract exited with code " + exitCode);
83:             }
84: 
85:             // Parse TSV output
86:             File tsvFile = new File(prefix + ".tsv");
87:             if (!tsvFile.exists()) {
88:                 return new PageResult(1, width, height, List.of());
89:             }
90: 
91:             List<TextBlock> blocks = parseTsv(tsvFile);
92:             return new PageResult(1, width, height, blocks);
93: 
94:         } catch (InterruptedException e) {
95:             Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
96:             throw new IOException("Tesseract was interrupted", e);
97:         } finally {
98:             // Cleanup temp files
99:             String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
100:             for (String ext : new String[]{".png", ".tsv"}) {
101:                 File f = new File(prefix + ext);
102:                 if (f.exists()) f.delete();
103:             }
104:         }
105:     }
106: 
107:     private List<TextBlock> parseTsv(File tsv) throws IOException {
108:         List<TextBlock> blocks = new ArrayList<>();
109:         try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(new java.io.FileReader(tsv))) {
110:             String header = reader.readLine(); // skip header
111:             if (header == null) return blocks;
112: 
113:             String line;
114:             while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null) {
115:                 if (line.trim().isEmpty()) continue;
116:                 String[] parts = line.split("\t");
117:                 // TSV columns: level, page_num, block_num, par_num, line_num, word_num,
118:                 //              left, top, width, height, conf, text
119:                 if (parts.length < 12) continue;
120: 
121:                 String level = parts[0];
122:                 String text = parts[11].trim();
123:                 if (!"5".equals(level) || text.isEmpty()) continue; // word level only
124: 
125:                 try {
126:                     int left = Integer.parseInt(parts[6]);
127:                     int top = Integer.parseInt(parts[7]);
128:                     int w = Integer.parseInt(parts[8]);
129:                     int h = Integer.parseInt(parts[9]);
130:                     double conf = Double.parseDouble(parts[10]);
131: 
132:                     blocks.add(new TextBlock(text, new Rectangle(left, top, w, h), conf));
133:                 } catch (NumberFormatException e) {
134:                     // skip malformed lines
135:                 }
136:             }
137:         }
138:         return blocks;
139:     }
140: }

(End of file - total 140 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
4: import java.io.File;
5: import java.io.IOException;
6: 
7: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;
8: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.CCITTFactory;
9: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.PDImageXObject;
10: 
11: import com.trulyfreeocr.util.PlatformUtils;
12: import com.trulyfreeocr.util.PlatformUtils.Os;
13: import com.trulyfreeocr.util.SubprocessRunner;
14: 
15: /**
16:  * Compresses a binary foreground mask via jbig2enc subprocess,
17:  * falling back to CCITT Group 4 fax encoding via PDFBox when
18:  * the native binary is unavailable.
19:  *
20:  * jbig2enc flags:
21:  *   -p  — Produce a global symbol dictionary (shared across pages).
22:  *   -s  — Refine smearing to improve symbol matching.
23:  */
24: public class JBIG2Compressor {
25: 
26:     /**
27:      * Holds compressed foreground-mask data.
28:      *
29:      * isJbig2  true = JBIG2 format (from jbig2enc); false = CCITT G4 fallback.
30:      * data     The compressed byte stream.
31:      * width    Original mask width  in pixels.
32:      * height   Original mask height in pixels.
33:      */
34:     public static class CompressionResult {
35:         private final boolean isJbig2;
36:         private final byte[] data;
37:         private final int width;
38:         private final int height;
39: 
40:         public CompressionResult(byte[] data, int width, int height, boolean isJbig2) {
41:             this.data = data;
42:             this.width = width;
43:             this.height = height;
44:             this.isJbig2 = isJbig2;
45:         }
46: 
47:         public boolean isJbig2() { return isJbig2; }
48:         public byte[] getData() { return data; }
49:         public int getWidth() { return width; }
50:         public int getHeight() { return height; }
51:     }
52: 
53:     /**
54:      * Attempts JBIG2 compression via jbig2enc; falls back to CCITT G4 on failure.
55:      *
56:      * @param foregroundMask  Binary image (TYPE_BYTE_BINARY, black=foreground).
57:      * @return CompressionResult with either JBIG2 or CCITT G4 data.
58:      */
59:     public CompressionResult compress(BufferedImage foregroundMask) throws IOException {
60:         File jbig2enc = findJbig2enc();
61:         if (jbig2enc != null) {
62:             try {
63:                 return compressWithJbig2enc(jbig2enc, foregroundMask);
64:             } catch (IOException e) {
65:                 // jbig2enc failed → fall through to fallback
66:             }
67:         }
68:         return compressWithFallback(foregroundMask);
69:     }
70: 
71:     /**
72:      * Locates the jbig2enc binary for the current OS.
73:      *
74:      * Searches native/{os}/jbig2enc (or jbig2enc.exe on Windows).
75:      * The base path can be overridden via system property "tfocr.native.dir"
76:      * (defaults to "native").
77:      *
78:      * @return The executable File, or null if not found.
79:      */
80:     private File findJbig2enc() {
81:         String nativeDir = System.getProperty("tfocr.native.dir", "native");
82:         Os os = PlatformUtils.detectOs();
83:         String dirName;
84:         switch (os) {
85:             case WINDOWS: dirName = "win"; break;
86:             case MAC: dirName = "mac"; break;
87:             default: dirName = "linux";
88:         }
89:         File exe = new File(nativeDir + "/" + dirName, os == Os.WINDOWS ? "jbig2enc.exe" : "jbig2enc");
90:         if (exe.canExecute()) return exe;
91:         return null;
92:     }
93: 
94:     /**
95:      * Runs jbig2enc and concatenates global sym + page jbig2 into one byte array.
96:      *
97:      * PDF JBIG2Decode streams require global segments (symbol dictionary)
98:      * followed by page data — exactly the .sym + .jbig2 layout that
99:      * jbig2enc produces.
100:      */
101:     private CompressionResult compressWithJbig2enc(File jbig2enc, BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {
102:         File tempPng = File.createTempFile("tfocr-jbig2-", ".png");
103:         tempPng.deleteOnExit();
104:         javax.imageio.ImageIO.write(mask, "png", tempPng);
105: 
106:         try {
107:             String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
108: 
109:             SubprocessRunner.Result result = SubprocessRunner.run(jbig2enc,
110:                     "-p", "-s", tempPng.getAbsolutePath());
111:             if (result.getExitCode() != 0) {
112:                 throw new IOException("jbig2enc failed: " + result.getStderrString());
113:             }
114: 
115:             File symFile = new File(prefix + ".sym");
116:             File jbig2File = new File(prefix + ".jbig2");
117: 
118:             byte[] symData = symFile.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(symFile.toPath()) : new byte[0];
119:             byte[] jbig2Data = jbig2File.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(jbig2File.toPath()) : new byte[0];
120: 
121:             byte[] combined = new byte[symData.length + jbig2Data.length];
122:             System.arraycopy(symData, 0, combined, 0, symData.length);
123:             System.arraycopy(jbig2Data, 0, combined, symData.length, jbig2Data.length);
124: 
125:             return new CompressionResult(combined, mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), true);
126:         } finally {
127:             String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
128:             for (String ext : new String[]{".png", ".sym", ".jbig2"}) {
129:                 File f = new File(prefix + ext);
130:                 if (f.exists()) f.delete();
131:             }
132:         }
133:     }
134: 
135:     /**
136:      * Fallback: encodes the mask as CCITT Group 4 fax using PDFBox's CCITTFactory.
137:      */
138:     private CompressionResult compressWithFallback(BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {
139:         // Create a throwaway PDDocument to use CCITTFactory
140:         try (PDDocument doc = new PDDocument()) {
141:             PDImageXObject ccitt = CCITTFactory.createFromImage(doc, mask);
142:             return new CompressionResult(extractStreamBytes(ccitt), mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), false);
143:         }
144:     }
145: 
146:     private static byte[] extractStreamBytes(PDImageXObject image) throws IOException {
147:         try (java.io.InputStream is = image.createInputStream()) {
148:             return is.readAllBytes();
149:         }
150:     }
151: }

(End of file - total 151 lines)
</content>

**Output:**

```
<path>/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/ImageSegmenter.java</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
4: 
5: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.SegmentedImage;
6: 
7: public class ImageSegmenter {
8: 
9:     /**
10:      * Orchestrates the full page-segmentation pipeline:
11:      *   grayscale -> background normalization -> Otsu binarization -> inpaint background.
12:      *
13:      * @param image  Input RGB page image (e.g. 300 DPI PDFBox render).
14:      * @return       SegmentedImage holding a binary foreground mask and a cleaned background.
15:      */
16:     public SegmentedImage segment(BufferedImage image) {
17:         int width = image.getWidth();
18:         int height = image.getHeight();
19: 
20:         // Step 1: Convert to grayscale
21:         int[] gray = toGrayscale(image);
22: 
23:         // Step 2: Background normalization
24:         // tileSize=64: 64x64 pixel tiles; 2^6 chosen for fast bit ops during interpolation.
25:         int tileSize = 64;
26:         double[] bgNormalized = backgroundNormalize(gray, width, height, tileSize);
27: 
28:         // Step 3: Otsu binarization
29:         int threshold = otsuThreshold(bgNormalized);
30:         BufferedImage foregroundMask = new BufferedImage(width, height, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);
31:         for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {
32:             for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
33:                 int idx = y * width + x;
34:                 // Foreground = 0 (black), Background = 1 (white) in binary image
35:                 // Text is dark on light bg, so pixel <= threshold = foreground
36:                 if (gray[idx] <= threshold) {
37:                     foregroundMask.setRGB(x, y, 0xFF000000); // ARGB: black, full opacity
38:                 } else {
39:                     foregroundMask.setRGB(x, y, 0xFFFFFFFF); // ARGB: white, full opacity
40:                 }
41:             }
42:         }
43: 
44:         // Step 4: Cleaned background — fill text regions with surrounding color
45:         // radius=3: search up to 3 px away for a background pixel to sample from.
46:         BufferedImage cleanedBackground = inpaintBackground(image, foregroundMask, 3);
47: 
48:         return new SegmentedImage(foregroundMask, cleanedBackground);
49:     }
50: 
51:     /**
52:      * Converts an RGB BufferedImage to a flat int array of grayscale values
53:      * (0 = black, 255 = white) using the ITU-R BT.601 luma formula.
54:      *
55:      * Magic numbers in luma formula:
56:      *   0.299, 0.587, 0.114 — perception-weighted coefficients for R/G/B.
57:      *   Human vision is most sensitive to green, least to blue.
58:      *   These sum to 1.0, so neutral gray (R=G=B) maps to itself.
59:      *   This is the same formula used in JPEG, NTSC/PAL, and most image libraries.
60:      *
61:      * Bit shifts in RGB extraction:
62:      *   (rgb >> 16) & 0xFF  — extract the red  byte (bits 16-23 of 0xAARRGGBB)
63:      *   (rgb >>  8) & 0xFF  — extract the green byte (bits  8-15)
64:      *   rgb        & 0xFF  — extract the blue byte (bits  0-7)
65:      */
66:     private int[] toGrayscale(BufferedImage image) {
67:         int width = image.getWidth();
68:         int height = image.getHeight();
69:         int[] gray = new int[width * height];
70: 
71:         for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {
72:             for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
73:                 int rgb = image.getRGB(x, y);
74:                 int r = (rgb >> 16) & 0xFF;
75:                 int g = (rgb >> 8) & 0xFF;
76:                 int b = rgb & 0xFF;
77:                 gray[y * width + x] = (int) (0.299 * r + 0.587 * g + 0.114 * b);
78:             }
79:         }
80:         return gray;
81:     }
82: 
83:     /**
84:      * Tile-based background normalization that corrects non-uniform illumination
85:      * (e.g. shadows, gradients, scanner vignetting).
86:      *
87:      * How it works:
88:      *   1. Divide the image into a grid of tiles (default 64x64 px).
89:      *   2. In each tile, estimate the "background level" as the 95th percentile
90:      *      of pixel intensities. Since text is dark on a light background, the
91:      *      bright tail of the histogram represents the page surface (background).
92:      *      The 95th percentile is used (instead of max) to be robust to noise.
93:      *   3. Build a smooth background surface across the page using bilinear
94:      *      interpolation between tile centers.
95:      *   4. Each pixel is then stretched: new_value = old_value * 255 / bg_estimate.
96:      *      Pixels at or near the background level map to 255 (white); dark text
97:      *      pixels stay dark because their value is far below the bg estimate.
98:      *
99:      * Magic numbers:
100:      *   tileSize = 64 — balances accuracy vs performance. 2^6 allows fast
101:      *     integer arithmetic for interpolation (tile centres are at multiples
102:      *     of 64).  Smaller = more detail but slower; larger = faster but coarser.
103:      *   0.95 — 95th percentile.  Picked empirically: the top 5% of brightness
104:      *     values in a tile are assumed to be background, not text or noise.
105:      *   255.0 — maximum 8-bit grayscale value (white).
106:      */
107:     private double[] backgroundNormalize(int[] gray, int width, int height, int tileSize) {
108:         int tilesX = (int) Math.ceil((double) width / tileSize);
109:         int tilesY = (int) Math.ceil((double) height / tileSize);
110: 
111:         // Sample background level per tile (95th percentile = bright, since text is dark)
112:         double[][] bgLevels = new double[tilesY][tilesX];
113:         for (int ty = 0; ty < tilesY; ty++) {
114:             for (int tx = 0; tx < tilesX; tx++) {
115:                 int startX = tx * tileSize;
116:                 int startY = ty * tileSize;
117:                 int endX = Math.min(startX + tileSize, width);
118:                 int endY = Math.min(startY + tileSize, height);
119:                 int count = (endX - startX) * (endY - startY);
120:                 int[] sorted = new int[count];
121:                 int idx = 0;
122:                 for (int y = startY; y < endY; y++) {
123:                     for (int x = startX; x < endX; x++) {
124:                         sorted[idx++] = gray[y * width + x];
125:                     }
126:                 }
127:                 java.util.Arrays.sort(sorted);
128:                 // 95th percentile as background estimate
129:                 bgLevels[ty][tx] = sorted[(int) (count * 0.95)];
130:             }
131:         }
132: 
133:         // Bilinear interpolation of background at each pixel
134:         double[] result = new double[gray.length];
135:         for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {
136:             for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
137:                 float fx = (float) x / tileSize;
138:                 float fy = (float) y / tileSize;
139:                 int tx0 = Math.min((int) Math.floor(fx), tilesX - 1);
140:                 int tx1 = Math.min(tx0 + 1, tilesX - 1);
141:                 int ty0 = Math.min((int) Math.floor(fy), tilesY - 1);
142:                 int ty1 = Math.min(ty0 + 1, tilesY - 1);
143: 
144:                 double bg = bilinearInterp(
145:                         bgLevels[ty0][tx0], bgLevels[ty0][tx1],
146:                         bgLevels[ty1][tx0], bgLevels[ty1][tx1],
147:                         fx - tx0, fy - ty0);
148: 
149:                 int idx = y * width + x;
150:                 if (bg > 0) {
151:                     // Normalize: stretch so that bg -> 255
152:                     result[idx] = Math.min(255.0, gray[idx] * 255.0 / bg);
153:                 } else {
154:                     result[idx] = gray[idx];
155:                 }
156:             }
157:         }
158:         return result;
159:     }
160: 
161:     /**
162:      * Standard bilinear interpolation over a unit square [0,1]x[0,1].
163:      *
164:      *   v00 .. v11  — values at the four corners (top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right).
165:      *   fx, fy      — fractional offsets within the square (0..1).
166:      *
167:      * Interpolation is performed in two passes:
168:      *   1. Linearly interpolate horizontally between the top corners, and between the bottom corners.
169:      *   2. Linearly interpolate vertically between the two horizontal results.
170:      * This is equivalent to: v = (1-fy)*((1-fx)*v00 + fx*v01) + fy*((1-fx)*v10 + fx*v11)
171:      *
172:      * No magic numbers — pure linear algebra.
173:      */
174:     private double bilinearInterp(double v00, double v01, double v10, double v11, double fx, double fy) {
175:         double top = v00 + (v01 - v00) * fx;
176:         double bot = v10 + (v11 - v10) * fx;
177:         return top + (bot - top) * fy;
178:     }
179: 
180:     /**
181:      * Otsu's method for automatic image thresholding.
182:      *
183:      * Theory: exhaustively search all 256 possible threshold values and pick the
184:      * one that maximises the inter-class variance between foreground and background
185:      * pixel distributions.  This gives optimal separation without any user tuning.
186:      *
187:      * Algorithm steps:
188:      *   1. Build a 256-bin histogram of pixel intensities.
189:      *   2. For each threshold t (0..255), split pixels into two classes:
190:      *      class B (background, intensities > t) and class F (foreground, ≤ t).
191:      *   3. Compute the between-class variance:
192:      *          σ² = wB * wF * (μB - μF)²
193:      *      where w = weight (proportion of pixels), μ = mean intensity.
194:      *   4. Return the t that maximises σ².
195:      *
196:      * Magic numbers:
197:      *   256 — number of bins in an 8-bit grayscale histogram (2^8 possible values).
198:      *   wB, wF  — weights (class pixel counts).  These are integers but the formula
199:      *     uses them as the product to give heavier weight to balanced splits.
200:      */
201:     private int otsuThreshold(double[] pixels) {
202:         int[] histogram = new int[256];
203:         for (double p : pixels) {
204:             int val = Math.min(255, Math.max(0, (int) Math.round(p)));
205:             histogram[val]++;
206:         }
207: 
208:         int total = pixels.length;
209:         double sum = 0;
210:         for (int i = 0; i < 256; i++) {
211:             sum += i * histogram[i];
212:         }
213: 
214:         double sumB = 0;
215:         int wB = 0;
216:         double maxVariance = 0;
217:         int threshold = 0;
218: 
219:         for (int i = 0; i < 256; i++) {
220:             wB += histogram[i];
221:             if (wB == 0) continue;
222:             int wF = total - wB;
223:             if (wF == 0) break;
224: 
225:             sumB += i * histogram[i];
226:             double meanB = sumB / wB;
227:             double meanF = (sum - sumB) / wF;
228:             double variance = (double) wB * wF * (meanB - meanF) * (meanB - meanF);
229: 
230:             if (variance > maxVariance) {
231:                 maxVariance = variance;
232:                 threshold = i;
233:             }
234:         }
235:         return threshold;
236:     }
237: 
238:     /**
239:      * Simple inpainting: replace foreground (text) pixels with the nearest
240:      * background pixel colour found within a search radius.
241:      *
242:      * This produces a "cleaned" page where text regions are filled in with
243:      * the surrounding page colour — useful as a background layer for PDF
244:      * re-assembly (the text will be overlaid as an invisible OCR layer).
245:      *
246:      * Algorithm:
247:      *   For each pixel that the mask identifies as foreground (black):
248:      *     1. Search outward in a square (top-left to bottom-right) within radius.
249:      *     2. Pick the first background (white in mask) pixel found.
250:      *     3. Copy that pixel's colour to the result.
251:      *   Background pixels are copied unchanged.
252:      *
253:      * This is a crude "nearest neighbour" inpaint — a real implementation would
254:      * use a proper diffusion or patch-match algorithm, but this is sufficient
255:      * for the page-background use case.
256:      *
257:      * Magic numbers:
258:      *   radius — search window size (passed in as parameter; typically 3).
259:      *     3 is used because typical text stroke widths at 300 DPI are 2-4 px.
260:      *     Larger values handle thicker strokes but are slower.
261:      *   0xFFFFFF — bitmask to extract only the RGB channels, discarding alpha.
262:      *     setRGB expects full ARGB, so foreground is 0xFF000000 (black w/ alpha=FF)
263:      *     and background is 0xFFFFFFFF (white w/ alpha=FF).
264:      *   Bit shifts for colour reconstruction:
265:      *     (bestR << 16) | (bestG << 8) | bestB — packs R, G, B into 0x00RRGGBB.
266:      *     Alpha is also needed: | 0xFF000000 for full opacity.
267:      *     (nrgb >> 16) & 0xFF, (nrgb >> 8) & 0xFF, nrgb & 0xFF — extract
268:      *     individual 8-bit colour channels from a 32-bit ARGB int.
269:      */
270:     private BufferedImage inpaintBackground(BufferedImage original, BufferedImage mask, int radius) {
271:         int width = original.getWidth();
272:         int height = original.getHeight();
273:         BufferedImage result = new BufferedImage(width, height, BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_RGB);
274: 
275:         for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {
276:             for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
277:                 int maskRgb = mask.getRGB(x, y);
278:                 boolean isForeground = (maskRgb & 0xFFFFFF) == 0;
279: 
280:                 if (!isForeground) {
281:                     result.setRGB(x, y, original.getRGB(x, y));
282:                 } else {
283:                     // Find nearest background pixel within radius
284:                     int bestR = 0, bestG = 0, bestB = 0;
285:                     int found = 0;
286:                     for (int dy = -radius; dy <= radius && found == 0; dy++) {
287:                         for (int dx = -radius; dx <= radius && found == 0; dx++) {
288:                             int nx = x + dx;
289:                             int ny = y + dy;
290:                             if (nx < 0 || nx >= width || ny < 0 || ny >= height) continue;
291:                             int nmask = mask.getRGB(nx, ny);
292:                             if ((nmask & 0xFFFFFF) != 0) {
293:                                 int nrgb = original.getRGB(nx, ny);
294:                                 bestR = (nrgb >> 16) & 0xFF;
295:                                 bestG = (nrgb >> 8) & 0xFF;
296:                                 bestB = nrgb & 0xFF;
297:                                 found++;
298:                             }
299:                         }
300:                     }
301:                     if (found > 0) {
302:                         int rgb = 0xFF000000 | (bestR << 16) | (bestG << 8) | bestB;
303:                         result.setRGB(x, y, rgb);
304:                     } else {
305:                         result.setRGB(x, y, original.getRGB(x, y));
306:                     }
307:                 }
308:             }
309:         }
310:         return result;
311:     }
312: }

(End of file - total 312 lines)
</content>
```

1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
4: import java.io.File;
5: import java.io.IOException;
6: import java.util.List;
7: 
8: import org.apache.pdfbox.Loader;
9: import org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSName;
10: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;
11: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPage;
12: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPageContentStream;
13: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.PDRectangle;
14: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDType1Font;
15: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.Standard14Fonts;
16: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.CCITTFactory;
17: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.LosslessFactory;
18: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.PDImageXObject;
19: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.state.RenderingMode;
20: 
21: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;
22: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.TextBlock;
23: 
24: /**
25:  * Re-assembles a searchable PDF from per-page inputs:
26:  *   - Source PDF (copied for page dimensions / media boxes)
27:  *   - Cleaned background images
28:  *   - Binary foreground masks (CCITT G4 compressed stencil overlay)
29:  *   - OCR results (invisible text layer)
30:  *
31:  * MRC-like layout (per page):
32:  *   1. Background layer: cleaned page image (Lossless PNG for now).
33:  *   2. Foreground mask: CCITT G4 compressed binary mask, drawn as a
34:  *      PDF ImageMask stencil in black — reveals only the text pixels
35:  *      on top of the background.
36:  *   3. Text layer: invisible OCR text (RenderingMode.NEITHER = Tr 3),
37:  *      selectable and searchable but not visible.
38:  *
39:  * Rendering mode NEITHER makes text invisible on screen/print while
40:  * keeping it selectable and searchable.
41:  *
42:  * Coordinate transformation:
43:  *   Page images at 300 DPI → PDF user space at 72 DPI.
44:  *   scaleX = pageWidth_pts  / imageWidth_px
45:  *   scaleY = pageHeight_pts / imageHeight_px
46:  *   Y is flipped: image origin is top-left, PDF origin is bottom-left.
47:  */
48: public class PDFAssembler {
49: 
50:     private static final PDType1Font FONT = new PDType1Font(Standard14Fonts.FontName.HELVETICA);
51: 
52:     /**
53:      * Builds a searchable PDF from background images and OCR results.
54:      *
55:      * @param sourcePdf    Original PDF (for per-page media boxes).
56:      * @param backgrounds  Per-page cleaned background images.
57:      * @param ocrResults   Per-page OCR results.
58:      * @return A new PDDocument with searchable text overlaid on background images.
59:      */
60:     private final MetadataPreserver preserver = new MetadataPreserver();
61: 
62:     public PDDocument assemble(File sourcePdf,
63:                                List<BufferedImage> backgrounds,
64:                                List<PageResult> ocrResults) throws IOException {
65:         return assemble(sourcePdf, backgrounds, null, ocrResults);
66:     }
67: 
68:     /**
69:      * Builds a searchable PDF with MRC-like foreground mask overlay.
70:      *
71:      * When foregroundMasks is provided, each mask is CCITT G4 compressed
72:      * and drawn as a PDF ImageMask stencil in black on top of the background.
73:      * This reproduces the original text pixels at full sharpness, while
74:      * the background layer carries the cleaned (de-speckled) page image.
75:      *
76:      * @param sourcePdf       Original PDF (for per-page media boxes).
77:      * @param backgrounds     Per-page cleaned background images.
78:      * @param foregroundMasks Per-page binary foreground masks (TYPE_BYTE_BINARY,
79:      *                        black=text, white=background), or null to skip.
80:      * @param ocrResults      Per-page OCR results.
81:      * @return A new PDDocument with foreground mask overlay and searchable text.
82:      */
83:     public PDDocument assemble(File sourcePdf,
84:                                List<BufferedImage> backgrounds,
85:                                List<BufferedImage> foregroundMasks,
86:                                List<PageResult> ocrResults) throws IOException {
87:         PDDocument output = new PDDocument();
88:         List<PDPage> outPages = new java.util.ArrayList<>();
89: 
90:         try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(sourcePdf)) {
91:             int pageCount = source.getNumberOfPages();
92:             for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {
93:                 PDPage sourcePage = source.getPage(i);
94:                 PDRectangle mediaBox = sourcePage.getMediaBox();
95: 
96:                 BufferedImage bgImage = backgrounds.get(i);
97:                 PageResult ocr = ocrResults.get(i);
98: 
99:                 PDPage outPage = new PDPage(mediaBox);
100:                 output.addPage(outPage);
101:                 outPages.add(outPage);
102: 
103:                 try (PDPageContentStream cs = new PDPageContentStream(output, outPage)) {
104:                     float pageW = mediaBox.getWidth();
105:                     float pageH = mediaBox.getHeight();
106: 
107:                     // Layer 1: background image
108:                     PDImageXObject bgXObject = LosslessFactory.createFromImage(output, bgImage);
109:                     cs.drawImage(bgXObject, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);
110: 
111:                     // Layer 2: foreground mask as CCITT G4 stencil overlay
112:                     if (foregroundMasks != null && i < foregroundMasks.size()) {
113:                         BufferedImage fgMask = foregroundMasks.get(i);
114:                         BufferedImage inverted = invertBinaryImage(fgMask);
115:                         PDImageXObject fgCCITT = CCITTFactory.createFromImage(output, inverted);
116:                         fgCCITT.getCOSObject().setBoolean(COSName.IMAGE_MASK, true);
117:                         fgCCITT.getCOSObject().removeItem(COSName.COLORSPACE);
118:                         cs.setNonStrokingColor(0f, 0f, 0f);
119:                         cs.drawImage(fgCCITT, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);
120:                     }
121: 
122:                     // Layer 3: invisible OCR text
123:                     float scaleX = pageW / ocr.getWidth();
124:                     float scaleY = pageH / ocr.getHeight();
125:                     cs.beginText();
126:                     cs.setFont(FONT, 1);
127:                     cs.setRenderingMode(RenderingMode.NEITHER);
128: 
129:                     for (TextBlock tb : ocr.getTextBlocks()) {
130:                         float x = tb.getBbox().x * scaleX;
131:                         float y = pageH - (tb.getBbox().y + tb.getBbox().height) * scaleY;
132:                         float fontSize = Math.max(tb.getBbox().height * scaleY, 1);
133:                         cs.setFont(FONT, fontSize);
134:                         cs.newLineAtOffset(x, y);
135:                         cs.showText(tb.getWord());
136:                         cs.newLineAtOffset(-x, -y);
137:                     }
138:                     cs.endText();
139:                 }
140:             }
141: 
142:             // Copy metadata from source to output
143:             preserver.preserve(source, output, outPages);
144:         }
145: 
146:         return output;
147:     }
148: 
149:     private static BufferedImage invertBinaryImage(BufferedImage mask) {
150:         int w = mask.getWidth();
151:         int h = mask.getHeight();
152:         BufferedImage inverted = new BufferedImage(w, h, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);
153:         for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
154:             for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
155:                 int rgb = mask.getRGB(x, y);
156:                 inverted.setRGB(x, y, (rgb & 0xFFFFFF) == 0 ? 0xFFFFFFFF : 0xFF000000);
157:             }
158:         }
159:         return inverted;
160:     }
161: 
162: }

(End of file - total 162 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>

1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
4: import java.io.File;
5: import java.io.IOException;
6: import java.util.ArrayList;
7: import java.util.List;
8: 
9: import org.apache.pdfbox.Loader;
10: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;
11: import org.apache.pdfbox.rendering.PDFRenderer;
12: 
13: /**
14:  * Renders each page of a PDF to a BufferedImage at 300 DPI
15:  * using PDFBox's PDFRenderer.
16:  *
17:  * 300 DPI is the standard scanning resolution for OCR:
18:  *   - US Letter (8.5"x11")  → 2550 × 3300 px
19:  *   - A4 (210×297 mm)        → 2480 × 3508 px
20:  *
21:  * The renderer uses PDFBox's built-in SFF (subpixel font finalizer)
22:  * for high-quality text rendering.
23:  *
24:  * Implements AutoCloseable so the underlying PDDocument is released.
25:  * Callers should use try-with-resources.
26:  */
27: public class PageExtractor implements AutoCloseable {
28: 
29:     private PDDocument document;
30: 
31:     /**
32:      * Loads a PDF and renders every page.
33:      *
34:      * @param pdf  Input PDF file.
35:      * @return List of BufferedImages, one per page, at 300 DPI.
36:      */
37:     public List<BufferedImage> extractPages(File pdf) throws IOException {
38:         document = Loader.loadPDF(pdf);
39:         PDFRenderer renderer = new PDFRenderer(document);
40:         int pageCount = document.getNumberOfPages();
41:         List<BufferedImage> pages = new ArrayList<>(pageCount);
42:         for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {
43:             BufferedImage img = renderer.renderImageWithDPI(i, 300);
44:             pages.add(img);
45:         }
46:         return pages;
47:     }
48: 
49:     @Override
50:     public void close() throws IOException {
51:         if (document != null) {
52:             document.close();
53:         }
54:     }
55: }

(End of file - total 55 lines)
</content>
