1: package com.trulyfreeocr.util;
2: 
3: /**
4:  * Cross-platform detection utilities.
5:  *
6:  * Provides consistent enum values for OS and CPU architecture,
7:  * and resolves the project JDK location via the TFOCR_JAVA_HOME
8:  * environment variable (falling back to the running JVM's java.home).
9:  *
10:  * OS detection is based on the os.name system property:
11:  *   "win" in any case   → Os.WINDOWS
12:  *   "mac" in any case   → Os.MAC
13:  *   anything else       → Os.LINUX
14:  *
15:  * Architecture detection is based on os.arch:
16:  *   "aarch64" or "arm64" → Arch.AARCH64
17:  *   anything else         → Arch.X64
18:  */
19: public class PlatformUtils {
20: 
21:     public enum Os { WINDOWS, LINUX, MAC }
22: 
23:     public enum Arch { X64, AARCH64 }
24: 
25:     public static Os detectOs() {
26:         String raw = System.getProperty("os.name").toLowerCase();
27:         if (raw.contains("win")) return Os.WINDOWS;
28:         if (raw.contains("mac")) return Os.MAC;
29:         return Os.LINUX;
30:     }
31: 
32:     public static Arch detectArch() {
33:         String raw = System.getProperty("os.arch").toLowerCase();
34:         if (raw.contains("aarch64") || raw.contains("arm64")) return Arch.AARCH64;
35:         return Arch.X64;
36:     }
37: 
38:     /**
39:      * Returns the project-specific JDK home.
40:      *
41:      * Checks TFOCR_JAVA_HOME first (set by run.sh/bootstrap.sh),
42:      * then falls back to the JVM that is running this code.
43:      */
44:     public static String getProjectJavaHome() {
45:         String env = System.getenv("TFOCR_JAVA_HOME");
46:         if (env != null && !env.isEmpty()) return env;
47:         return System.getProperty("java.home");
48:     }
49: }

1: package com.trulyfreeocr.util;
2: 
3: import java.io.BufferedReader;
4: import java.io.File;
5: import java.io.IOException;
6: import java.io.InputStreamReader;
7: import java.util.ArrayList;
8: import java.util.List;
9: import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;
10: 
11: /**
12:  * Utility for running external processes and capturing stdout/stderr.
13:  *
14:  * Two convenience modes:
15:  *   run(executable, args...)      — blocks until completion (no timeout).
16:  *   run(executable, args, ms)     — kills the process after <ms> milliseconds.
17:  *
18:  * Stderr is NOT merged with stdout (redirectErrorStream = false) so
19:  * callers can distinguish normal output from error diagnostics.
20:  */
21: public class SubprocessRunner {
22: 
23:     /**
24:      * Executes a command with no timeout.
25:      *
26:      * @param executable  The binary to run.
27:      * @param args        Variable-length argument list.
28:      * @return Result containing exit code, stdout, and stderr.
29:      */
30:     public static Result run(File executable, String... args) throws IOException {
31:         return run(executable, List.of(args), -1);
32:     }
33: 
34:     /**
35:      * Executes a command with an optional timeout.
36:      *
37:      * @param executable  The binary to run.
38:      * @param args        Argument list.
39:      * @param timeoutMs   Max wall-clock time in milliseconds (&le;0 = no timeout).
40:      * @return Result containing exit code, stdout, and stderr.
41:      */
42:     public static Result run(File executable, List<String> args, long timeoutMs) throws IOException {
43:         List<String> command = new ArrayList<>();
44:         command.add(executable.getAbsolutePath());
45:         command.addAll(args);
46: 
47:         ProcessBuilder pb = new ProcessBuilder(command);
48:         pb.redirectErrorStream(false);
49: 
50:         Process process = pb.start();
51: 
52:         if (timeoutMs > 0) {
53:             try {
54:                 boolean finished = process.waitFor(timeoutMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);
55:                 if (!finished) {
56:                     process.destroyForcibly();
57:                     throw new IOException("Process timed out after " + timeoutMs + "ms: " + executable.getName());
58:                 }
59:             } catch (InterruptedException e) {
60:                 process.destroyForcibly();
61:                 Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
62:                 throw new IOException("Process was interrupted", e);
63:             }
64:         } else {
65:             try {
66:                 process.waitFor();
67:             } catch (InterruptedException e) {
68:                 process.destroyForcibly();
69:                 Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
70:                 throw new IOException("Process was interrupted", e);
71:             }
72:         }
73: 
74:         byte[] stdout = process.getInputStream().readAllBytes();
75:         byte[] stderr = process.getErrorStream().readAllBytes();
76:         int exitCode = process.exitValue();
77: 
78:         return new Result(exitCode, stdout, stderr);
79:     }
80: 
81:     /**
82:      * Holds the subprocess output.
83:      *
84:      * exitCode  Process exit status (0 = success).
85:      * stdout    Raw bytes written to standard output.
86:      * stderr    Raw bytes written to standard error.
87:      */
88:     public static class Result {
89:         private final int exitCode;
90:         private final byte[] stdout;
91:         private final byte[] stderr;
92: 
93:         public Result(int exitCode, byte[] stdout, byte[] stderr) {
94:             this.exitCode = exitCode;
95:             this.stdout = stdout;
96:             this.stderr = stderr;
97:         }
98: 
99:         public int getExitCode() { return exitCode; }
100:         public byte[] getStdout() { return stdout; }
101:         public byte[] getStderr() { return stderr; }
102:         public String getStdoutString() { return new String(stdout); }
103:         public String getStderrString() { return new String(stderr); }
104:     }
105: }

(End of file - total 105 lines)
</content>

1: package com.trulyfreeocr.model;
2: 
3: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
4: 
5: /**
6:  * Output of the ImageSegmenter pipeline.
7:  *
8:  * foregroundMask    Binary image (TYPE_BYTE_BINARY) where black pixels
9:  *                   represent text/foreground and white is background.
10:  * cleanedBackground The original page image with text regions inpainted
11:  *                   (filled in with surrounding colour), suitable as a
12:  *                   JPEG background layer in the output PDF.
13:  */
14: public class SegmentedImage {
15: 
16:     private final BufferedImage foregroundMask;
17:     private final BufferedImage cleanedBackground;
18: 
19:     public SegmentedImage(BufferedImage foregroundMask, BufferedImage cleanedBackground) {
20:         this.foregroundMask = foregroundMask;

```
<path>/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/MetadataPreserver.java</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.io.IOException;
4: import java.util.List;
5: 
6: import org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSArray;
7: import org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSDictionary;
8: import org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSName;
9: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;
10: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocumentInformation;
11: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPage;
12: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.PDMetadata;
13: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.documentinterchange.logicalstructure.PDMarkInfo;
14: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.annotation.PDAnnotation;
15: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.documentnavigation.outline.PDDocumentOutline;
16: 
17: /**
18:  * Copies metadata from a source PDF to an output PDF.
19:  *
20:  * Preserved elements:
21:  *   - PDDocumentInformation (title, author, subject, keywords, creator, producer)
22:  *   - PDDocumentOutline (bookmark tree) — COS-level deep copy
23:  *   - Per-page PDAnnotation lists — each annotation COS dictionary is duplicated
24:  *   - XML metadata stream (XMP)
25:  *
26:  * Limitations:
27:  *   - Annotation page references (e.g. link destinations) are not remapped.
28:  *   - Named destinations are preserved but may reference missing pages.
29:  *   - Outline entries pointing to specific pages are kept but not remapped
30:  *     (the output pages are in the same order, so page-number-based links
31:  *     generally still work).
32:  */
33: public class MetadataPreserver {
34: 
35:     /**
36:      * Copies all metadata from source to output.
37:      *
38:      * @param source      The original PDF.
39:      * @param output      The newly assembled PDF.
40:      * @param outputPages Output pages indexed to match source pages.
41:      */
42:     public void preserve(PDDocument source, PDDocument output, List<PDPage> outputPages) throws IOException {
43:         copyDocumentInfo(source, output);
44:         copyOutline(source, output);
45:         copyAnnotations(source, outputPages);
46:         copyXmlMetadata(source, output);
47:     }
48: 
49:     private void copyDocumentInfo(PDDocument source, PDDocument output) {
50:         PDDocumentInformation info = source.getDocumentInformation();
51:         if (info != null) {
52:             PDDocumentInformation dest = new PDDocumentInformation();
53:             copyIfSet(info::getTitle, dest::setTitle);
54:             copyIfSet(info::getAuthor, dest::setAuthor);
55:             copyIfSet(info::getSubject, dest::setSubject);
56:             copyIfSet(info::getKeywords, dest::setKeywords);
57:             copyIfSet(info::getCreator, dest::setCreator);
58:             copyIfSet(info::getProducer, dest::setProducer);
59:             output.setDocumentInformation(dest);
60:         }
61:     }
62: 
63:     private void copyIfSet(ThrowingSupplier<String> getter, ThrowingConsumer<String> setter) {
64:         try {
65:             String val = getter.get();
66:             if (val != null) setter.accept(val);
67:         } catch (Exception e) {
68:             // skip
69:         }
70:     }
71: 
72:     @FunctionalInterface
73:     private interface ThrowingSupplier<T> {
74:         T get() throws Exception;
75:     }
76: 
77:     @FunctionalInterface
78:     private interface ThrowingConsumer<T> {
79:         void accept(T t) throws Exception;
80:     }
81: 
82:     private void copyOutline(PDDocument source, PDDocument output) throws IOException {
83:         PDDocumentOutline srcOutline = source.getDocumentCatalog().getDocumentOutline();
84:         if (srcOutline == null) return;
85: 
86:         // Deep-copy the outline COS dictionary tree
87:         COSDictionary srcDict = srcOutline.getCOSObject();
88:         COSDictionary dstDict = deepCopyCOSDictionary(srcDict);
89:         PDDocumentOutline dstOutline = new PDDocumentOutline(dstDict);
90:         output.getDocumentCatalog().setDocumentOutline(dstOutline);
91:     }
92: 
93:     private void copyAnnotations(PDDocument source, List<PDPage> outputPages) throws IOException {
94:         int pageCount = Math.min(source.getNumberOfPages(), outputPages.size());
95:         for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {
96:             PDPage srcPage = source.getPage(i);
97:             PDPage dstPage = outputPages.get(i);
98: 
99:             List<PDAnnotation> annotations = srcPage.getAnnotations();
100:             if (annotations.isEmpty()) continue;
101: 
102:             COSArray dstAnnots = new COSArray();
103:             for (PDAnnotation ann : annotations) {
104:                 COSDictionary cloned = deepCopyCOSDictionary(ann.getCOSObject());
105:                 dstAnnots.add(cloned);
106:             }
107:             dstPage.getCOSObject().setItem(COSName.ANNOTS, dstAnnots);
108:         }
109:     }
110: 
111:     private void copyXmlMetadata(PDDocument source, PDDocument output) throws IOException {
112:         PDMetadata srcMeta = source.getDocumentCatalog().getMetadata();
113:         if (srcMeta == null) return;
114: 
115:         try (var in = srcMeta.createInputStream()) {
116:             byte[] data = in.readAllBytes();
117:             PDMetadata dstMeta = new PDMetadata(output);
118:             dstMeta.importXMPMetadata(data);
119:             output.getDocumentCatalog().setMetadata(dstMeta);
120:         } catch (Exception e) {
121:             // Non-critical — skip XML metadata copy on failure
122:         }
123:     }
124: 
125:     private static COSDictionary deepCopyCOSDictionary(COSDictionary original) {
126:         COSDictionary copy = new COSDictionary();
127:         for (var entry : original.entrySet()) {
128:             COSName key = entry.getKey();
129:             var value = entry.getValue();
130:             if (value instanceof COSDictionary dict) {
131:                 copy.setItem(key, deepCopyCOSDictionary(dict));
132:             } else if (value instanceof COSArray arr) {
133:                 copy.setItem(key, deepCopyCOSArray(arr));
134:             } else {
135:                 copy.setItem(key, value);
136:             }
137:         }
138:         return copy;
139:     }
140: 
141:     private static COSArray deepCopyCOSArray(COSArray original) {
142:         COSArray copy = new COSArray();
143:         for (var value : original) {
144:             if (value instanceof COSDictionary dict) {
145:                 copy.add(deepCopyCOSDictionary(dict));
146:             } else if (value instanceof COSArray arr) {
147:                 copy.add(deepCopyCOSArray(arr));
148:             } else {
149:                 copy.add(value);
150:             }
151:         }
152:         return copy;
153:     }
154: }

(End of file - total 154 lines)
</content>
```

    // Paths
    "native.dir": "./native",

    // Tesseract options
    "tesseract.language": "eng",
    "tesseract.psm": 1,
    
    // PDF rendering

    // Image segmentation
    "segmenter.tileSize": 64,
    "segmenter.percentile": 0.95,
    "segmenter.inpaintRadius": 3,
    
    // JBIG2 compression

    // PDF assembly

    "pdf.minFontSize": 1,
    
    // Output

}
```

**settings.jsonc** content:

```jsonc
{
    // ===== Paths =====
    // Directory containing native binaries (jbig2enc).
    // Can be overridden via --native-dir CLI flag.
    "native.dir": "./native",

    // Directory containing Tesseract language data (*.traineddata).
    // Can be overridden via --tessdata-dir CLI flag or TESSDATA_PREFIX env var.
    "tessdata.dir": "./tessdata",

    // Path to the Tesseract executable.
    "tesseract.path": "tesseract",

    // ===== Tesseract Options =====
    // Language model(s) for OCR. Multiple languages: "eng+fra"
    "tesseract.language": "eng",

    // Page segmentation mode:
    //   0 = OSD only   1 = Auto + OSD   3 = Auto (no OSD)
    //   6 = Single block   7 = Single line   11 = Sparse text
    //   13 = Raw line
    "tesseract.psm": 1,

    // ===== PDF Rendering =====
    // DPI for rendering PDF pages to images before OCR.
    //   300 = standard OCR quality
    //   200 = faster, slightly lower accuracy
    //   400+ = higher accuracy, slower
    "rendering.dpi": 300,

    // ===== Image Segmentation =====
    // Tile size in pixels for background normalization grid.
    // Smaller = more detail but slower; larger = faster but coarser.
    "segmenter.tileSize": 64,

    // Percentile used to estimate background level per tile (0.0 - 1.0).
    // 0.95 means the 95th percentile of brightness is treated as background.
    // Increase for pages with very little text; decrease for dense text.
    "segmenter.percentile": 0.95,

    // Search radius in pixels for inpainting text regions.
    // Should be ~1.5× the thickest text stroke width.
    "segmenter.inpaintRadius": 3,

    // ===== JBIG2 Compression =====
    // Flags passed to jbig2enc binary.
    //   -p = global symbol dictionary (best compression for multi-page)
    //   -s = refine smearing
    "jbig2enc.flags": "-p -s",

    // ===== PDF Assembly =====
    // Font for invisible OCR text layer.
    // Must be a PDF Standard 14 font (no embedding needed):
    //   HELVETICA, TIMES_ROMAN, COURIER, etc.
    "pdf.font": "HELVETICA",

    // Minimum font size in points for OCR text.
    // Prevents zero-size fonts from causing PDF errors.
    "pdf.minFontSize": 1,

    // ===== Output =====
    // Default output PDF file path.
    // Can be overridden via -o / --output CLI flag.
    "output.file": "output.pdf"
}
```

Now for Settings.java, I need to implement:
1. Reading the file
2. Stripping comments
3. Parsing JSON key-value pairs
4. Providing typed getters

Multiple: \"eng+fra\"\n    \"tesseract.language\": \"eng\",\n\n    // Page segmentation mode:\n    //   0 = OSD only      1 = Auto + OSD     3 = Auto (no OSD)\n    //   6 = Single block  7 = Single line   11 = Sparse text\n    //  13 = Raw line\n    \"tesseract.psm\": 1,\n\n    // ===== PDF Rendering =====\n    // DPI for rendering PDF pages to images before OCR.\n    //   300 = standard OCR quality\n    //   200 = faster, lower accuracy\n    \"rendering.dpi\": 300,\n\n    // ===== Image Segmentation =====\n    // Tile size (px) for background normalization grid.\n    // Smaller = more detail; larger = faster.\n    \"segmenter.tileSize\": 64,\n\n    // Background-level percentile per tile (0.0 - 1.0).\n    // 0.95 = 95th percentile.  Increase for sparse text.\n    \"segmenter.percentile\": 0.95,\n\n    // Inpainting search radius (px) for filling text regions.\n    // Should be ~1.5x the thickest text stroke.\n    \"segmenter.inpaintRadius\": 3,\n\n    // ===== JBIG2 Compression =====\n    // Flags passed to the jbig2enc binary.\n    //   -p = global symbol dictionary\n    //   -s = refine smearing\n    \"jbig2enc.flags\": \"-p -s\",\n\n    // ===== PDF Assembly =====\n    // Standard 14 font for the invisible OCR text layer.\n    //   HELVETICA, TIMES_ROMAN, COURIER, etc.\n    \"pdf.font\": \"HELVETICA\",\n\n    // Minimum font size (pt) to prevent zero-size fonts.\n    \"pdf.minFontSize\": 1,\n\n    // ===== Output =====\n    // Default output PDF path.\n    // Override via -o / --output CLI flag.\n    \"output.file\": \"output.pdf\"\n}\n"
}

{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/util/Settings.java",
  "content": "package com.trulyfreeocr.util;\n\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.nio.file.Files;\nimport java.nio.file.Path;\nimport java.nio.file.Paths;\nimport java.util.HashMap;\nimport java.util.Map;\n\npublic class Settings {\n\n    private static Settings instance;\n\n    private final Map<String, String> values;\n\n    private static final Path DEFAULT_PATH = Paths.get(\"settings.jsonc\");\n\n    // System property key for overriding the settings file location.\n    private static final String PROP_KEY = \"tfocr.settings\";\n\n    public Settings() {\n        this.values = new HashMap<>();\n    }\n\n    public Settings(Map<String, String> values) {\n        this.values = new HashMap<>(values);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Loads settings from the default location (settings.jsonc in CWD),\n     * or from the path specified by the \"tfocr.settings\" system property.\n     * Does nothing if the file does not exist — all getters fall back to\n     * their supplied defaults.\n     */\n    public static Settings load() {\n        Settings s = new Settings();\n        Path path = findPath();\n        if (path != null && Files.exists(path)) {\n            try {\n                String raw = Files.readString(path);\n                s.values.putAll(parseJsonc(raw));\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                // file missing or unreadable — use defaults\n            }\n        }\n        instance = s;\n        return s;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns the singleton instance.  Must be preceded by a call to load().\n     */\n    public static Settings getInstance() {\n        if (instance == null) {\n            instance = new Settings();\n        }\n        return instance;\n    }\n\n    // ---- typed accessors with defaults ----\n\n    public String getString(String key, String defaultValue) {\n        return values.getOrDefault(key, defaultValue);\n    }\n\n    public int getInt(String key, int defaultValue) {\n        String v = values.get(key);\n        if (v == null) return defaultValue;\n        try { return Integer.parseInt(v); } catch (NumberFormatException e) { return defaultValue; }\n    }\n\n    public double getDouble(String key, double defaultValue) {\n        String v = values.get(key);\n        if (v == null) return defaultValue;\n        try { return Double.parseDouble(v); } catch (NumberFormatException e) { return defaultValue; }\n    }\n\n    public boolean getBoolean(String key, boolean defaultValue) {\n        String v = values.get(key);\n        if (v == null) return defaultValue;\n        return \"true\".equalsIgnoreCase(v);\n    }\n\n    // ---- path resolution ----\n\n    private static Path findPath() {\n        String prop = System.getProperty(PROP_KEY);\n        if (prop != null && !prop.isEmpty()) {\n            return Paths.get(prop);\n        }\n        return DEFAULT_PATH;\n    }\n\n    // ---- minimal JSONC parser (flat objects only) ----\n\n    static Map<String, String> parseJsonc(String raw) {\n        String stripped = stripComments(raw);\n        return parseJson(stripped);\n    }\n\n    private static String stripComments(String input) {\n        StringBuilder out = new StringBuilder(input.length());\n        int i = 0;\n        while (i < input.length()) {\n            if (i + 1 < input.length() && input.charAt(i) == '/' && input.charAt(i + 1) == '/') {\n                // line comment — skip to end of line\n                i += 2;\n                while (i < input.length() && input.charAt(i) != '\\n') i++;\n                continue;\n            }\n            if (i + 1 < input.length() && input.charAt(i) == '/' && input.charAt(i + 1) == '*') {\n                // block comment — skip to */\n                i += 2;\n                while (i + 1 < input.length() && !(input.charAt(i) == '*' && input.charAt(i + 1) == '/')) i++;\n                i += 2; // skip */\n                continue;\n            }\n            out.append(input.charAt(i));\n            i++;\n        }\n        return out.toString();\n    }\n\n    private static Map<String, String> parseJson(String input) {\n        Map<String, String> result = new HashMap<>();\n        int i = skipWhitespace(input, 0);\n        if (i >= input.length() || input.charAt(i) != '{') return result;\n        i = skipWhitespace(input, i + 1);\n\n        while (i < input.length()) {\n            if (input.charAt(i) == '}') break;\n            if (input.charAt(i) == ',') { i = skipWhitespace(input, i + 1); continue; }\n\n            // parse key\n            if (input.charAt(i) != '\"') break;\n            int keyStart = i + 1;\n            int keyEnd = findStringEnd(input, keyStart);\n            if (keyEnd < 0) break;\n            String key = input.substring(keyStart, keyEnd);\n            i = skipWhitespace(input, keyEnd + 1);\n\n            if (i >= input.length() || input.charAt(i) != ':') break;\n            i = skipWhitespace(input, i + 1);\n\n            // parse value\n            if (i >= input.length()) break;\n            char c = input.charAt(i);\n            String value;\n            if (c == '\"') {\n                int valStart = i + 1;\n                int valEnd = findStringEnd(input, valStart);\n                if (valEnd < 0) break;\n                value = input.substring(valStart, valEnd);\n                i = skipWhitespace(input, valEnd + 1);\n            } else if (c == 't' && input.startsWith(\"true\", i)) {\n                value = \"true\";\n                i = skipWhitespace(input, i + 4);\n            } else if (c == 'f' && input.startsWith(\"false\", i)) {\n                value = \"false\";\n                i = skipWhitespace(input, i + 5);\n            } else if (c == 'n' && input.startsWith(\"null\", i)) {\n                value = \"null\";\n                i = skipWhitespace(input, i + 4);\n            } else {\n                // number\n                int valEnd = i;\n                while (valEnd < input.length() && (Character.isDigit(input.charAt(valEnd))\n                        || input.charAt(valEnd) == '.' || input.charAt(valEnd) == '-'\n                        || input.charAt(valEnd) == '+' || input.charAt(valEnd) == 'e'\n                        || input.charAt(valEnd) == 'E')) {\n                    valEnd++;\n                }\n                if (valEnd == i) break;\n                value = input.substring(i, valEnd);\n                i = skipWhitespace(input, valEnd);\n            }\n            result.put(key, value);\n        }\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    private static int skipWhitespace(String s, int pos) {\n        while (pos < s.length() && Character.isWhitespace(s.charAt(pos))) pos++;\n        return pos;\n    }\n\n    private static int findStringEnd(String s, int start) {\n        int i = start;\n        while (i < s.length()) {\n            char c = s.charAt(i);\n            if (c == '\\\\') {\n                i += 2; // skip escaped char\n            } else if (c == '\"') {\n                return i;\n            } else {\n                i++;\n            }\n        }\n        return -1;\n    }\n}\n"
}

  "oldString": "public class PageExtractor implements AutoCloseable {\n\n    private PDDocument document;\n\n    /**\n     * Loads a PDF and renders every page.\n     *\n     * @param pdf  Input PDF file.\n     * @return List of BufferedImages, one per page, at 300 DPI.\n     */\n    public List<BufferedImage> extractPages(File pdf) throws IOException {\n        document = Loader.loadPDF(pdf);\n        PDFRenderer renderer = new PDFRenderer(document);\n        int pageCount = document.getNumberOfPages();\n        List<BufferedImage> pages = new ArrayList<>(pageCount);\n        for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {\n            BufferedImage img = renderer.renderImageWithDPI(i, 300);\n            pages.add(img);\n        }\n        return pages;\n    }",
  "newString": "public class PageExtractor implements AutoCloseable {\n\n    private PDDocument document;\n    private final float dpi;\n\n    public PageExtractor() {\n        this(300f);\n    }\n\n    public PageExtractor(float dpi) {\n        this.dpi = dpi;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Loads a PDF and renders every page.\n     *\n     * @param pdf  Input PDF file.\n     * @return List of BufferedImages, one per page, at the configured DPI.\n     */\n    public List<BufferedImage> extractPages(File pdf) throws IOException {\n        document = Loader.loadPDF(pdf);\n        PDFRenderer renderer = new PDFRenderer(document);\n        int pageCount = document.getNumberOfPages();\n        List<BufferedImage> pages = new ArrayList<>(pageCount);\n        for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {\n            BufferedImage img = renderer.renderImageWithDPI(i, dpi);\n            pages.add(img);\n        }\n        return pages;\n    }"
}

  "oldString": "public class ImageSegmenter {\n\n    /**\n     * Orchestrates the full page-segmentation pipeline:\n     *   grayscale -> background normalization -> Otsu binarization -> inpaint background.\n     *\n     * @param image  Input RGB page image (e.g. 300 DPI PDFBox render).\n     * @return       SegmentedImage holding a binary foreground mask and a cleaned background.\n     */\n    public SegmentedImage segment(BufferedImage image) {\n        int width = image.getWidth();\n        int height = image.getHeight();\n\n        // Step 1: Convert to grayscale\n        int[] gray = toGrayscale(image);\n\n        // Step 2: Background normalization\n        // tileSize=64: 64x64 pixel tiles; 2^6 chosen for fast bit ops during interpolation.\n        int tileSize = 64;\n        double[] bgNormalized = backgroundNormalize(gray, width, height, tileSize);\n\n        // Step 3: Otsu binarization\n        int threshold = otsuThreshold(bgNormalized);\n        BufferedImage foregroundMask = new BufferedImage(width, height, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);\n        for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {\n                int idx = y * width + x;\n                // Foreground = 0 (black), Background = 1 (white) in binary image\n                // Text is dark on light bg, so pixel <= threshold = foreground\n                if (gray[idx] <= threshold) {\n                    foregroundMask.setRGB(x, y, 0xFF000000); // ARGB: black, full opacity\n                } else {\n                    foregroundMask.setRGB(x, y, 0xFFFFFFFF); // ARGB: white, full opacity\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Step 4: Cleaned background — fill text regions with surrounding color\n        // radius=3: search up to 3 px away for a background pixel to sample from.\n        BufferedImage cleanedBackground = inpaintBackground(image, foregroundMask, 3);\n\n        return new SegmentedImage(foregroundMask, cleanedBackground);\n    }",
  "newString": "public class ImageSegmenter {\n\n    private final int tileSize;\n    private final double percentile;\n    private final int inpaintRadius;\n\n    public ImageSegmenter() {\n        this(64, 0.95, 3);\n    }\n\n    public ImageSegmenter(int tileSize, double percentile, int inpaintRadius) {\n        this.tileSize = tileSize;\n        this.percentile = percentile;\n        this.inpaintRadius = inpaintRadius;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Orchestrates the full page-segmentation pipeline:\n     *   grayscale -> background normalization -> Otsu binarization -> inpaint background.\n     *\n     * @param image  Input RGB page image (e.g. 300 DPI PDFBox render).\n     * @return       SegmentedImage holding a binary foreground mask and a cleaned background.\n     */\n    public SegmentedImage segment(BufferedImage image) {\n        int width = image.getWidth();\n        int height = image.getHeight();\n\n        // Step 1: Convert to grayscale\n        int[] gray = toGrayscale(image);\n\n        // Step 2: Background normalization\n        double[] bgNormalized = backgroundNormalize(gray, width, height, tileSize);\n\n        // Step 3: Otsu binarization\n        int threshold = otsuThreshold(bgNormalized);\n        BufferedImage foregroundMask = new BufferedImage(width, height, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);\n        for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {\n                int idx = y * width + x;\n                // Foreground = 0 (black), Background = 1 (white) in binary image\n                // Text is dark on light bg, so pixel <= threshold = foreground\n                if (gray[idx] <= threshold) {\n                    foregroundMask.setRGB(x, y, 0xFF000000); // ARGB: black, full opacity\n                } else {\n                    foregroundMask.setRGB(x, y, 0xFFFFFFFF); // ARGB: white, full opacity\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Step 4: Cleaned background — fill text regions with surrounding color\n        BufferedImage cleanedBackground = inpaintBackground(image, foregroundMask, inpaintRadius);\n\n        return new SegmentedImage(foregroundMask, cleanedBackground);\n    }"
}

   public PageResult ocr(BufferedImage image) throws IOException {\n        int width = image.getWidth();\n        int height = image.getHeight();\n\n        // Write image to temp file\n        File tempPng = File.createTempFile(\"tfocr-\", \".png\");\n        tempPng.deleteOnExit();\n        ImageIO.write(image, \"png\", tempPng);\n\n        try {\n            // Run tesseract with TSV output for word-level bounding boxes\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n            ProcessBuilder pb = new ProcessBuilder(\n                    \"tesseract\",\n                    tempPng.getAbsolutePath(),\n                    prefix,\n                    \"-l\", \"eng\",\n                    \"--psm\", \"1\",\n                    \"tsv\"\n            );\n            pb.environment().put(\"TESSDATA_PREFIX\", tessdataDir.getAbsolutePath());\n            pb.redirectErrorStream(true);\n\n            Process process = pb.start();\n            try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(\n                    new InputStreamReader(process.getInputStream()))) {\n                // Read and discard stdout (status messages go here)\n                while (reader.readLine() != null) { }\n            }\n            int exitCode = process.waitFor();\n            if (exitCode != 0) {\n                throw new IOException(\"Tesseract exited with code \" + exitCode);\n            }\n\n            // Parse TSV output\n            File tsvFile = new File(prefix + \".tsv\");\n            if (!tsvFile.exists()) {\n                return new (InterruptedException e) {\n            Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n            throw new IOException(\"Tesseract was interrupted\", e);\n        } finally {\n            // Cleanup temp   "newString": "public class OCREngine {\n\n    private final File tessdataDir;\n    private final String tesseractPath;\n    private final String language;\n    private final String psm;\n\n    public OCREngine() {\n        this(null, \"tesseract\", \"eng\", \"1\");\n    }\n\n    public OCREngine(String tessdataDir, String tesseractPath, String language, String psm) {\n        this.tesseractPath = tesseractPath;\n        this.language = language;\n        this.psm = psm;\n\n        if (tessdataDir != null && !tessdataDir.isEmpty()) {\n            this.tessdataDir = new File(tessdataDir);\n        } else {\n            String env = System.getenv(\"TESSDATA_PREFIX\");\n            if (env != null && !env.isEmpty()) {\n                this.tessdataDir = new File(env);\n            } else {\n                this.tessdataDir = new File(\"/usr/share/tesseract-ocr/5/tessdata\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    public PageResult ocr(BufferedImage image) throws IOException {\n        int width = image.getWidth();\n        int height = image.getHeight();\n\n        // Write image to temp file\n        File tempPng = File.createTempFile(\"tfocr-\", \".png\");\n        tempPng.deleteOnExit();\n        ImageIO.write(image, \"png\", tempPng);\n\n        try {\n            // Run tesseract with TSV output for word-level bounding boxes\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n            ProcessBuilder pb = new ProcessBuilder(\n                    tesseractPath,\n                    tempPng.getAbsolutePath(),\n                    prefix,\n                    \"-l\", language,\n                    \"--psm\", psm,\n                    \"tsv\"\n            );\n            pb.environment().put(\"TESSDATA_PREFIX\", tessdataDir.getAbsolutePath());\n            pb.redirectErrorStream(true);\n\n            Process process = pb.start();\n            try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(\n                    new InputStreamReader(process.getInputStream()))) {\n                // Read and discard stdout (status messages go here)\n                while (reader.readLine() != null) { }\n            }\n            int exitCode = process.waitFor();\n            if (exitCode != 0) {\n                throw new IOException(\"Tesseract exited with code \" + exitCode);\n            }\n\n            // Parse TSV output\n            File tsvFile = new File(prefix + \".tsv\");\n            if (!tsvFile.exists()) {\n                return new PageResult(1, width, height, List.of());\n            }\n\n            List<TextBlock> blocks = parseTsv(tsvFile);\n            return new PageResult(1, width, height, blocks);\n\n        } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n            Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n            throw new IOException(\"Tesseract was interrupted\", e);\n        } finally {\n            // Cleanup temp files\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n            for (String ext : new String[]{\".png\", \".tsv\"}) {\n                File f = new File(prefix + ext);\n                if (f.exists()) f.delete();\n            }\n        }\n    }"
}

  "newString": "public class PDFAssembler {\n\n    private final PDType1Font font;\n    private final float minFontSize;\n    private final MetadataPreserver preserver = new MetadataPreserver();\n\n    public PDFAssembler() {\n        this(\"HELVETICA\", 1f);\n    }\n\n    public PDFAssembler(String fontName, float minFontSize) {\n        Standard14Fonts.FontName resolved;\n        try {\n            resolved = Standard14Fonts.FontName.valueOf(fontName);\n        } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {\n            resolved = Standard14Fonts.FontName.HELVETICA;\n        }\n        this.font = new PDType1Font(resolved);\n        this.minFontSize = minFontSize;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Builds a searchable PDF from background images and OCR results.\n     *\n     * @param sourcePdf    Original PDF (for per-page media boxes).\n     * @param backgrounds  Per-page cleaned background images.\n     * @param ocrResults   Per-page OCR results.\n     * @return A new PDDocument with searchable text overlaid on background images.\n     */\n    public PDDocument assemble(File sourcePdf,\n                               List<BufferedImage> backgrounds,\n                               List<PageResult> ocrResults) throws IOException {\n        return assemble(sourcePdf, backgrounds, null, ocrResults);\n    }"
}

  "newString": "package com.trulyfreeocr;\n\nimport java.awt.image.BufferedImage;\nimport java.io.File;\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.concurrent.Callable;\n\nimport picocli.CommandLine;\nimport picocli.CommandLine.Command;\nimport picocli.CommandLine.Option;\nimport picocli.CommandLine.Parameters;\n\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.model.SegmentedImage;\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline.ImageSegmenter;\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline.JBIG2Compressor;\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline.MetadataPreserver;\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline.OCREngine;\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline.PDFAssembler;\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline.PageExtractor;\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.util.Settings;\n\n@Command(\n    name = \"trulyfreeocr\",\n    version = \"1.0.0\",\n    description = \"Commercially-safe OCR pipeline for non-searchable PDFs\",\n    mixinStandardHelpOptions = true\n)\npublic class TrulyFreeOCR implements Callable<Integer> {\n\n    @Parameters(index = \"0\", description = \"Input PDF file\")\n    private File inputFile;\n\n    @Option(names = {\"-o\", \"--output\"}, description = \"Output PDF file\")\n    private File outputFile;\n\n    @Option(names = {\"--native-dir\"}, description = \"Directory containing native binaries\")\n    private File nativeDir;\n\n    @Option(names = {\"--tessdata-dir\"}, description = \"Directory containing Tesseract language data\")\n    private File tessdataDir;\n\n    @Option(names = {\"--settings\"}, description = \"Path to settings.jsonc file\")\n    private File settingsFile;\n\n    @Option(names = {\"--dpi\"}, description = \"Rendering DPI for PDF page images\")\n    private Float dpi;\n\n    @Option(names = {\"--language\"}, description = \"Tesseract language model\")\n    private String language;\n\n    @Option(names = {\"--psm\"}, description = \"Tesseract page segmentation mode\")\n    private String psm;\n\n    @Override\n    public Integer call() {\n        Settings settings = Settings.load();\n        if (settingsFile != null) {\n            System.setProperty(\"tfocr.settings\", settingsFile.getAbsolutePath());\n            settings = Settings.load();\n        }\n\n        System.out.println(\"TrulyFreeOCR v1.0.0\");\n        System.out.println(\"  Input:  \" + inputFile);\n\n        try {\n            // Resolve each parameter: CLI arg > settings.jsonc > hardcoded default\n            File resolvedOutput = outputFile != null ? outputFile\n                    : new File(settings.getString(\"output.file\", \"output.pdf\"));\n            String resolvedTessdata = tessdataDir != null ? tessdataDir.getAbsolutePath()\n                    : settings.getString(\"tessdata.dir\", \"./tessdata\");\n            String resolvedNative = nativeDir != null ? nativeDir.getAbsolutePath()\n                    : settings.getString(\"native.dir\", \"native\");\n\n            System.out.println(\"  Output: \" + resolvedOutput);\n\n            // Create pipeline components with configured values\n            float resolvedDpi = dpi != null ? dpi\n                    : (float) settings.getDouble(\"rendering.dpi\", 300);\n            String resolvedLang = language != null ? language\n                    : settings.getString(\"tesseract.language\", \"eng\");\n            String resolvedPsm = psm != null ? psm\n                    : settings.getString(\"tesseract.psm\", \"1\");\n\n            PageExtractor extractor = new PageExtractor(resolvedDpi);\n            ImageSegmenter segmenter = new ImageSegmenter(\n                    settings.getInt(\"segmenter.tileSize\", 64),\n                    settings.getDouble(\"segmenter.percentile\", 0.95),\n                    settings.getInt(\"segmenter.inpaintRadius\", 3)\n            );\n            OCREngine ocrEngine = new OCREngine(\n                    resolvedTessdata,\n                    settings.getString(\"tesseract.path\", \"tesseract\"),\n                    resolvedLang,\n                    resolvedPsm\n            );\n            JBIG2Compressor compressor = new JBIG2Compressor(resolvedNative);\n            PDFAssembler assembler = new PDFAssembler(\n                    settings.getString(\"pdf.font\", \"HELVETICA\"),\n                    (float) settings.getDouble(\"pdf.minFontSize\", 1.0)\n            );\n\n            // Run the pipeline\n            runPipeline(inputFile, resolvedOutput, extractor, segmenter, ocrEngine, compressor, assembler);\n\n            System.out.println(\"Done.\");\n            return 0;\n\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            System.err.println(\"Error: \" + e.getMessage());\n            e.printStackTrace();\n            return 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void runPipeline(File inputFile, File outputFile,\n                             PageExtractor extractor, ImageSegmenter segmenter,\n                             OCREngine ocrEngine, JBIG2Compressor compressor,\n                             PDFAssembler assembler) throws IOException {\n\n        // Phase 1: Extract pages as images\n        System.out.println(\"  Extracting pages...\");\n        List<BufferedImage> pages;\n        try (PageExtractor pe = extractor) {\n            pages = pe.extractPages(inputFile);\n        }\n\n        // Phase 2: Segment each page (background normalization + binarization)\n        System.out.println(\"  Segmenting pages...\");\n        List<BufferedImage> backgrounds = new ArrayList<>(pages.size());\n        List<BufferedImage> foregroundMasks = new ArrayList<>(pages.size());\n        for (BufferedImage page : pages) {\n            SegmentedImage seg = segmenter.segment(page);\n            backgrounds.add(seg.getCleanedBackground());\n            foregroundMasks.add(seg.getForegroundMask());\n        }\n\n        // Phase 3: OCR each page\n        System.out.println(\"  Running OCR...\");\n        List<PageResult> ocrResults = new ArrayList<>(pages.size());\n        for (BufferedImage page : pages) {\n            ocrResults.add(ocrEngine.ocr(page));\n        }\n\n        // Phase 4: Assemble searchable PDF with foreground mask overlay\n        System.out.println(\"  Assembling PDF...\");\n        try (var doc = assembler.assemble(inputFile, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults)) {\n            doc.save(outputFile);\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static void main(String[] args) {\n        int exitCode = new CommandLine(new TrulyFreeOCR()).execute(args);\n        System.exit(exitCode);\n    }\n}"
}

Done. Here's what changed:

**`settings.jsonc`** — new file at project root with 18 commented settings grouped by category (paths, Tesseract options, rendering DPI, segmentation parameters, JBIG2 flags, PDF assembly, output).

**`Settings.java`** — JSONC parser that strips `//` and `/* */` comments, then parses flat key-value pairs. Provides `getString`/`getInt`/`getDouble`/`getBoolean` with fallback defaults. Loads from `settings.jsonc` in CWD or from `tfocr.settings` system property. Singleton access via `Settings.getInstance()`.

**Pipeline component updates** (all keep no-arg constructors for test backward compatibility):
- `PageExtractor(float dpi)` — DPI is now configurable
- `ImageSegmenter(int tileSize, double percentile, int inpaintRadius)` — all three are now configurable
- `OCREngine(String tessdataDir, String tesseractPath, String language, String psm)` — all four configurable
- `JBIG2Compressor(String nativeDir)` — native directory no longer read from system property
- `PDFAssembler(String fontName, float minFontSize)` — font and min size configurable

**`TrulyFreeOCR`** — now loads settings on startup and runs the full pipeline (was stubbed). Resolution order: CLI arg > settings.jsonc > hardcoded default. Also removed unused `MetadataPreserver` import.

**`build.gradle.kts`** — removed Tess4J dependency (OCREngine uses CLI), removed JNA `jvmArgs`, added explicit PDFBox dependency.

```
<path>/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/README.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # TrulyFreeOCR
2: 
3: Commercially-safe OCR pipeline for non-searchable PDFs. All dependencies use permissive open-source licenses (Apache 2.0 / MIT / BSD) — no AGPL, no proprietary components.
4: 
5: **Input**: PDF with non-searchable pages (image-only scans)  
6: **Output**: Searchable, compressed PDF
7: 
8: ---
9: 
10: ## Pipeline
11: 
12: ```
13: Input PDF
14:     |
15:     v
16: PageExtractor  — PDFBox PDFRenderer at 300 DPI → per-page BufferedImage
17:     |
18:     v
19: ImageSegmenter — Pure-Java foreground/background segmentation:
20:                    grayscale → background normalize → Otsu binarize → inpaint
21:     |
22:     +----+----+
23:     |         |
24:     v         v
25: JBIG2Comp.   OCREngine      (parallel)
26:     |         |
27:     | jbig2enc| Tesseract CLI
28:     | subproc | word-level bboxes with TSV output
29:     |         |
30:     +----+----+
31:     |
32:     v
33: PDFAssembler — JPEG background + invisible OCR text (rendering mode 3)
34:     |
35:     v
36: Output PDF (searchable, compressed)
37: ```
38: 
39: ---
40: 
41: ## Implemented Components
42: 
43: ### PageExtractor
44: Renders each PDF page at 300 DPI via PDFBox's `PDFRenderer.renderImageWithDPI()`.
45: 
46: **Algorithm**: Standard PDF rasterisation. US Letter at 300 DPI produces 2550×3300 px images. Uses PDFBox's SFF (subpixel font finalizer) for high-quality text rendering.
47: 
48: ### ImageSegmenter
49: Pure-Java page segmentation (no Leptonica dependency). Four sub-steps:
50: 
51: 1. **Grayscale conversion** — ITU-R BT.601 luma formula: `Y = 0.299R + 0.587G + 0.114B`  
52:    Perception-weighted coefficients matching human visual sensitivity (most sensitive to green, least to blue).
53: 
54: 2. **Background normalization** — Corrects non-uniform illumination (shadows, scanner vignetting).
55:    - Divides the image into 64×64 px tiles
56:    - Estimates background level per tile using the **95th percentile** of pixel intensities (text is dark, so the bright tail of the histogram represents the page surface)
57:    - Builds a smooth background surface via **bilinear interpolation** between tile centres
58:    - Stretches each pixel: `new = old × 255 / bg_estimate` (background pixels map to white, text stays dark)
59: 
60: 3. **Otsu binarization** — Automatically finds the optimal threshold by maximising **inter-class variance**:
61:    `σ² = wB × wF × (μB − μF)²` where `w` = class weight and `μ` = class mean.
62:    Exhaustively searches all 256 grayscale levels for the split with the best separation.
63: 
64: 4. **Inpainting** — Replaces foreground (text) pixels with the nearest background pixel colour within a 3-pixel radius. Produces a cleaned background layer for PDF re-assembly.
65: 
66: ### OCREngine
67: Runs Tesseract as a **subprocess** (not JNA/Tess4J) to avoid native-library version mismatches with system Leptonica.
68: 
69: - Input: BufferedImage → temp PNG
70: - CLI flags: `tesseract input.png output -l eng --psm 1 tsv`
71: - Output: TSV file parsed for **word-level** bounding boxes (`level=5` rows with `left, top, width, height, conf, text`)
72: - Each word returned as a `TextBlock` with bounding box and confidence score
73: 
74: ### JBIG2Compressor
75: Attempts compression via **jbig2enc** subprocess with `-p -s` flags (symbol table + smearing refinement).
76: 
77: - jbig2enc outputs a `.sym` file (global symbol dictionary) and a `.jbig2` file (page data), concatenated for PDF JBIG2Decode streams
78: - Falls back to PNG byte-array output when jbig2enc is not available
79: 
80: ### PDFAssembler
81: Re-assembles the output PDF using PDFBox:
82: 
83: - **Background layer**: Cleaned background image embedded via `LosslessFactory.createFromImage()`
84: - **Text layer**: OCR words at their bounding-box positions, rendered in **mode 3 (NEITHER)** — invisible on screen/print, but selectable and searchable
85: - **Coordinate transform**: `scaleX = pageWidth_pts / imageWidth_px`, Y-flip because image origin is top-left and PDF origin is bottom-left
86: - Uses Helvetica (Standard 14 Font) — no embedding needed
87: 
88: ---
89: 
90: ## Building
91: 
92: ```bash
93: # Build the fat JAR
94: ./gradlew build
95: 
96: # Run all tests
97: ./gradlew test
98: ```
99: 
100: Requires **Java 21+** (JDK) and **Tesseract OCR** CLI (`tesseract` on PATH) with English language data installed.
101: 
102: ## Testing
103: 
104: Tests use generated sample PDFs in `tests/test-files/`:
105: - `blank.pdf` — single blank US Letter page
106: - `simple-text.pdf` — one page with lorem ipsum text
107: - `multi-page.pdf` — three-page document with distinct content per page
108: - `two-column.pdf` — two-column layout
109: - `with-annotations.pdf` — bookmarks, links, and comments
110: - `noisy-scan.pdf` — simulated scanner noise and skew
111: 
112: Regenerate test PDFs with:
113: ```bash
114: python tests/generate_test_pdfs.py
115: ```
116: 
117: ## Current Status
118: 
119: | Phase | Component | Status |
120: |-------|-----------|--------|
121: | 1 | Gradle scaffold + CLI | Done |
122: | 2 | PageExtractor | Done |
123: | 3 | OCREngine | Done |
124: | 4 | ImageSegmenter | Done |
125: | 5 | PDFAssembler (basic) | Done |
126: | 6 | JBIG2Compressor + SubprocessRunner | Done |
127: | 7 | PDFAssembler JBIG2 integration | Pending |
128: | 8 | MetadataPreserver | Pending |
129: | 9 | bootstrap scripts | Pending |
130: | 10 | Integration tests | Pending |
131: 
132: ## Dependencies
133: 
134: ### Build & Runtime (from Gradle)
135: 
136: | Dependency | Version | License | Project |
137: |---|---|---|---|
138: | Tess4J | 5.18.0 | Apache 2.0 | https://github.com/nguyenq/tess4j |
139: | picocli | 4.7.6 | Apache 2.0 | https://picocli.info |
140: | PDFBox | 3.0.6 | Apache 2.0 | https://pdfbox.apache.org |
141: | pdfbox-tools | 3.0.6 | Apache 2.0 | https://pdfbox.apache.org |
142: | JNA | 5.18.1 | Apache 2.0 / LGPL 2.1 | https://github.com/java-native-access/jna |
143: | jai-imageio-core | 1.4.0 | BSD 3-Clause | https://github.com/jai-imageio/jai-imageio-core |
144: | commons-io | 2.21.0 | Apache 2.0 | https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-io/ |
145: | lept4j | 1.23.0 | Apache 2.0 | https://github.com/nguyenq/lept4j |
146: | jboss-vfs | 3.2.17.Final | Apache 2.0 | https://github.com/jbossas/jboss-vfs |
147: | slf4j-api | 2.0.17 | MIT | https://www.slf4j.org |
148: | jbig2-imageio | 3.0.4 | Apache 2.0 | https://pdfbox.apache.org |
149: | bcpkix-jdk18on | 1.82 | Bouncy Castle (MIT-like) | https://www.bouncycastle.org |
150: | Shadow plugin | 8.1.1 | Apache 2.0 | https://github.com/johnrengelman/shadow |
151: 
152: ### Test (from Gradle)
153: 
154: | Dependency | Version | License | Project |
155: |---|---|---|---|
156: | JUnit Jupiter | 5.11.4 | EPL 2.0 | https://junit.org/junit5/ |
157: | JUnit Platform Launcher | 1.11.4 | EPL 2.0 | https://junit.org/junit5/ |
158: 
159: ### Native / Bundled
160: 
161: | Dependency | License | Project |
162: |---|---|---|

164: | Gradle | Apache 2.0 | https://gradle.org |
165: | Tesseract OCR | Apache 2.0 | https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tesseract |
166: | Leptonica | BSD 2-Clause | https://github.com/DanBloomberg/leptonica |
167: | jbig2enc | Apache 2.0 | https://github.com/agl/jbig2enc |
168: 
169: ## License
170: 
171: TrulyFreeOCR itself is made available under the Apache 2.0 License. All dependencies listed above use permissive open-source licenses compatible with commercial use. A full `NOTICE.md` with copyright attributions is included in the repository.

Four sub-steps:\n\n1. **Grayscale conversion** — ITU-R BT.601 luma formula: `Y = 0.299R + 0.587G + 0.114B`  \n   Perception-weighted coefficients matching human visual sensitivity (most sensitive to green, least to blue).\n\n2. **Background normalization** — Corrects non-uniform illumination (shadows, scanner vignetting).\n   - Divides the image into tiles (configurable via `segmenter.tileSize`, default 64×64 px)\n   - Estimates background level per tile using a configurable percentile (default 95th) of pixel intensities\n   - Builds a smooth background surface via **bilinear interpolation** between tile centres\n   - Stretches each pixel: `new = old × 255 / bg_estimate` (background pixels map to white, text stays dark)\n\n3. **Otsu binarization** — Automatically finds the optimal threshold by maximising **inter-class variance**:\n   `σ² = wB × wF × (μB − μF)²` where `w` = class weight and `μ` = class mean.\n   Exhaustively searches all 256 grayscale levels for the split with the best separation.\n\n4. **Inpainting** — Replaces foreground (text) pixels with the nearest background pixel colour within a configurable radius (default 3 px). 1: # TrulyFreeOCR
2: 
3: Commercially-safe OCR pipeline for non-searchable PDFs. All dependencies use permissive open-source licenses (Apache 2.0 / MIT / BSD) — no AGPL, no proprietary components.
4: 
5: **Input**: PDF with non-searchable pages (image-only scans)  
6: **Output**: Searchable, compressed PDF
7: 
8: ---
9:

<type>file</type>
<content>
40: 
41: ## Implemented Components
42: 
43: ### PageExtractor
44: Renders each PDF page at 300 DPI via PDFBox's `PDFRenderer.renderImageWithDPI()`.
45: 
46: **Algorithm**: Standard PDF rasterisation. US Letter at 300 DPI produces 2550×3300 px images. Uses PDFBox's SFF (subpixel font finalizer) for high-quality text rendering.
47: 
48: ### ImageSegmenter
49: Pure-Java page segmentation (no Leptonica dependency). Four sub-steps:
50: 
51: 1. **Grayscale conversion** — ITU-R BT.601 luma formula: `Y = 0.299R + 0.587G + 0.114B`  
52:    Perception-weighted coefficients matching human visual sensitivity (most sensitive to green, least to blue).
53: 
54: 2. **Background normalization** — Corrects non-uniform illumination (shadows, scanner vignetting).
55:    - Divides the image into 64×64 px tiles
56:    - Estimates background level per tile using the **95th percentile** of pixel intensities (text is dark, so the bright tail of the histogram represents the page surface)
57:    - Builds a smooth background surface via **bilinear interpolation** between tile centres
58:    - Stretches each pixel: `new = old × 255 / bg_estimate` (background pixels map to white, text stays dark)
59: 
60: 3. **Otsu binarization** — Automatically finds the optimal threshold by maximising **inter-class variance**:
61:    `σ² = wB × wF × (μB − μF)²` where `w` = class weight and `μ` = class mean.
62:    Exhaustively searches all 256 grayscale levels for the split with the best separation.
63: 
64: 4. **Inpainting** — Replaces foreground (text) pixels with the nearest background pixel colour within a 3-pixel radius. Produces a cleaned background layer for PDF re-assembly.
65: 
66: ### PageExtractor
67: Renders each PDF page via PDFBox's `PDFRenderer.renderImageWithDPI()`. Default DPI is 300, configurable via `settings.jsonc` or `--dpi` CLI flag.
68: 
69: ### ImageSegmenter
70: Pure-Java page segmentation (no Leptonica dependency). Four sub-steps:
71: 
72: 1. **Grayscale conversion** — ITU-R BT.601 luma formula: `Y = 0.299R + 0.587G + 0.114B`  
73:    Perception-weighted coefficients matching human visual sensitivity (most sensitive to green, least to blue).
74: 
75: 2. **Background normalization** — Corrects non-uniform illumination (shadows, scanner vignetting).
76:    - Divides the image into tiles (configurable via `segmenter.tileSize`, default 64×64 px)
77:    - Estimates background level per tile using a configurable percentile (default 95th) of pixel intensities
78:    - Builds a smooth background surface via **bilinear interpolation** between tile centres
79:    - Stretches each pixel: `new = old × 255 / bg_estimate` (background pixels map to white, text stays dark)
80: 
81: 3. **Otsu binarization** — Automatically finds the optimal threshold by maximising **inter-class variance**:
82:    `σ² = wB × wF × (μB − μF)²` where `w` = class weight and `μ` = class mean.
83:    Exhaustively searches all 256 grayscale levels for the split with the best separation.
84: 
85: 4. **Inpainting** — Replaces foreground (text) pixels with the nearest background pixel colour within a configurable radius (default 3 px). Produces a cleaned background layer for PDF re-assembly.
86: 
87: ### OCREngine
88: Runs Tesseract as a **subprocess** (not JNA/Tess4J) to avoid native-library version mismatches with system Leptonica.
89: 
90: - Input: BufferedImage → temp PNG
91: - CLI flags: `tesseract input.png output -l eng --psm 1 tsv` (language and PSM configurable)
92: - Output: TSV file parsed for **word-level** bounding boxes (`level=5` rows with `left, top, width, height, conf, text`)
93: - Each word returned as a `TextBlock` with bounding box and confidence score
94: 
95: ### JBIG2Compressor
96: Attempts compression via **jbig2enc** subprocess with `-p -s` flags (symbol table + smearing refinement).
97: 
98: - jbig2enc outputs a `.sym` file (global symbol dictionary) and a `.jbig2` file (page data), concatenated for PDF JBIG2Decode streams
99: - Falls back to PNG byte-array output when jbig2enc is not available

(Showing lines 40-99 of 192. Use offset=100 to continue.)
</content>
```

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/README.md",
  "oldString": "### JBIG2Compressor\nAttempts compression via **jbig2enc** subprocess with `-p -s` flags (symbol table + smearing refinement).\n\n- jbig2enc outputs a `.sym` file (global symbol dictionary) and a `.jbig2` file (page data), concatenated for PDF JBIG2Decode streams\n- Falls back to PNG byte-array output when jbig2enc is not available\n\n### PDFAssembler\nRe-assembles the output PDF using PDFBox:\n\n- **Background layer**: Cleaned background image embedded via `LosslessFactory.createFromImage()`\n- **Text layer**: OCR words at their bounding-box positions, rendered in **mode 3 (NEITHER)** — invisible on screen/print, but selectable and searchable\n- **Coordinate transform**: `scaleX = pageWidth_pts / imageWidth_px`, Y-flip because image origin is top-left and PDF origin is bottom-left\n- Uses Helvetica (Standard 14 Font) — no embedding needed\n\n---",
  "newString": "### JBIG2Compressor\nAttempts compression via **jbig2enc** subprocess (flags configurable via `jbig2enc.flags`, default `-p -s`).\n\n- jbig2enc outputs a `.sym` file (global symbol dictionary) and a `.jbig2` file (page data), concatenated for PDF JBIG2Decode streams\n- Falls back to **CCITT Group 4** fax encoding via PDFBox's `CCITTFactory` when jbig2enc is unavailable\n\n### PDFAssembler\nRe-assembles the output PDF using PDFBox with an MRC-like layer structure:\n\n- **Layer 1 (background)**: Cleaned page image embedded via `LosslessFactory.createFromImage()`\n- **Layer 2 (foreground stencil)**: Binary mask compressed as CCITT G4 `ImageMask` — reproduces text pixels at full sharpness on top of the cleaned background\n- **Layer 3 (text)**: OCR words at their bounding-box positions, rendered in **mode 3 (NEITHER)** — invisible on screen/print, but selectable and searchable\n- Uses a configurable Standard 14 font (default Helvetica) — no embedding needed\n- **Coordinate transform**: `scaleX = pageWidth_pts / imageWidth_px`, Y-flip because image origin is top-left and PDF origin is bottom-left\n\n### MetadataPreserver\nCopies document metadata from the source PDF to the output:\n- **Document information** — title, author, subject, keywords, creator, producer\n- **Outlines** (bookmark tree) — COS-level deep copy\n- **Per-page annotations** — text notes, highlights, links (each annotation COS dictionary is cloned)\n- **XML metadata** (XMP stream)\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\nAll pipeline parameters are configurable via `settings.jsonc` in the project root directory (JSON with `//` and `/* */` comments). CLI flags override file settings, which override hardcoded defaults.\n\n| Setting | Default | Description |\n|---|---|---|\n| `native.dir` | `./native` | Native binaries directory (jbig2enc, etc.) |\n| `tessdata.dir` | `./tessdata` | Tesseract language data directory |\n| `tesseract.path` | `tesseract` | Tesseract executable path/name |\n| `tesseract.language` | `eng` | Tesseract language model |\n| `tesseract.psm` | `1` | Page segmentation mode |\n| `rendering.dpi` | `300` | PDF rendering resolution |\n| `segmenter.tileSize` | `64` | Background normalization tile size (px) |\n| `segmenter.percentile` | `0.95` | Background level percentile per tile |\n| `segmenter.inpaintRadius` | `3` | Inpainting search radius (px) |\n| `jbig2enc.flags` | `-p -s` | Flags passed to jbig2enc |\n| `pdf.font` | `HELVETICA` | Standard 14 font for OCR text |\n| `pdf.minFontSize` | `1` | Minimum OCR text font size (pt) |\n| `output.file` | `output.pdf` | Default output file path |\n\nCLI flags that override corresponding settings: `--dpi`, `--language`, `--psm`, `--native-dir`, `--tessdata-dir`, `-o`/`--output`, `--settings` (path to a custom settings file).\n\nTo use a custom settings file path:\n```bash\njava -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar --settings /path/to/custom.jsonc input.pdf\n```\n\n---"
}
```

<path>/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/README.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # TrulyFreeOCR
2: 
3: Commercially-safe OCR pipeline for non-searchable PDFs. All dependencies use permissive open-source licenses (Apache 2.0 / MIT / BSD) — no AGPL, no proprietary components.
4: 
5: **Input**: PDF with non-searchable pages (image-only scans)  
6: **Output**: Searchable, compressed PDF
7: 
8: ---
9: 
10: ## Pipeline
11: 
12: ```
13: Input PDF
14:     |
15:     v
16: PageExtractor  — PDFBox PDFRenderer (configurable DPI) → per-page BufferedImage
17:     |
18:     v
19: ImageSegmenter — Pure-Java foreground/background segmentation:
20:                    grayscale → background normalize → Otsu binarize → inpaint
21:     |
22:     +----+----+
23:     |         |
24:     v         v
25: JBIG2Comp.   OCREngine      (parallel)
26:     |         |
27:     | jbig2enc| Tesseract CLI
28:     | subproc | word-level bboxes with TSV output
29:     |         |
30:     +----+----+
31:     |
32:     v
33: PDFAssembler — Background layer + CCITT G4 foreground mask stencil
34:     |            + invisible OCR text (rendering mode 3)
35:     v
36: Output PDF (searchable, compressed)
37: ```
38: 
39: ---
40: 
41: ## Implemented Components
42: 
43: ### PageExtractor
44: Renders each PDF page via PDFBox's `PDFRenderer.renderImageWithDPI()`. Default DPI is 300, configurable via `settings.jsonc` or `--dpi` CLI flag.
45: 
46: ### ImageSegmenter
47: Pure-Java page segmentation (no Leptonica dependency). Four sub-steps:
48: 
49: 1. **Grayscale conversion** — ITU-R BT.601 luma formula: `Y = 0.299R + 0.587G + 0.114B`  
50:    Perception-weighted coefficients matching human visual sensitivity (most sensitive to green, least to blue).
51: 
52: 2. **Background normalization** — Corrects non-uniform illumination (shadows, scanner vignetting).
53:    - Divides the image into tiles (configurable via `segmenter.tileSize`, default 64×64 px)
54:    - Estimates background level per tile using a configurable percentile (default 95th) of pixel intensities
55:    - Builds a smooth background surface via **bilinear interpolation** between tile centres
56:    - Stretches each pixel: `new = old × 255 / bg_estimate` (background pixels map to white, text stays dark)
57: 
58: 3. **Otsu binarization** — Automatically finds the optimal threshold by maximising **inter-class variance**:
59:    `σ² = wB × wF × (μB − μF)²` where `w` = class weight and `μ` = class mean.
60:    Exhaustively searches all 256 grayscale levels for the split with the best separation.
61: 
62: 4. **Inpainting** — Replaces foreground (text) pixels with the nearest background pixel colour within a configurable radius (default 3 px). Produces a cleaned background layer for PDF re-assembly.
63: 
64: ### OCREngine
65: Runs Tesseract as a **subprocess** (not JNA/Tess4J) to avoid native-library version mismatches with system Leptonica.
66: 
67: - Input: BufferedImage → temp PNG
68: - CLI flags: `tesseract input.png output -l eng --psm 1 tsv` (language and PSM configurable)
69: - Output: TSV file parsed for **word-level** bounding boxes (`level=5` rows with `left, top, width, height, conf, text`)
70: - Each word returned as a `TextBlock` with bounding box and confidence score
71: 
72: ### JBIG2Compressor
73: Attempts compression via **jbig2enc** subprocess (flags configurable via `jbig2enc.flags`, default `-p -s`).
74: 
75: - jbig2enc outputs a `.sym` file (global symbol dictionary) and a `.jbig2` file (page data), concatenated for PDF JBIG2Decode streams
76: - Falls back to **CCITT Group 4** fax encoding via PDFBox's `CCITTFactory` when jbig2enc is unavailable
77: 
78: ### PDFAssembler
79: Re-assembles the output PDF using PDFBox with an MRC-like layer structure:
80: 
81: - **Layer 1 (background)**: Cleaned page image embedded via `LosslessFactory.createFromImage()`
82: - **Layer 2 (foreground stencil)**: Binary mask compressed as CCITT G4 `ImageMask` — reproduces text pixels at full sharpness on top of the cleaned background
83: - **Layer 3 (text)**: OCR words at their bounding-box positions, rendered in **mode 3 (NEITHER)** — invisible on screen/print, but selectable and searchable
84: - Uses a configurable Standard 14 font (default Helvetica) — no embedding needed
85: - **Coordinate transform**: `scaleX = pageWidth_pts / imageWidth_px`, Y-flip because image origin is top-left and PDF origin is bottom-left
86: 
87: ### MetadataPreserver
88: Copies document metadata from the source PDF to the output:
89: - **Document information** — title, author, subject, keywords, creator, producer
90: - **Outlines** (bookmark tree) — COS-level deep copy
91: - **Per-page annotations** — text notes, highlights, links (each annotation COS dictionary is cloned)
92: - **XML metadata** (XMP stream)
93: 
94: ---
95: 
96: ## Configuration
97: 
98: All pipeline parameters are configurable via `settings.jsonc` in the project root directory (JSON with `//` and `/* */` comments). CLI flags override file settings, which override hardcoded defaults.
99: 
100: | Setting | Default | Description |
101: |---|---|---|
102: | `native.dir` | `./native` | Native binaries directory (jbig2enc, etc.) |
103: | `tessdata.dir` | `./tessdata` | Tesseract language data directory |
104: | `tesseract.path` | `tesseract` | Tesseract executable path/name |
105: | `tesseract.language` | `eng` | Tesseract language model |
106: | `tesseract.psm` | `1` | Page segmentation mode |
107: | `rendering.dpi` | `300` | PDF rendering resolution |
108: | `segmenter.tileSize` | `64` | Background normalization tile size (px) |
109: | `segmenter.percentile` | `0.95` | Background level percentile per tile |
110: | `segmenter.inpaintRadius` | `3` | Inpainting search radius (px) |
111: | `jbig2enc.flags` | `-p -s` | Flags passed to jbig2enc |
112: | `pdf.font` | `HELVETICA` | Standard 14 font for OCR text |
113: | `pdf.minFontSize` | `1` | Minimum OCR text font size (pt) |
114: | `output.file` | `output.pdf` | Default output file path |
115: 
116: CLI flags that override corresponding settings: `--dpi`, `--language`, `--psm`, `--native-dir`, `--tessdata-dir`, `-o`/`--output`, `--settings` (path to a custom settings file).
117: 
118: To use a custom settings file path:
119: ```bash
120: java -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar --settings /path/to/custom.jsonc input.pdf
121: ```
122: 
123: ---
124: 
125: ## Building
126: 
127: ```bash
128: # Build the fat JAR
129: ./gradlew build
130: 
131: # Run all tests
132: ./gradlew test
133: ```
134: 
135: Requires **Java 21+** (JDK) and **Tesseract OCR** CLI (`tesseract` on PATH) with English language data installed.
136: 
137: ## Testing
138: 
139: Tests use generated sample PDFs in `tests/test-files/`:
140: - `blank.pdf` — single blank US Letter page
141: - `simple-text.pdf` — one page with lorem ipsum text
142: - `multi-page.pdf` — three-page document with distinct content per page
143: - `two-column.pdf` — two-column layout
144: - `with-annotations.pdf` — bookmarks, links, and comments
145: - `noisy-scan.pdf` — simulated scanner noise and skew
146: 
147: Regenerate test PDFs with:
148: ```bash
149: python tests/generate_test_pdfs.py
150: ```
151: 
152: ## Current Status
153: 
154: | Phase | Component | Status |
155: |-------|-----------|--------|
156: | 1 | Gradle scaffold + CLI | Done |
157: | 2 | PageExtractor | Done |
158: | 3 | OCREngine | Done |
159: | 4 | ImageSegmenter | Done |
160: | 5 | PDFAssembler (basic) | Done |
161: | 6 | JBIG2Compressor + SubprocessRunner | Done |
162: | 7 | PDFAssembler JBIG2 integration | Done |
163: | 8 | MetadataPreserver | Done |
164: | 9 | bootstrap scripts | Done |
165: | 10 | Integration tests | Done |
166: 
167: ## Dependencies
168: 
169: ### Build & Runtime (from Gradle)
170: 
171: | Dependency | Version | License | Project |
172: |---|---|---|---|
173: | PDFBox | 3.0.6 | Apache 2.0 | https://pdfbox.apache.org |
174: | picocli | 4.7.6 | Apache 2.0 | https://picocli.info |
175: | Shadow plugin | 8.1.1 | Apache 2.0 | https://github.com/johnrengelman/shadow |
176: 
177: ### Test (from Gradle)
178: 
179: | Dependency | Version | License | Project |
180: |---|---|---|---|
181: | JUnit Jupiter | 5.11.4 | EPL 2.0 | https://junit.org/junit5/ |
182: | JUnit Platform Launcher | 1.11.4 | EPL 2.0 | https://junit.org/junit5/ |
183: 
184: ### Native / Bundled
185: 
186: | Dependency | License | Project |
187: |---|---|---|
188: | OpenJDK 21 LTS | GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception | https://adoptium.net |
189: | Gradle | Apache 2.0 | https://gradle.org |
190: | Tesseract OCR | Apache 2.0 | https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tesseract |
191: | Leptonica | BSD 2-Clause | https://github.com/DanBloomberg/leptonica |
192: | jbig2enc | Apache 2.0 | https://github.com/agl/jbig2enc |
193: 
194: ## License
195: 
196: TrulyFreeOCR itself is made available under the Apache 2.0 License. All dependencies listed above use permissive open-source licenses compatible with commercial use. A full `NOTICE.md` with copyright attributions is included in the repository.

(End of file - total 196 lines)
</content>
```
