```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr;
2: 
3: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
4: import java.io.File;
5: import java.io.IOException;
6: import java.util.ArrayList;
7: import java.util.List;
8: import java.util.concurrent.Callable;
9: 
10: import picocli.CommandLine;
11: import picocli.CommandLine.Command;
12: import picocli.CommandLine.Option;
13: import picocli.CommandLine.Parameters;
14: 
15: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;
16: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.SegmentedImage;
17: import com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline.ImageSegmenter;
18: import com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline.JBIG2Compressor;
19: import com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline.OCREngine;
20: import com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline.PDFAssembler;
21: import com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline.PageExtractor;
22: import com.trulyfreeocr.util.Settings;
23: 
24: @Command(
25:     name = "trulyfreeocr",
26:     version = "1.0.0",
27:     description = "Commercially-safe OCR pipeline for non-searchable PDFs",
28:     mixinStandardHelpOptions = true
29: )
30: public class TrulyFreeOCR implements Callable<Integer> {
31: 
32:     @Parameters(index = "0", description = "Input PDF file")
33:     private File inputFile;
34: 
35:     @Option(names = {"-o", "--output"}, description = "Output PDF file")
36:     private File outputFile;
37: 
38:     @Option(names = {"--native-dir"}, description = "Directory containing native binaries")
39:     private File nativeDir;
40: 
41:     @Option(names = {"--tessdata-dir"}, description = "Directory containing Tesseract language data")
42:     private File tessdataDir;
43: 
44:     @Option(names = {"--settings"}, description = "Path to settings.jsonc file")
45:     private File settingsFile;
46: 
47:     @Option(names = {"--dpi"}, description = "Rendering DPI for PDF page images")
48:     private Float dpi;
49: 
50:     @Option(names = {"--language"}, description = "Tesseract language model")
51:     private String language;
52: 
53:     @Option(names = {"--psm"}, description = "Tesseract page segmentation mode")
54:     private String psm;
55: 
56:     @Override
57:     public Integer call() {
58:         Settings settings = Settings.load();
59:         if (settingsFile != null) {
60:             System.setProperty("tfocr.settings", settingsFile.getAbsolutePath());
61:             settings = Settings.load();
62:         }
63: 
64:         System.out.println("TrulyFreeOCR v1.0.0");
65:         System.out.println("  Input:  " + inputFile);
66: 
67:         try {
68:             // Resolve each parameter: CLI arg > settings.jsonc > hardcoded default
69:             File resolvedOutput = outputFile != null ? outputFile
70:                     : new File(settings.getString("output.file", "output.pdf"));
71:             String resolvedTessdata = tessdataDir != null ? tessdataDir.getAbsolutePath()
72:                     : settings.getString("tessdata.dir", "./tessdata");
73:             String resolvedNative = nativeDir != null ? nativeDir.getAbsolutePath()
74:                     : settings.getString("native.dir", "native");
75: 
76:             System.out.println("  Output: " + resolvedOutput);
77: 
78:             // Create pipeline components with configured values
79:             float resolvedDpi = dpi != null ? dpi
80:                     : (float) settings.getDouble("rendering.dpi", 300);
81:             String resolvedLang = language != null ? language
82:                     : settings.getString("tesseract.language", "eng");
83:             String resolvedPsm = psm != null ? psm
84:                     : settings.getString("tesseract.psm", "1");
85: 
86:             PageExtractor extractor = new PageExtractor(resolvedDpi);
87:             ImageSegmenter segmenter = new ImageSegmenter(
88:                     settings.getInt("segmenter.tileSize", 64),
89:                     settings.getDouble("segmenter.percentile", 0.95),
90:                     settings.getInt("segmenter.inpaintRadius", 3)
91:             );
92:             OCREngine ocrEngine = new OCREngine(
93:                     resolvedTessdata,
94:                     settings.getString("tesseract.path", "tesseract"),
95:                     resolvedLang,
96:                     resolvedPsm
97:             );
98:             JBIG2Compressor compressor = new JBIG2Compressor(resolvedNative);
99:             PDFAssembler assembler = new PDFAssembler(
100:                     settings.getString("pdf.font", "HELVETICA"),
101:                     (float) settings.getDouble("pdf.minFontSize", 1.0)
102:             );
103: 
104:             // Run the pipeline
105:             long t0 = System.nanoTime();
106:             runPipeline(inputFile, resolvedOutput, extractor, segmenter, ocrEngine, compressor, assembler);
107:             long elapsed = System.nanoTime() - t0;
108: 
109:             System.out.printf("Done.  Total time: %.1fs%n", elapsed / 1e9);
110:             return 0;
111: 
112:         } catch (Exception e) {
113:             System.err.println("Error: " + e.getMessage());
114:             e.printStackTrace();
115:             return 1;
116:         }
117:     }
118: 
119:     private void runPipeline(File inputFile, File outputFile,
120:                              PageExtractor extractor, ImageSegmenter segmenter,
121:                              OCREngine ocrEngine, JBIG2Compressor compressor,
122:                              PDFAssembler assembler) throws IOException {
123: 
124:         // Phase 1: Extract pages as images
125:         long t1 = System.nanoTime();
126:         System.out.println("  Extracting pages...");
127:         List<BufferedImage> pages;
128:         try (PageExtractor pe = extractor) {
129:             pages = pe.extractPages(inputFile);
130:         }
131:         long t2 = System.nanoTime();
132:         System.out.printf("    Extracted %d pages in %.1fs%n", pages.size(), (t2 - t1) / 1e9);
133: 
134:         // Phase 2: Segment each page (background normalization + binarization)
135:         System.out.println("  Segmenting pages...");
136:         List<BufferedImage> backgrounds = new ArrayList<>(pages.size());
137:         List<BufferedImage> foregroundMasks = new ArrayList<>(pages.size());
138:         for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {
139:             long ts = System.nanoTime();
140:             SegmentedImage seg = segmenter.segment(pages.get(i));
141:             double elapsed = (System.nanoTime() - ts) / 1e9;
142:             backgrounds.add(seg.getCleanedBackground());
143:             foregroundMasks.add(seg.getForegroundMask());
144:             System.out.printf("    Page %3d: %.1fs%n", i + 1, elapsed);
145:         }
146:         long t3 = System.nanoTime();
147:         System.out.printf("    Segmentation done in %.1fs%n", (t3 - t2) / 1e9);
148: 
149:         // Phase 3: OCR each page
150:         System.out.println("  Running OCR...");
151:         List<PageResult> ocrResults = new ArrayList<>(pages.size());
152:         for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {
153:             long ts = System.nanoTime();
154:             ocrResults.add(ocrEngine.ocr(pages.get(i)));
155:             double elapsed = (System.nanoTime() - ts) / 1e9;
156:             System.out.printf("    Page %3d: %.1fs%n", i + 1, elapsed);
157:         }
158:         long t4 = System.nanoTime();
159:         System.out.printf("    OCR done in %.1fs%n", (t4 - t3) / 1e9);
160: 
161:         // Phase 4: Assemble searchable PDF with foreground mask overlay
162:         System.out.println("  Assembling PDF...");
163:         try (var doc = assembler.assemble(inputFile, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults)) {
164:             doc.save(outputFile);
165:         }
166:         long t5 = System.nanoTime();
167:         System.out.printf("    Assembly done in %.1fs%n", (t5 - t4) / 1e9);
168:     }
169: 
170:     public static void main(String[] args) {
171:         int exitCode = new CommandLine(new TrulyFreeOCR()).execute(args);
172:         System.exit(exitCode);
173:     }
174: }
```

### Tool: read

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/tests/generate_eval_corpus.py"
```

```
<path>/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/tests/generate_eval_corpus.py</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env python3
2: """Generate realistic evaluation corpus for OCR accuracy testing.
3: 
4: Downloads public-domain text from Project Gutenberg, renders it as
5: image-only PDFs at various page counts, and writes ground-truth JSON
6: for automated WER/CER evaluation.
7: 
8: Generated files (saved in tests/eval-corpus/):
9:   sherlock-holmes-010p.pdf  + sherlock-holmes-010p.json  (10 pages)
10:   sherlock-holmes-050p.pdf  + sherlock-holmes-050p.json  (50 pages)
11:   sherlock-holmes-200p.pdf  + sherlock-holmes-200p.json  (200 pages)
12:   sherlock-holmes-500p.pdf  + sherlock-holmes-500p.json  (500 pages)
13: 
14: Run once to generate the corpus, then reuse across eval runs.
15: Pass --force to regenerate from scratch.
16: """
17: 
18: import argparse
19: import gzip
20: import io
21: import json
22: import os
23: import re
24: import sys
25: import urllib.request
26: 
27: from PIL import Image, ImageDraw, ImageFont
28: import fitz
29: 
30: OUT_DIR = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "eval-corpus")
31: os.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
32: 
33: # Same layout constants as tests/generate_test_pdfs.py
34: FONT_DIR = "/usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu"
35: W, H = 612, 792  # US Letter at 72 DPI
36: MARGIN = 50
37: FONT_SZ = 11
38: LINE_HEIGHT = 17
39: MAX_WIDTH = W - 2 * MARGIN  # pixels available for text per line
40: 
41: GUTENBERG_URL = "https://www.gutenberg.org/files/1661/1661-0.txt"
42: 
43: PAGE_COUNTS = [10, 50, 200, 500]
44: 
45: 
46: def download_text(url):
47:     print(f"  Downloading {url}...")
48:     with urllib.request.urlopen(url) as resp:
49:         data = resp.read()
50:     # Gutenberg serves UTF-8 or Windows-1252; try UTF-8 first.
51:     try:
52:         return data.decode("utf-8")
53:     except UnicodeDecodeError:
54:         return data.decode("windows-1252")
55: 
56: 
57: def strip_gutenberg_boilerplate(text):
58:     # Remove header: keep everything after "START OF THIS PROJECT GUTENBERG EBOOK"
59:     m = re.search(r"\*\*\* START OF (THIS PROJECT|THE PROJECT) GUTENBERG EBOOK.*?\*\*\*", text)
60:     if m:
61:         text = text[m.end():]
62:     # Remove footer: stop before "END OF THIS PROJECT GUTENBERG EBOOK"
63:     m = re.search(r"\*\*\* END OF (THIS PROJECT|THE PROJECT) GUTENBERG EBOOK.*?\*\*\*", text)
64:     if m:
65:         text = text[:m.start()]
66:     return text.strip()
67: 
68: 
69: def clean_line(line):
70:     # Keep only printable ASCII-ish text; drop Gutenberg illustration markers etc.
71:     line = line.strip()
72:     if not line:
73:         return ""
74:     # Skip lines that are mostly punctuation or illustration markers
75:     if re.search(r"[_=]{4,}", line):
76:         return ""
77:     if re.search(r"^\[Illustration", line):
78:         return ""
79:     return line
80: 
81: 
82: def get_font(size=FONT_SZ):
83:     return ImageFont.truetype(os.path.join(FONT_DIR, "DejaVuSans.ttf"), size)
84: 
85: 
86: def wrap_line(draw, text, font, max_width):
87:     """Word-wrap text to fit within max_width pixels using the given font."""
88:     words = text.split()
89:     if not words:
90:         return [""]
91:     lines = []
92:     current = words[0]
93:     for w in words[1:]:
94:         candidate = current + " " + w
95:         w_px = draw.textlength(candidate, font=font)
96:         if w_px <= max_width:
97:             current = candidate
98:         else:
99:             lines.append(current)
100:             current = w
101:     lines.append(current)
102:     return lines
103: 
104: 
105: def render_page(draw, font, page_lines, y_start):
106:     """Render a list of lines onto the draw surface, returning the final y."""
107:     y = y_start
108:     for line in page_lines:
109:         if line:
110:             draw.text((MARGIN, y), line, fill="black", font=font)
111:         y += LINE_HEIGHT
112:     return y
113: 
114: 
115: def make_pdf_and_ground_truth(all_lines, page_count, output_stem, font):
116:     """Render the first `page_count` pages and produce an image PDF + JSON."""
117:     pages_img = []
118:     ground_truth = []
119: 
120:     draw = None
121:     current_page_words = []
122:     lines_this_page = 0
123:     max_lines = (H - MARGIN) // LINE_HEIGHT  # ~33 lines per page
124: 
125:     for line in all_lines:
126:         if lines_this_page == 0:
127:             img = Image.new("RGB", (W, H), "white")
128:             draw = ImageDraw.Draw(img)
129:             current_page_words = []
130: 
131:         if line:
132:             render_page(draw, font, [line], MARGIN + lines_this_page * LINE_HEIGHT)
133:             current_page_words.extend(line.split())
134: 
135:         lines_this_page += 1
136: 
137:         if lines_this_page >= max_lines:
138:             pages_img.append(img)
139:             ground_truth.append({"page": len(ground_truth) + 1, "words": current_page_words})
140:             lines_this_page = 0
141: 
142:             if len(pages_img) >= page_count:
143:                 break
144: 
145:     # Flush partial page if not full
146:     if lines_this_page > 0 and len(pages_img) < page_count and draw is not None:
147:         pages_img.append(img)
148:         ground_truth.append({"page": len(ground_truth) + 1, "words": current_page_words})
149: 
150:     if not pages_img:
151:         print(f"    Warning: no pages generated for {output_stem}")
152:         return
153: 
154:     # Truncate to requested page count
155:     pages_img = pages_img[:page_count]
156:     ground_truth = ground_truth[:page_count]
157: 
158:     # Save PDF
159:     pdf_path = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, output_stem + ".pdf")
160:     doc = fitz.open()
161:     for img in pages_img:
162:         buf = io.BytesIO()
163:         img.save(buf, format="JPEG", quality=95)
164:         buf.seek(0)
165:         pix = fitz.Pixmap(buf.read())
166:         page = doc.new_page(width=W, height=H)
167:         page.insert_image(fitz.Rect(0, 0, W, H), pixmap=pix)
168:     doc.save(pdf_path)
169:     doc.close()
170:     print(f"    {pdf_path} ({len(pages_img)} pages, {sum(len(p['words']) for p in ground_truth)} words)")
171: 
172:     # Save ground truth JSON
173:     json_path = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, output_stem + ".json")
174:     with open(json_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
175:         json.dump({
176:             "source": GUTENBERG_URL,
177:             "title": "The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes",
178:             "pages": ground_truth,
179:         }, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
180:     print(f"    {json_path}")
181: 
182: 
183: def generate(force=False):
184:     for pc in PAGE_COUNTS:
185:         pdf_path = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, f"sherlock-holmes-{pc:03d}p.pdf")
186:         if os.path.exists(pdf_path) and not force:
187:             print(f"  {pdf_path} exists, skipping (use --force to regenerate)")
188: 
189:     text = download_text(GUTENBERG_URL)
190:     text = strip_gutenberg_boilerplate(text)
191:     print(f"  Corpus length: {len(text)} chars")
192: 
193:     lines = [clean_line(l) for l in text.split("\n")]
194:     lines = [l for l in lines if l is not None]
195: 
196:     # Build a flat list of printable lines (non-empty)
197:     printable_lines = [l for l in lines if l]
198: 
199:     empty_img = Image.new("RGB", (W, H), "white")
200:     empty_draw = ImageDraw.Draw(empty_img)
201:     font = get_font()
202: 
203:     total_available = len(printable_lines)
204:     max_lines_per_page = (H - MARGIN) // LINE_HEIGHT
205:     total_pages_available = total_available // max_lines_per_page + 1
206:     print(f"  Text yields ~{total_pages_available} pages at {max_lines_per_page} lines/page")
207: 
208:     for pc in PAGE_COUNTS:
209:         output_stem = f"sherlock-holmes-{pc:03d}p"
210:         pdf_path = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, output_stem + ".pdf")
211:         if os.path.exists(pdf_path) and not force:
212:             print(f"  {output_stem} exists, skipping")
213:             continue
214:         if pc > total_pages_available:
215:             print(f"  Warning: requested {pc} pages but corpus has only ~{total_pages_available}; truncating")
216:         make_pdf_and_ground_truth(printable_lines, min(pc, total_pages_available), output_stem, font)
217:
```

Now I need to add the `--searchable` flag to generate_eval_corpus.py. When `--searchable` is passed, I'll embed invisible text in the PDF using PyMuPDF. When not passed, it stays as image-only (current behavior).

For embedding invisible text in PyMuPDF, I need to:
1. Create the page with the image as before
2. Add invisible text using `page.insert_text()` or `page.addFreetextAnnot()` 
3. The text needs to be invisible (rendering mode 3 = invisible)

With PyMuPDF (fitz), invisible text can be added via the low-level PDF operations or by setting the text rendering mode. Let me check how to do this properly.

Actually, in PyMuPDF, I can add text with a text rendering mode of 3 (invisible). Here's the approach:

```python
    # Insert text at appropriate positions
```

But the challenge is: we need to position the text correctly. Currently, the PIL image renders text at certain positions, and we'd need to match those positions in the PDF.

A simpler approach: instead of matching exact positions, we can insert the text using `page.insert_text()` with a font size and position that matches the rendering. But this is complex because we'd need to track the x,y positions of each word.

Even simpler: I can use `fitz` to add text using `page.insert_text()` with a small font and invisible render mode. Or even better, I can use `page.add_freetext_annot()` with `fill_color=None` to make it invisible.

Actually, the simplest way to create a searchable PDF with PyMuPDF is to write the invisible text at the bottom of the page or spread across the page area. It doesn't need to be pixel-perfect — it just needs to be selectable/searchable text in the correct reading order.

Or I can take an even simpler approach: just dump the text as a single invisible block on each page, positioned at the top-left. It'll be searchable/selectable even if the positions aren't exact.

- Each line starts at y = MARGIN + line_index * LINE_HEIGHT
- Each word is at some x position

But I don't have individual word positions from the rendering. However, I can estimate using text width calculations.

Let me simplify: I'll add the text per line, using the same positions as in the PIL rendering. I'll use PyMuPDF's text insertion with the same font and size.

Actually, the simplest approach that gives reasonable positions: use `get_text_width` to estimate positions, or just position the text at the same coordinates as the image rendering.


```json
  "oldString": "def make_pdf_and_ground_truth(all_lines, page_count, output_stem, font):\n    \"\"\"Render the first `page_count` pages and produce an image PDF + JSON.\"\"\"\n    pages_img = []\n    ground_truth = []\n\n    draw = None\n    current_page_words = []\n    lines_this_page = 0\n    max_lines = (H - MARGIN) // LINE_HEIGHT  # ~33 lines per page\n\n    for line in all_lines:\n        if lines_this_page == 0:\n            img = Image.new(\"RGB\", (W, H), \"white\")\n            draw = ImageDraw.Draw(img)\n            current_page_words = []\n\n        if line:\n            render_page(draw, font, [line], MARGIN + lines_this_page * LINE_HEIGHT)\n            current_page_words.extend(line.split())\n\n        lines_this_page += 1\n\n        if lines_this_page >= max_lines:\n            pages_img.append(img)\n            ground_truth.append({\"page\": len(ground_truth) + 1, \"words\": current_page_words})\n            lines_this_page = 0\n\n            if len(pages_img) >= page_count:\n                break\n\n    # Flush partial page if not full\n    if lines_this_page > 0 and len(pages_img) < page_count and draw is not None:\n        pages_img.append(img)\n        ground_truth.append({\"page\": len(ground_truth) + 1, \"words\": current_page_words})\n\n    if not pages_img:\n        print(f\"    Warning: no pages generated for {output_stem}\")\n        return\n\n    # Truncate to requested page count\n    pages_img = pages_img[:page_count]\n    ground_truth = ground_truth[:page_count]\n\n    # Save PDF\n    pdf_path = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, output_stem + \".pdf\")\n    doc = fitz.open()\n    for img in pages_img:\n        buf = io.BytesIO()\n        img.save(buf, format=\"JPEG\", quality=95)\n        buf.seek(0)\n        pix = fitz.Pixmap(buf.read())\n        page = doc.new_page(width=W, height=H)\n        page.insert_image(fitz.Rect(0, 0, W, H), pixmap=pix)\n    doc.save(pdf_path)\n    doc.close()\n    print(f\"    {pdf_path} ({len(pages_img)} pages, {sum(len(p['words']) for p in ground_truth)} words)\")\n\n    # Save ground truth JSON\n    json_path = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, output_stem + \".json\")\n    with open(json_path, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump({\n            \"source\": GUTENBERG_URL,\n            \"title\": \"The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes\",\n            \"pages\": ground_truth,\n        }, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)\n    print(f\"    {json_path}\")",
  "newString": "def make_pdf_and_ground_truth(all_lines, page_count, output_stem, font, searchable=False):\n    \"\"\"Render the first `page_count` pages and produce a PDF + JSON.\n\n    When searchable=True, embeds an invisible text layer so the PDF\n    can be used as input for MRC re-OCR evaluation.\n    \"\"\"\n    pages_data = []  # list of (pil_image, word_list, text_lines)\n\n    draw = None\n    current_page_words = []\n    current_page_lines = []\n    lines_this_page = 0\n    max_lines = (H - MARGIN) // LINE_HEIGHT  # ~33 lines per page\n\n    for line in all_lines:\n        if lines_this_page == 0:\n            img = Image.new(\"RGB\", (W, H), \"white\")\n            draw = ImageDraw.Draw(img)\n            current_page_words = []\n            current_page_lines = []\n\n        if line:\n            render_page(draw, font, [line], MARGIN + lines_this_page * LINE_HEIGHT)\n            current_page_words.extend(line.split())\n            current_page_lines.append(line)\n        else:\n            current_page_lines.append(\"\")\n\n        lines_this_page += 1\n\n        if lines_this_page >= max_lines:\n            pages_data.append((img, current_page_words, current_page_lines))\n            lines_this_page = 0\n\n            if len(pages_data) >= page_count:\n                break\n\n    # Flush partial page if not full\n    if lines_this_page > 0 and len(pages_data) < page_count and draw is not None:\n        pages_data.append((img, current_page_words, current_page_lines))\n\n    if not pages_data:\n        print(f\"    Warning: no pages generated for {output_stem}\")\n        return\n\n    # Truncate to requested page count\n    pages_data = pages_data[:page_count]\n\n    ground_truth = [{\"page\": i + 1, \"words\": w} for i, (_, w, _) in enumerate(pages_data)]\n\n    # Save PDF\n    pdf_path = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, output_stem + \".pdf\")\n    doc = fitz.open()\n    for pil_img, _, page_lines in pages_data:\n        buf = io.BytesIO()\n        pil_img.save(buf, format=\"JPEG\", quality=95)\n        buf.seek(0)\n        pix = fitz.Pixmap(buf.read())\n        page = doc.new_page(width=W, height=H)\n        page.insert_image(fitz.Rect(0, 0, W, H), pixmap=pix)\n\n        if searchable:\n            tw = fitz.TextWriter(page.rect)\n            y = MARGIN\n            for line_text in page_lines:\n                if line_text:\n                    tw.append(fitz.Point(MARGIN, y), line_text, fontsize=FONT_SZ)\n                y += LINE_HEIGHT\n            tw.write_text(page, render_mode=3)\n\n    doc.save(pdf_path)\n    doc.close()\n    print(f\"    {pdf_path} ({len(pages_data)} pages, {sum(len(w) for _, w, _ in pages_data)} words)\"\n          f\" {'[searchable]' if searchable else '[image-only]'}\")\n\n    # Save ground truth JSON\n    json_path = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, output_stem + \".json\")\n    with open(json_path, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump({\n            \"source\": GUTENBERG_URL,\n            \"title\": \"The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes\",\n            \"pages\": ground_truth,\n        }, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)\n    print(f\"    {json_path}\")"
}
```

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/tests/generate_eval_corpus.py",
  "newString": "def generate(force=False, searchable=False):\n    for pc in PAGE_COUNTS:\n        pdf_path = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, f\"sherlock-holmes-{pc:03d}p.pdf\")\n        if os.path.exists(pdf_path) and not force:\n            print(f\"  {pdf_path} exists, skipping (use --force to regenerate)\")\n\n    text = download_text(GUTENBERG_URL)\n    text = strip_gutenberg_boilerplate(text)\n    print(f\"  Corpus length: {len(text)} chars\")\n\n    lines = [clean_line(l) for l in text.split(\"\\n\")]\n    lines = [l for l in lines if l is not None]\n\n    # Build a flat list of printable lines (non-empty)\n    printable_lines = [l for l in lines if l]\n\n    empty_img = Image.new(\"RGB\", (W, H), \"white\")\n    empty_draw = ImageDraw.Draw(empty_img)\n    font = get_font()\n\n    total_available = len(printable_lines)\n    max_lines_per_page = (H - MARGIN) // LINE_HEIGHT\n    total_pages_available = total_available // max_lines_per_page + 1\n    print(f\"  Text yields ~{total_pages_available} pages at {max_lines_per_page} lines/page\")\n\n    for pc in PAGE_COUNTS:\n        output_stem = f\"sherlock-holmes-{pc:03d}p\"\n        pdf_path = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, output_stem + \".pdf\")\n        if os.path.exists(pdf_path) and not force:\n            print(f\"  {output_stem} exists, skipping\")\n            continue\n        if pc > total_pages_available:\n            print(f\"  Warning: requested {pc} pages but corpus has only ~{total_pages_available}; truncating\")\n        make_pdf_and_ground_truth(printable_lines, min(pc, total_pages_available), output_stem, font, searchable=searchable)\n\n    print(\"Done.\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Generate OCR evaluation corpus\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--force\", action=\"store_true\", help=\"Regenerate existing files\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--searchable\", action=\"store_true\", help=\"Embed invisible text layer for MRC re-OCR testing\")\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n    generate(force=args.force, searchable=args.searchable)",
  "oldString": "def generate(force=False):\n    for pc in PAGE_COUNTS:\n        pdf_path = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, f\"sherlock-holmes-{pc:03d}p.pdf\")\n        if os.path.exists(pdf_path) and not force:\n            print(f\"  {pdf_path} exists, skipping (use --force to regenerate)\")\n\n    text = download_text(GUTENBERG_URL)\n    text = strip_gutenberg_boilerplate(text)\n    print(f\"  Corpus length: {len(text)} chars\")\n\n    lines = [clean_line(l) for l in text.split(\"\\n\")]\n    lines = [l for l in lines if l is not None]\n\n    # Build a flat list of printable lines (non-empty)\n    printable_lines = [l for l in lines if l]\n\n    empty_img = Image.new(\"RGB\", (W, H), \"white\")\n    empty_draw = ImageDraw.Draw(empty_img)\n    font = get_font()\n\n    total_available = len(printable_lines)\n    max_lines_per_page = (H - MARGIN) // LINE_HEIGHT\n    total_pages_available = total_available // max_lines_per_page + 1\n    print(f\"  Text yields ~{total_pages_available} pages at {max_lines_per_page} lines/page\")\n\n    for pc in PAGE_COUNTS:\n        output_stem = f\"sherlock-holmes-{pc:03d}p\"\n        pdf_path = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, output_stem + \".pdf\")\n        if os.path.exists(pdf_path) and not force:\n            print(f\"  {output_stem} exists, skipping\")\n            continue\n        if pc > total_pages_available:\n            print(f\"  Warning: requested {pc} pages but corpus has only ~{total_pages_available}; truncating\")\n        make_pdf_and_ground_truth(printable_lines, min(pc, total_pages_available), output_stem, font)\n\n    print(\"Done.\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Generate OCR evaluation corpus\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--force\", action=\"store_true\", help=\"Regenerate existing files\")\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n    generate(force=args.force)"
}
```

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/TrulyFreeOCR.java",
  "newString": "    private void runPipeline(File inputFile, File outputFile,\n                             PageExtractor extractor, ImageSegmenter segmenter,\n                             OCREngine ocrEngine, JBIG2Compressor compressor,\n                             PDFAssembler assembler, boolean useMrc) throws IOException {\n\n        // Phase 1: Extract pages as images\n        long t1 = System.nanoTime();\n        System.out.println(\"  Extracting pages...\");\n        List<BufferedImage> pages;\n        try (PageExtractor pe = extractor) {\n            pages = pe.extractPages(inputFile);\n        }\n        long t2 = System.nanoTime();\n        System.out.printf(\"    Extracted %d pages in %.1fs%n\", pages.size(), (t2 - t1) / 1e9);\n\n        List<BufferedImage> backgrounds;\n        List<BufferedImage> foregroundMasks;\n\n        if (useMrc) {\n            // Phase 2a: Segment — background normalization + foreground binarization\n            System.out.println(\"  Segmenting pages...\");\n            backgrounds = new ArrayList<>(pages.size());\n            foregroundMasks = new ArrayList<>(pages.size());\n            for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {\n                long ts = System.nanoTime();\n                SegmentedImage seg = segmenter.segment(pages.get(i));\n                double elapsed = (System.nanoTime() - ts) / 1e9;\n                backgrounds.add(seg.getCleanedBackground());\n                foregroundMasks.add(seg.getForegroundMask());\n                System.out.printf(\"    Page %3d: %.1fs%n\", i + 1, elapsed);\n            }\n            long t3 = System.nanoTime();\n            System.out.printf(\"    Segmentation done in %.1fs%n\", (t3 - t2) / 1e9);\n        } else {\n            System.out.println(\"  MRC disabled — using original pages as backgrounds\");\n            backgrounds = new ArrayList<>(pages);\n            foregroundMasks = null;\n        }\n\n        // Phase 3: OCR each page\n        System.out.println(\"  Running OCR...\");\n        List<PageResult> ocrResults = new ArrayList<>(pages.size());\n        for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {\n            long ts = System.nanoTime();\n            ocrResults.add(ocrEngine.ocr(pages.get(i)));\n            double elapsed = (System.nanoTime() - ts) / 1e9;\n            System.out.printf(\"    Page %3d: %.1fs%n\", i + 1, elapsed);\n        }\n        long t4 = System.nanoTime();\n        System.out.printf(\"    OCR done in %.1fs%n\", (t4 - (useMrc ? t3 : t2)) / 1e9);\n\n        // Phase 4: Assemble searchable PDF with optional MRC foreground overlay\n        System.out.println(\"  Assembling PDF...\");\n        try (var doc = assembler.assemble(inputFile, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults)) {\n            doc.save(outputFile);\n        }\n        long t5 = System.nanoTime();\n        System.out.printf(\"    Assembly done in %.1fs%n\", (t5 - t4) / 1e9);\n    }",
  "oldString": "    private void runPipeline(File inputFile, File outputFile,\n                             PageExtractor extractor, ImageSegmenter segmenter,\n                             OCREngine ocrEngine, JBIG2Compressor compressor,\n                             PDFAssembler assembler) throws IOException {\n\n        // Phase 1: Extract pages as images\n        long t1 = System.nanoTime();\n        System.out.println(\"  Extracting pages...\");\n        List<BufferedImage> pages;\n        try (PageExtractor pe = extractor) {\n            pages = pe.extractPages(inputFile);\n        }\n        long t2 = System.nanoTime();\n        System.out.printf(\"    Extracted %d pages in %.1fs%n\", pages.size(), (t2 - t1) / 1e9);\n\n        // Phase 2: Segment each page (background normalization + binarization)\n        System.out.println(\"  Segmenting pages...\");\n        List<BufferedImage> backgrounds = new ArrayList<>(pages.size());\n        List<BufferedImage> foregroundMasks = new ArrayList<>(pages.size());\n        for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {\n            long ts = System.nanoTime();\n            SegmentedImage seg = segmenter.segment(pages.get(i));\n            double elapsed = (System.nanoTime() - ts) / 1e9;\n            backgrounds.add(seg.getCleanedBackground());\n            foregroundMasks.add(seg.getForegroundMask());\n            System.out.printf(\"    Page %3d: %.1fs%n\", i + 1, elapsed);\n        }\n        long t3 = System.nanoTime();\n        System.out.printf(\"    Segmentation done in %.1fs%n\", (t3 - t2) / 1e9);\n\n        // Phase 3: OCR each page\n        System.out.println(\"  Running OCR...\");\n        List<PageResult> ocrResults = new ArrayList<>(pages.size());\n        for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {\n            long ts = System.nanoTime();\n            ocrResults.add(ocrEngine.ocr(pages.get(i)));\n            double elapsed = (System.nanoTime() - ts) / 1e9;\n            System.out.printf(\"    Page %3d: %.1fs%n\", i + 1, elapsed);\n        }\n        long t4 = System.nanoTime();\n        System.out.printf(\"    OCR done in %.1fs%n\", (t4 - t3) / 1e9);\n\n        // Phase 4: Assemble searchable PDF with foreground mask overlay\n        System.out.println(\"  Assembling PDF...\");\n        try (var doc = assembler.assemble(inputFile, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults)) {\n            doc.save(outputFile);\n        }\n        long t5 = System.nanoTime();\n        System.out.printf(\"    Assembly done in %.1fs%n\", (t5 - t4) / 1e9);\n    }"
}
```

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Now I need to modify PDFAssembler to use JPEG for backgrounds when foregroundMasks is null (non-MRC mode). Currently it always uses `LosslessFactory.createFromImage` which is lossless PNG. For non-MRC mode, JPEG would be much more compact and appropriate for photographic/page images.

PDFBox has `JPEGFactory.createFromImage` for JPEG compression. Let me use that when no foreground masks are provided.

</details>

**Input:**

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Wait, if `useMrc` is false, `t3` won't be initialized but it's used in `(t4 - (useMrc ? t3 : t2))`. If useMrc is false, the ternary evaluates to `t2`, so `t3` isn't accessed. But Java still requires it to be definitely assigned. Let me initialize it.

</details>

All changes done. Here's a summary:

### `tests/generate_eval_corpus.py`
- Added `--searchable` flag — embeds invisible text layer via PyMuPDF `TextWriter` with `render_mode=3`
- Default (no flag): image-only PDFs (current behavior for OCR eval)
- With `--searchable`: same PDFs but with selectable/searchable text (for MRC re-OCR benchmarks)

### Pipeline (`TrulyFreeOCR.java`)
- Added `--no-mrc` CLI flag (overrides `pipeline.mrc.enabled` in settings)
- MRC **enabled** (default): extract → segment (background PNG + foreground JBIG2) → OCR → assemble with MRC
- MRC **disabled** (`--no-mrc`): extract → OCR → assemble (original image JPEG + text layer only, no compression)

### `PDFAssembler.java`
- Non-MRC mode: uses `JPEGFactory.createFromImage` (85% quality, compact) for backgrounds
- MRC mode: uses `LosslessFactory.createFromImage` (PNG, preserves cleaned background)

### `settings.jsonc`
- Added `pipeline.mrc.enabled` setting with documentation

### Usage
```bash
# MRC mode (default)
./jdk/bin/java -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar \
  --settings settings.jsonc input.pdf -o output.pdf

# Non-MRC mode (baseline for comparison)
./jdk/bin/java -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar --no-mrc \
  --settings settings.jsonc input.pdf -o output.pdf
```

You're right — PyMuPDF is AGPL. The current script already uses it for PDF creation, but I shouldn't add more PyMuPDF code. Let me **revert the `--searchable` flag** and explain the alternative.

The searchable input isn't actually needed for MRC evaluation. Here's why:

**MRC benchmark without searchable input:**
1. `--no-mrc` on image-only corpus → output A (no compression, JPEG baseline)
2. MRC enabled (default) on image-only corpus → output B (with JBIG2 foreground)
3. Compare sizes of A vs B → that's your MRC effectiveness measurement

Both modes start from the same image-only input and produce searchable output. The difference is whether the output uses JBIG2 foreground compression + cleaned background (MRC) or just raw JPEG images + text layer (no MRC).

For text rendering with word wrapping:

```java
FontMetrics fm = g.getFontMetrics(font);
int lineHeight = fm.getHeight(); // or use 17 as in Python
int maxWidth = 612 - 2 * 50; // 512

// Word wrap
List<String> wrapText(String text, FontMetrics fm, int maxWidth) {
    String[] words = text.split(" ");
    List<String> lines = new ArrayList<>();
    StringBuilder current = new StringBuilder(words[0]);
    for (int i = 1; i < words.length; i++) {
        String candidate = current + " " + words[i];
        if (fm.stringWidth(candidate) <= maxWidth) {
            current = new StringBuilder(candidate);
        } else {
            lines.add(current.toString());
            current = new StringBuilder(words[i]);
        }
    }
    lines.add(current.toString());
    return lines;
}
```

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.eval;
2: 
3: import java.io.IOException;
4: import java.nio.file.Files;
5: import java.nio.file.Path;
6: import java.util.ArrayList;
7: import java.util.List;
8: 
9: /**
10:  * Reads the ground-truth JSON files produced by generate_eval_corpus.py.
11:  *
12:  * Format:
13:  *   {"source":"...","title":"...","pages":[{"page":1,"words":["a","b",...]},...]}
14:  */
15: public class GroundTruth {
16: 
17:     private final String source;
18:     private final String title;
19:     private final List<List<String>> pages; // page index → list of words
20: 
21:     public GroundTruth(String source, String title, List<List<String>> pages) {
22:         this.source = source;
23:         this.title = title;
24:         this.pages = pages;
25:     }
26: 
27:     public String getSource() { return source; }
28:     public String getTitle() { return title; }
29:     public int getPageCount() { return pages.size(); }
30:     public List<String> getWords(int page) { return pages.get(page); }
31: 
32:     public static GroundTruth load(Path jsonPath) throws IOException {
33:         String raw = Files.readString(jsonPath);
34:         Parser p = new Parser(raw);
35:         return p.parse();
36:     }
37: 
38:     // ── minimal recursive-descent JSON parser ──────────────────────────
39: 
40:     private static class Parser {
41:         private final String s;
42:         private int pos;
43: 
44:         Parser(String s) { this.s = s; this.pos = 0; }
45: 
46:         GroundTruth parse() {
47:             expect('{');
48:             String src = null, title = null;
49:             List<List<String>> pages = null;
50:             skipWs();
51: 
52:             while (pos < s.length() && s.charAt(pos) != '}') {
53:                 String key = parseString();
54:                 skipWs();
55:                 expect(':');
56:                 skipWs();
57:                 switch (key) {
58:                     case "source" -> src = parseString();
59:                     case "title" -> title = parseString();
60:                     case "pages" -> pages = parsePages();
61:                     default -> skipValue();
62:                 }
63:                 skipWs();
64:                 skip(',');
65:                 skipWs();
66:             }
67:             return new GroundTruth(src, title, pages);
68:         }
69: 
70:         private List<List<String>> parsePages() {
71:             List<List<String>> result = new ArrayList<>();
72:             expect('[');
73:             skipWs();
74:             while (pos < s.length() && s.charAt(pos) != ']') {
75:                 result.add(parsePage());
76:                 skipWs();
77:                 skip(',');
78:                 skipWs();
79:             }
80:             expect(']');
81:             return result;
82:         }
83: 
84:         private List<String> parsePage() {
85:             List<String> words = null;
86:             expect('{');
87:             skipWs();
88:             while (pos < s.length() && s.charAt(pos) != '}') {
89:                 String key = parseString();
90:                 skipWs();
91:                 expect(':');
92:                 skipWs();
93:                 if (key.equals("words")) {
94:                     words = parseStringArray();
95:                 } else {
96:                     skipValue();
97:                 }
98:                 skipWs();
99:                 skip(',');
100:                 skipWs();
101:             }
102:             expect('}');
103:             return words;
104:         }
105: 
106:         private List<String> parseStringArray() {
107:             List<String> result = new ArrayList<>();
108:             expect('[');
109:             skipWs();
110:             while (pos < s.length() && s.charAt(pos) != ']') {
111:                 result.add(parseString());
112:                 skipWs();
113:                 skip(',');
114:                 skipWs();
115:             }
116:             expect(']');
117:             return result;
118:         }
119: 
120:         private String parseString() {
121:             skipWs();
122:             expect('"');
123:             StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
124:             while (pos < s.length() && s.charAt(pos) != '"') {
125:                 if (s.charAt(pos) == '\\') {
126:                     pos++;
127:                     if (pos < s.length()) sb.append(s.charAt(pos++));
128:                 } else {
129:                     sb.append(s.charAt(pos++));
130:                 }
131:             }
132:             expect('"');
133:             return sb.toString();
134:         }
135: 
136:         private void skipValue() {
137:             skipWs();
138:             if (pos >= s.length()) return;
139:             char c = s.charAt(pos);
140:             if (c == '"') { parseString(); return; }
141:             if (c == '[' || c == '{') {
142:                 int depth = 1;
143:                 pos++;
144:                 while (pos < s.length() && depth > 0) {
145:                     if (s.charAt(pos) == '"') { parseString(); continue; }
146:                     if (s.charAt(pos) == '[' || s.charAt(pos) == '{') depth++;
147:                     if (s.charAt(pos) == ']' || s.charAt(pos) == '}') depth--;
148:                     pos++;
149:                 }
150:                 return;
151:             }
152:             while (pos < s.length() && s.charAt(pos) != ',' && s.charAt(pos) != '}' && s.charAt(pos) != ']') pos++;
153:         }
154: 
155:         private void skip(char c) {
156:             skipWs();
157:             if (pos < s.length() && s.charAt(pos) == c) pos++;
158:         }
159: 
160:         private void expect(char c) {
161:             skipWs();
162:             if (pos >= s.length() || s.charAt(pos) != c)
163:                 throw new RuntimeException("Expected '" + c + "' at pos " + pos + " got '" + (pos < s.length() ? s.charAt(pos) : "EOF") + "'");
164:             pos++;
165:         }
166: 
167:         private void skipWs() {
168:             while (pos < s.length() && Character.isWhitespace(s.charAt(pos))) pos++;
169:         }
170:     }
171: }

(End of file - total 171 lines)
</content>
```

```
72:             List<String> gtWords = normalize(gt.getWords(i));

81:             double precision = computePrecision(ocrWords, gtWords);
82:             double meanConf = result.getTextBlocks().stream()

84: 
85:             String flag = pageWer > WER_THRESHOLD ? " ***" : "";

90:

92:

95:         List<String> allOcr = new ArrayList<>();
96:         int totalGtWords = 0, totalOcrWords = 0;
97:         double sumConf = 0;
98:         int confCount = 0;
99:         int flaggedPages = 0;
100: 
101:         for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
102:             List<String> gw = normalize(gt.getWords(i));
103:             List<String> ow = normalize(results.get(i).getTextBlocks().stream()
104:                     .map(tb -> tb.getWord()).collect(Collectors.toList()));
105:             allGt.addAll(gw);
106:             allOcr.addAll(ow);
107:             totalGtWords += gw.size();
108:             totalOcrWords += ow.size();
109:             for (var tb : results.get(i).getTextBlocks()) {
110:                 sumConf += tb.getConfidence();
111:                 confCount++;
112:             }
113: 
114:             double pageWer = Levenshtein.wer(ow, gw);
115:             if (pageWer > WER_THRESHOLD) flaggedPages++;
116:         }
117: 
118:         String gtFull = String.join(" ", allGt);
119:         String ocrFull = String.join(" ", allOcr);
120: 
121:         double aggregateWer = Levenshtein.wer(allOcr, allGt);
122:         double aggregateCer = Levenshtein.cer(ocrFull, gtFull);
123:         double aggregateRecall = computeRecall(allOcr, allGt);
124:         double aggregatePrecision = computePrecision(allOcr, allGt);
125:         double meanConf = confCount > 0 ? sumConf / confCount : 0;
126: 
127:         if (flaggedPages > 0) {
128:             System.out.println("\n  Pages with WER > " + (WER_THRESHOLD * 100) + "%: " + flaggedPages + " / " + n);
129:         } else {
130:             System.out.println("\n  Pages with WER > " + (WER_THRESHOLD * 100) + "%: none");
131:         }
132: 
133:         System.out.println("\n  Document summary:");

135:         System.out.printf("    Total GT words:       %d%n", totalGtWords);
136:         System.out.printf("    Total OCR words:      %d%n", totalOcrWords);
137:         System.out.printf("    Aggregate WER:        %.1f%%%n", aggregateWer * 100);
138:         System.out.printf("    Aggregate CER:        %.1f%%%n", aggregateCer * 100);
139:         System.out.printf("    Word recall:          %.1f%%%n", aggregateRecall * 100);
140:         System.out.printf("    Word precision:       %.1f%%%n", aggregatePrecision * 100);
141:         System.out.printf("    Mean confidence:      %.1f%n", meanConf);
142:         System.out.printf("    Total time:           %.1fs%n", totalTime / 1000.0);
143:         System.out.printf("    Time per page:        %.2fs%n", totalTime / 1000.0 / n);
144:     }

148:     private List<String> normalize(List<String> words) {
149:         return words.stream()
150:                 .map(w -> w.replaceAll("^[^a-zA-Z0-9]+", ""))
151:                 .map(w -> w.replaceAll("[^a-zA-Z0-9]+$", ""))
152:                 .map(String::toLowerCase)

156: 
157:     private double computeRecall(List<String> ocrWords, List<String> gtWords) {
158:         if (gtWords.isEmpty()) return 1.0;
159:         long found = gtWords.stream().filter(w -> ocrWords.contains(w)).count();
160:         return (double) found / gtWords.size();
161:     }
162:

164:         if (ocrWords.isEmpty()) return 1.0;
165:         long found = ocrWords.stream().filter(w -> gtWords.contains(w)).count();
166:         return (double) found / ocrWords.size();
167:     }
168: }
```

Looking at the Python code more carefully:
- `clean_line(l)` cleans each line
- Lines are rendered as-is using `render_page(draw, font, [line], ...)` 
- There's no word wrapping per line — each cleaned line is rendered on one text line

So the Java version just needs to:
1. Split text by "\n"
2. Clean each line
3. For non-empty lines, render them at (MARGIN, MARGIN + line_index * LINE_HEIGHT)
4. Split the line into words for ground truth

This is simpler than I thought. Each "line" from the source text becomes exactly one rendered line (no additional wrapping).

Actually wait, that means the Python code doesn't word-wrap at all! Each line is rendered as-is. But the Gutenberg text has lines that are already broken at ~70 characters. So the rendering just places each line at the correct y position.

```java
        // Get font
        Font font = loadFont();
        // Generate
        for (int pc : PAGE_COUNTS) {
            generatePdf(lines, pc, font);
        }
        return 0;

}

 @Option(names = \"--force\", description = \"Regenerate existing files\")\n    private boolean force;\n\n    private Font font;\n\n    @Override\n    public Integer call() {\n        try {\n lines/page\");\n\n            for (int pc : PAGE_COUNTS) {\n                String stem = OUTPUT_STEM + \"-\" + String.format(\"%03d\", pc) + \"p\";\n                Path pdfPath = OUT_DIR.resolve(stem + \".pdf\");\n                if (Files.exists(pdfPath) && !force) {\n                    System.out.println(\"  \" + stem + \" exists, skipping (use --force to regenerate)\");\n                    continue;\n                }\n                if (pc > totalPages) {\n                    System.out.println(\"  Warning: requested \" + pc\n                            + \" pages but corpus has only ~\" + totalPages + \"; truncating\");\n                }\n                generate(lines, Math.min(pc, totalPages), stem);\n            }\n\n            System.out.println(\"Done.\");\n            return 0;\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            System.err.println(\"Error: \" + e.getMessage());\n            e.printStackTrace();\n            return 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── Text download & cleanup ──────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    private String downloadText(String urlStr) throws IOException {\n        URL url = new URL(urlStr);\n        StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();\n        try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(\n                new InputStreamReader(url.openStream(), StandardCharsets.UTF_8))) {\n            String line;\n            while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null) {\n                sb.append(line).append(\"\\n\");\n            }\n        }\n        return sb.toString();\n    }\n\n    private String stripGutenberg(String text) {\n        var m = HEADER_PATTERN.matcher(text);\n        if (m.find()) {\n            text = text.substring(m.end());\n        }\n        m = FOOTER_PATTERN.matcher(text);\n        if (m.find()) {\n            text = text.substring(0, m.start());\n        }\n        return text.strip();\n    }\n\n    private List<String> cleanLines(String text) {\n        List<String> result = new ArrayList<>();\n        for (String line : text.split(\"\\n\")) {\n            line = line.strip();\n            if (line.isEmpty()) continue;\n            if (DASH_LINE.matcher(line).find()) continue;\n            if (ILLUSTRATION.matcher(line).find()) continue;\n            result.add(line);\n        }\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    // ── Font loading ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    private Font loadFont() {\n        // Try DejaVu Sans from standard system path, fall back to SansSerif\n        File ttf = new File(\"/usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu/DejaVuSans.ttf\");\n        if (ttf.exists()) {\n            try {\n                return Font.createFont(Font.TRUETYPE_FONT, ttf).deriveFont((float) FONT_SZ);\n            } catch (Exception e) {\n                // fall through\n            }\n        }\n        return new Font(\"SansSerif\", Font.PLAIN, FONT_SZ);\n    }\n\n    // ── PDF + JSON generation ────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    private void generate(List<String> lines, int pageCount, String stem)\n            throws IOException {\n\n        // Partition lines into pages, collecting ground-truth words per page\n        List<List<String>> pageWords = new ArrayList<>();\n        List<List<String>> pageLines = new ArrayList<>();\n\n        List<String> currentLines = new ArrayList<>();\n        List<String> currentWords = new ArrayList<>();\n\n        for (String line : lines) {\n            currentLines.add(line);\n            if (!line.isEmpty()) {\n                for (String w : line.split(\"\\\\s+\")) {\n                    if (!w.isEmpty()) currentWords.add(w);\n                }\n            }\n            if (currentLines.size() >= MAX_LINES) {\n                pageWords.add(currentWords);\n                pageLines.add(currentLines);\n                currentLines = new ArrayList<>();\n                currentWords = new ArrayList<>();\n                if (pageWords.size() >= pageCount) break;\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (!currentLines.isEmpty() && pageWords.size() < pageCount) {\n            pageWords.add(currentWords);\n            pageLines.add(currentLines);\n        }\n\n        // Truncate\n        while (pageWords.size() > pageCount) {\n            pageWords.remove(pageWords.size() - 1);\n            pageLines.remove(pageLines.size() - 1);\n        }\n\n        // Build PDF\n        Path pdfPath = OUT_DIR.resolve(stem + \".pdf\");\n        try (PDDocument doc = new PDDocument()) {\n            for (int i = 0; i < pageLines.size(); i++) {\n                BufferedImage img = renderPage(pageLines.get(i));\n                PDImageXObject pdImage = jpegFromImage(img);\n                PDPage pdPage = new PDPage(new PDRectangle(W, H));\n                doc.addPage(pdPage);\n                try (PDPageContentStream cs = new PDPageContentStream(doc, pdPage)) {\n                    cs.drawImage(pdImage, 0, 0, W, H);\n                }\n            }\n            doc.save(pdfPath.toFile());\n        }\n        int wordCount = pageWords.stream().mapToInt(List::size).sum();\n        System.out.println(\"    \" + pdfPath + \" (\" + pageWords.size()\n                + \" pages, \" + wordCount + \" words)\");\n\n        // Write JSON\n        writeJson(stem, pageWords);\n    }\n\n    private BufferedImage renderPage(List<String> lines) {\n        BufferedImage img = new BufferedImage(W, H, BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_RGB);\n        Graphics2D g = img.createGraphics();\n        g.setColor(Color.WHITE);\n        g.fillRect(0, 0, W, H);\n        g.setColor(Color.BLACK);\n        g.setFont(font);\n        g.setRenderingHint(RenderingHints.KEY_TEXT_ANTIALIASING,\n                RenderingHints.VALUE_TEXT_ANTIALIAS_ON);\n\n        int y = MARGIN;\n        for (String line : lines) {\n            if (!line.isEmpty()) {\n                g.drawString(line, MARGIN, y);\n            }\n            y += LINE_HEIGHT;\n        }\n        g.dispose();\n        return img;\n    }\n\n    private PDImageXObject jpegFromImage(BufferedImage img) throws IOException {\n        ByteArrayOutputStream baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream();\n        ImageIO.write(img, \"JPEG\", baos);\n        baos.close();\n        ByteArrayInputStream bais = new ByteArrayInputStream(baos.toByteArray());\n        return PDImageXObject.createFromFileByExtension(\n                new File(\"dummy.jpg\"), Loader.loadPDF(bais.readAllBytes()));\n    }\n\n    private PDImageXObject createPdImage(PDDocument doc, BufferedImage img)\n            throws IOException {\n        ByteArrayOutputStream baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream();\n        ImageIO.write(img, \"JPEG\", baos);\n        baos.close();\n        // PDFBox JPEGFactory would be cleaner, but requires a PDDocument reference\n        // that we already have as `doc`.\n        return org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.JPEGFactory\n                .createFromImage(doc, img, 0.95f);\n    }\n\n    // ── JSON writer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    private void writeJson(String stem, List<List<String>> pageWords)\n            throws IOException {\n        Path jsonPath = OUT_DIR.resolve(stem + \".json\");\n        try (Writer w = new OutputStreamWriter(\n                Files.newOutputStream(jsonPath), StandardCharsets.UTF_8)) {\n            w.write(\"{\\n\");\n            w.write(\"  \\\"source\\\": \\\"\" + escapeJson(GUTENBERG_URL) + \"\\\",\\n\");\n            w.write(\"  \\\"title\\\": \\\"\" + escapeJson(TITLE) + \"\\\",\\n\");\n            w.write(\"  \\\"pages\\\": [\\n\");\n            for (int i = 0; i < pageWords.size(); i++) {\n                w.write(\"    {\\\"page\\\": \" + (i + 1) + \", \\\"words\\\": [\");\n                List<String> words = pageWords.get(i);\n                for (int j = 0; j < words.size(); j++) {\n                    if (j > 0) w.write(\", \");\n                    w.write(\"\\\"\" + escapeJson(words.get(j)) + \"\\\"\");\n                }\n                w.write(\"]}\");\n                if (i < pageWords.size() - 1) w.write(\",\");\n                w.write(\"\\n\");\n            }\n            w.write(\"  ]\\n\");\n            w.write(\"}\\n\");\n        }\n        System.out.println(\"    \" + jsonPath);\n    }\n\n    private String escapeJson(String s) {\n        return s.replace(\"\\\\\", \"\\\\\\\\\")\n                .replace(\"\\\"\", \"\\\\\\\"\")\n                .replace(\"\\n\", \"\\\\n\")\n                .replace(\"\\r\", \"\\\\r\")\n                .replace(\"\\t\", \"\\\\t\");\n    }\n\n    // ── main ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    public static void main(String[] args) {\n        int exitCode = new CommandLine(new CorpusGenerator()).execute(args);\n        System.exit(exitCode);\n    }\n}\n"
}
```

```
<path>/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/tests/generate_test_pdfs.py</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env python3
2: """Generate test PDFs for TrulyFreeOCR test-driven development.
3: 
4: Each PDF is image-based (non-searchable) to simulate scanned documents.
5: Creates files in tests/test-files/. Run from project root.
6: """
7: 
8: import io
9: import os
10: from PIL import Image, ImageDraw, ImageFont
11: import fitz  # PyMuPDF
12: 
13: OUT_DIR = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "test-files")
14: os.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
15: 
16: FONT_DIR = "/usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu"
17: W, H = 612, 792  # US Letter at 72 DPI
18: MARGIN = 50
19: FONT_SZ = 14
20: 
21: def font(name="DejaVuSans.ttf", sz=FONT_SZ):
22:     return ImageFont.truetype(os.path.join(FONT_DIR, name), sz)
23: 
24: def new_page():
25:     img = Image.new("RGB", (W, H), "white")
26:     draw = ImageDraw.Draw(img)
27:     return img, draw
28: 
29: def save_image_pdf(pages, path):
30:     doc = fitz.open()
31:     for img in pages:
32:         buf = io.BytesIO()
33:         img.save(buf, format="JPEG", quality=95)
34:         buf.seek(0)
35:         pix = fitz.Pixmap(buf.read())
36:         page = doc.new_page(width=W, height=H)
37:         page.insert_image(fitz.Rect(0, 0, W, H), pixmap=pix)
38:     doc.save(path)
39:     doc.close()
40: 
41: 
42: # ── 1. blank.pdf ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43: def make_blank():
44:     img, _ = new_page()
45:     save_image_pdf([img], os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "blank.pdf"))
46:     print("  blank.pdf")
47: 
48: # ── 2. simple-text.pdf ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
49: def make_simple_text():
50:     img, d = new_page()
51:     fnt = font()
52:     lines = [
53:         "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.",
54:         "Pack my box with five dozen liquor jugs.",
55:         "Sphinx of black quartz, judge my vow.",
56:         "",
57:         "This is a test document for OCR validation.",
58:         "It contains English text rendered as an image,",
59:         "simulating a scanned page that is not searchable.",
60:         "",
61:         "The pipeline should extract this text via Tesseract,",
62:         "then embed it as invisible text over the image.",
63:         "The output PDF should be searchable while retaining",
64:         "the original visual appearance.",
65:     ]
66:     y = MARGIN
67:     for line in lines:
68:         d.text((MARGIN, y), line, fill="black", font=fnt)
69:         y += 22
70:     save_image_pdf([img], os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "simple-text.pdf"))
71:     print("  simple-text.pdf")
72: 
73: # ── 3. multi-page.pdf ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
74: def make_multi_page():
75:     pages = []
76:     page_data = [
77:         ["Page 1: Introduction",
78:          "This is the first page of a multi-page document.",
79:          "It contains introductory text about the topic.",
80:          "Each page has different content to test that",
81:          "the OCR pipeline processes all pages correctly."],
82:         ["Page 2: Methodology",
83:          "The approach uses a 6-step pipeline:",
84:          "1. Page extraction via PDFBox at 300 DPI",
85:          "2. Image segmentation via Leptonica (lept4j)",
86:          "3. JBIG2 compression of text mask",
87:          "4. OCR via Tesseract (Tess4J)",
88:          "5. PDF re-assembly with searchable text",
89:          "6. Metadata preservation (bookmarks, annotations)"],
90:         ["Page 3: Results",
91:          "The output is a searchable, highly-compressed PDF",
92:          "that maintains the original visual appearance",
93:          "while adding a hidden text layer for searching.",
94:          "All metadata from the source PDF is preserved."],
95:     ]
96:     for title, *body in page_data:
97:         img, d = new_page()
98:         d.text((MARGIN, MARGIN), title, fill="black",
99:                font=font("DejaVuSans.ttf", 18))
100:         y = MARGIN + 40
101:         for line in body:
102:             d.text((MARGIN, y), line, fill="black", font=font())
103:             y += 22
104:         pages.append(img)
105:     save_image_pdf(pages, os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "multi-page.pdf"))
106:     print("  multi-page.pdf")
107: 
108: # ── 4. two-column.pdf ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
109: def make_two_column():
110:     img, d = new_page()
111:     col_w = (W - 3 * MARGIN) // 2
112:     x1, x2 = MARGIN, MARGIN * 2 + col_w
113:     fnt = font()
114:     body = (
115:         "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing "
116:         "elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et "
117:         "dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis "
118:         "nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut aliquip "
119:         "ex ea commodo consequat. Duis aute irure dolor in "
120:         "reprehenderit in voluptate velit esse cillum dolore "
121:         "eu fugiat nulla pariatur. Excepteur sint occaecat "
122:         "cupidatat non proident, sunt in culpa qui officia "
123:         "deserunt mollit anim id est laborum."
124:     )
125:     # Wrap into ~40-char lines
126:     words = body.split()
127:     lines = []
128:     for i in range(0, len(words), 8):
129:         lines.append(" ".join(words[i:i+8]))
130: 
131:     # Left column
132:     d.text((x1, MARGIN), "Left Column", fill="black",
133:            font=font("DejaVuSans.ttf", 16))
134:     y = MARGIN + 30
135:     for line in lines:
136:         d.text((x1, y), line, fill="black", font=fnt)
137:         y += 18
138: 
139:     # Right column
140:     d.text((x2, MARGIN), "Right Column", fill="black",
141:            font=font("DejaVuSans.ttf", 16))
142:     y = MARGIN + 30
143:     for line in lines:
144:         d.text((x2, y), line, fill="black", font=fnt)
145:         y += 18
146: 
147:     save_image_pdf([img], os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "two-column.pdf"))
148:     print("  two-column.pdf")
149: 
150: # ── 5. with-annotations.pdf ─────────────────────────────────────────────
151: def make_with_annotations():
152:     pages = []
153:     # Create multi-page document with bookmarks
154:     titles = ["Chapter 1: Getting Started",
155:               "Chapter 2: Configuration",
156:               "Chapter 3: Usage"]
157:     for i, title in enumerate(titles, 1):
158:         img, d = new_page()
159:         d.text((MARGIN, MARGIN), title, fill="black",
160:                font=font("DejaVuSans.ttf", 18))
161:         d.text((MARGIN, MARGIN + 40),
162:                f"This is page {i} of the annotated document.",
163:                fill="black", font=font())
164:         d.text((MARGIN, MARGIN + 70),
165:                "This PDF includes bookmarks and annotations.",
166:                fill="black", font=font())
167:         pages.append(img)
168: 
169:     doc = fitz.open()
170:     for i, img in enumerate(pages):
171:         buf = io.BytesIO()
172:         img.save(buf, format="JPEG", quality=95)
173:         buf.seek(0)
174:         pix = fitz.Pixmap(buf.read())
175:         page = doc.new_page(width=W, height=H)
176:         page.insert_image(fitz.Rect(0, 0, W, H), pixmap=pix)
177: 
178:         # Add text annotation on first page
179:         if i == 0:
180:             annot = page.add_text_annot(
181:                 (100, 100), "This is a sticky note annotation."
182:             )
183: 
184:         # Add underline annotation on first page
185:         if i == 0:
186:             page.add_underline_annot(
187:                 fitz.Rect(MARGIN, MARGIN, MARGIN + 200, MARGIN + 20)
188:             )
189: 
190:     # Add bookmarks (outline)
191:     toc = [
192:         [1, "Chapter 1", 1],
193:         [1, "Chapter 2", 2],
194:         [1, "Chapter 3", 3],
195:     ]
196:     doc.set_toc(toc)
197: 
198:     doc.save(os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "with-annotations.pdf"))
199:     doc.close()
200:     print("  with-annotations.pdf")
201: 
202: # ── 6. noisy-scan.pdf ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
203: def make_noisy_scan():
204:     import random
205:     random.seed(42)
206:     img, d = new_page()
207:     fnt = font()
208: 
209:     lines = [
210:         "This page simulates a noisy scanned document.",
211:         "It includes speckles, uneven lighting, and",
212:         "slight rotation to test image preprocessing.",
213:     ]
214:     y = MARGIN
215:     for line in lines:
216:         d.text((MARGIN, y), line, fill="black", font=fnt)
217:         y += 22
218: 
219:     # Add noise
220:     pixels = img.load()
221:     for _ in range(3000):
222:         x = random.randint(0, W - 1)
223:         y = random.randint(0, H - 1)
224:         v = random.randint(0, 60)
225:         pixels[x, y] = (v, v, v)
226: 
227:     # Add a gradient (simulating uneven lighting)
228:     for y in range(H):
229:         shade = int(30 * (y / H))
230:         for x in range(W):
231:             r, g, b = pixels[x, y]
232:             pixels[x, y] = (
233:                 max(0, r - shade),
234:                 max(0, g - shade),
235:                 max(0, b - shade),
236:             )
237: 
238:     save_image_pdf([img], os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "noisy-scan.pdf"))
239:     print("  noisy-scan.pdf")
240: 
241: 
242: if __name__ == "__main__":
243:     print("Generating test PDFs...")
244:     make_blank()
245:     make_simple_text()
246:     make_multi_page()
247:     make_two_column()
248:     make_with_annotations()
249:     make_noisy_scan()
250:     print(f"\nDone. Files in {OUT_DIR}/")

(End of file - total 250 lines)
```

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

OK, so `tests/generate_test_pdfs.py` generates 6 PDFs:
1. `blank.pdf` — blank page
2. `simple-text.pdf` — single page with pangrams
3. `multi-page.pdf` — 3 pages with different content
4. `two-column.pdf` — 2-column layout
5. `with-annotations.pdf` — 3 pages with annotations and bookmarks
6. `noisy-scan.pdf` — page with added noise and gradient

These are used by unit tests across multiple test classes (ImageSegmenterTest, OCREngineTest, PageExtractorTest, PDFAssemblerTest, MetadataPreserverTest, PipelineIntegrationTest).

The complex ones are:
- `with-annotations.pdf` uses PyMuPDF annotations (text annotations, underline annotations, table of contents/bookmarks)
- `noisy-scan.pdf` adds pixel noise and gradient to the image
- `two-column.pdf` renders two columns of text

For PDF annotations/bookmarks, PDFBox can create them but it's more complex. Let me check what PDFBox supports:
- Text annotations: PDFBox can create `PDTextAnnotation`
- Underline annotations: PDFBox can create `PDAnnotationTextMarkup` with subtype underline
- Bookmarks/outline: PDFBox supports `PDDocumentOutline` and `PDPageFitWidthDestination`

This is a significant amount of work but straightforward. Let me create `TestPdfGenerator.java` in `src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/eval/` or maybe a better location. Actually, since it's for unit tests, I'll put it in `src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/` or a new package. Let me put it in a `util` package or just in the test root.

Actually, let me think about where to put it. The existing Python script is in `tests/` directory (outside Java source). The Java equivalent should be somewhere in the test sources. Let me put it in `src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/TestPdfGenerator.java` — simple, no package confusion.

Let me read the PipelineIntegrationTest to understand what tests need which PDFs, especially the annotations test.

</details>

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;
4: 
5: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
6: import java.io.File;
7: import java.io.IOException;
8: import java.util.ArrayList;
9: import java.util.List;
10: 
11: import org.apache.pdfbox.Loader;
12: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;
13: import org.apache.pdfbox.text.PDFTextStripper;
14: import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;
15: import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
16: 
17: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;
18: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.SegmentedImage;
19: 
20: /**
21:  * End-to-end integration tests running the full pipeline
22:  * (PageExtractor → ImageSegmenter → OCREngine → PDFAssembler)
23:  * on all sample PDFs and verifying the output.
24:  */
25: class PipelineIntegrationTest {
26: 
27:     static PageExtractor extractor;
28:     static ImageSegmenter segmenter;
29:     static OCREngine engine;
30:     static PDFAssembler assembler;
31: 
32:     @BeforeAll
33:     static void setup() {
34:         extractor = new PageExtractor();
35:         segmenter = new ImageSegmenter();
36:         engine = new OCREngine();
37:         assembler = new PDFAssembler();
38:     }
39: 
40:     @Test
41:     void fullPipeline_simpleText_outputIsSearchable() throws IOException {
42:         runPipeline("simple-text.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
43:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
44:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
45:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("quick"),
46:                     "Output should contain 'quick' from the sample text");
47:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("brown"),
48:                     "Output should contain 'brown' from the sample text");
49:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("fox"),
50:                     "Output should contain 'fox' from the sample text");
51:         });
52:     }
53: 
54:     @Test
55:     void fullPipeline_blankPage_outputHasNoSearchableText() throws IOException {
56:         runPipeline("blank.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
57:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
58:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
59:             assertTrue(text.trim().isEmpty(),
60:                     "Blank page should have no searchable text, but got: '" + text + "'");
61:         });
62:     }
63: 
64:     @Test
65:     void fullPipeline_multiPage_preservesPageCount() throws IOException {
66:         runPipeline("multi-page.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
67:             assertEquals(3, output.getNumberOfPages(),
68:                     "Three-page input should produce three-page output");
69:         });
70:     }
71: 
72:     @Test
73:     void fullPipeline_multiPage_eachPageHasText() throws IOException {
74:         runPipeline("multi-page.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
75:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
76:             for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
77:                 stripper.setStartPage(i + 1);
78:                 stripper.setEndPage(i + 1);
79:                 String text = stripper.getText(output);
80:                 assertFalse(text.trim().isEmpty(),
81:                         "Page " + (i + 1) + " should have extractable text");
82:             }
83:         });
84:     }
85: 
86:     @Test
87:     void fullPipeline_withAnnotations_preservesMetadata() throws IOException {
88:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/with-annotations.pdf");
89:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
90:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
91:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
92:         var foregroundMasks = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getForegroundMask).toList();
93:         var ocrResults = processOcr(pages);
94: 
95:         try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(input);
96:              PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults)) {
97: 
98:             // Verify document info preserved
99:             var srcInfo = source.getDocumentInformation();
100:             var dstInfo = output.getDocumentInformation();
101:             assertEquals(srcInfo.getTitle(), dstInfo.getTitle());
102:             assertEquals(srcInfo.getAuthor(), dstInfo.getAuthor());
103: 
104:             // Verify outline preserved if source has one
105:             var srcOutline = source.getDocumentCatalog().getDocumentOutline();
106:             if (srcOutline != null) {
107:                 var dstOutline = output.getDocumentCatalog().getDocumentOutline();
108:                 assertNotNull(dstOutline);
109:             }
110:         }
111:     }
112: 
113:     @Test
114:     void fullPipeline_twoColumn_outputIsSearchable() throws IOException {
115:         runPipeline("two-column.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
116:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
117:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
118:             assertFalse(text.trim().isEmpty(), "Two-column PDF should have searchable text");
119:             // Should contain content from both columns
120:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("lorem") || text.toLowerCase().contains("ipsum"),
121:                     "Two-column output should contain sample text");
122:         });
123:     }
124: 
125:     @Test
126:     void fullPipeline_simpleText_withForegroundMask() throws IOException {
127:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf");
128:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
129:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
130:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
131:         var foregroundMasks = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getForegroundMask).toList();
132:         var ocrResults = processOcr(pages);
133: 
134:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults)) {
135:             assertNotNull(output);
136:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
137:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
138:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("quick"));
139:         }
140:     }
141: 
142:     @Test
143:     void fullPipeline_outputFileSizeIsReasonable() throws IOException {
144:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf");
145:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
146:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
147:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
148:         var foregroundMasks = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getForegroundMask).toList();
149:         var ocrResults = processOcr(pages);
150: 
151:         File tempOutput = File.createTempFile("tfocr-integration-", ".pdf");
152:         tempOutput.deleteOnExit();
153:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults)) {
154:             output.save(tempOutput);
155:         }
156:         // Output should be reasonably sized (not trivially tiny, not enormous)
157:         long size = tempOutput.length();
158:         assertTrue(size > 1000, "Output PDF should be at least 1KB, was " + size + " bytes");
159:         // At 300 DPI for US Letter, even compressed, expect > 10KB
160:         assertTrue(size < 100_000_000, "Output PDF should be under 100MB, was " + size + " bytes");
161:     }
162: 
163:     // ── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
164: 
165:     @FunctionalInterface
166:     private interface OutputValidator {
167:         void validate(PDDocument output, File sourceFile) throws IOException;
168:     }
169: 
170:     private void runPipeline(String testPdfName, OutputValidator validator) throws IOException {
171:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/" + testPdfName);
172:         assertTrue(input.exists(), "Test PDF not found: " + input);
173: 
174:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
175:         assertNotNull(pages);
176:         assertFalse(pages.isEmpty());
177: 
178:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
179:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
180:         var ocrResults = processOcr(pages);
181: 
182:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {
183:             assertNotNull(output);
184:             validator.validate(output, input);
185:         }
186:     }
187: 
188:     private List<PageResult> processOcr(List<BufferedImage> pages) {
189:         return pages.stream().map(p -> {
190:             try { return engine.ocr(p); }
191:             catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
192:         }).toList();
193:     }
194: }

(End of file - total 194 lines)
```
