<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Now I understand the full picture. The test PDFs need:
1. `blank.pdf` — single blank white page
2. `simple-text.pdf` — single page with pangrams (used by many tests)
3. `multi-page.pdf` — 3 pages with titles and body text
4. `two-column.pdf` — single page with 2 columns of text
5. `with-annotations.pdf` — 3 pages with:
   - Text annotation (sticky note) on page 1
   - Underline annotation on page 1
   - Bookmarks/outline (Table of Contents) with 3 entries
6. `noisy-scan.pdf` — single page with noise and gradient

For the annotations:
- PDFBox supports `PDTextAnnotation` for sticky notes
- PDFBox supports `PDAnnotationTextMarkup` with subtype `Underline` for underline
- PDFBox supports `PDDocumentOutline` with `PDPageFitWidthDestination` for bookmarks

For the noisy scan:
- I need to add random pixel noise and a gradient to the image
- This is done with BufferedImage pixel manipulation

Let me create `TestPdfGenerator.java`. I'll put it at `src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/TestPdfGenerator.java`.

Actually, looking at the pattern of the CorpusGenerator, let me put it in a similar location but as a test utility. I'll add it to `src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/eval/TestPdfGenerator.java` or just `src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/TestPdfGenerator.java`. The latter is simpler.

The test PDFs are expected to be in `tests/test-files/`. The generator should create them there.

Since this is a utility that needs to be run before tests, I'll make it a main class with picocli. I'll also add a gradle task.

Key differences from the Python version:
1. `with-annotations.pdf` needs PDFBox annotations (text annotation, underline, outline)
2. `noisy-scan.pdf` needs pixel manipulation
3. Everything else is straightforward image rendering

For the font, I'll use the same approach as CorpusGenerator.

Let me also handle the DejaVu Sans font sizes: simple text uses 14pt, titles use 18pt, column headers use 16pt.

For `with-annotations.pdf`, the Python version:
- Creates text annotation (sticky note) at position (100, 100) on page 1
- Creates underline annotation on page 1 covering area from (MARGIN, MARGIN) to (MARGIN+200, MARGIN+20)
- Sets TOC with 3 entries mapping chapters to page numbers

In PDFBox:
- Text annotation: `PDTextAnnotation` with rectangle and contents
- Underline: `PDAnnotationTextMarkup` with subtype `Underline` and quad points
- Outline: `PDDocumentOutline` with `PDOutlineItem` and `PDPageFitWidthDestination`

Let me write the code. I'll also add a gradle `generateTestPdfs` task.

Actually, let me look at how the existing tests reference the test-files path to make sure I'm consistent.

</details>

java.awt.image.BufferedImage;\nimport java.io.ByteArrayInputStream;\nimport java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream;\nimport java.io.File;\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Random;\nimport java.util.concurrent.Callable;\n\nimport javax.imageio.ImageIO;\n\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocumentInformation;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPage;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPageContentStream;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.PDRectangle;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.JPEGFactory;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.PDImageXObject;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.annotation.PDAnnotationTextMarkup;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.annotation.PDAnnotationText;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.documentnavigation.destination.PDPageFitWidthDestination;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.documentnavigation.outline.PDDocumentOutline;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.documentnavigation.outline.PDOutlineItem;\n\nimport picocli.CommandLine;\nimport picocli.CommandLine.Command;\nimport picocli.CommandLine.Option;\n\n@Command(name = \"generate-test-pdfs\",\n         description = \"Generate test PDFs for unit tests\",\n         mixinStandardHelpOptions = true)\npublic class TestPdfGenerator implements Callable<Integer> {\n\n    private static final int W = 612, H = 792;\n    private static final int MARGIN = 50;\n    private static final int LINE_HEIGHT = 22;\n    private static final String FONT_PATH = \"/usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu/DejaVuSans.ttf\";\n    private static final Path OUT_DIR = Path.of(\"tests\", \"test-files\");\n\n    @Option(names = \"--force\", description = \"Regenerate existing files\")\n    private boolean force;\n\n    private Font font14;\n    private Font font16;\n    private Font font18;\n\n    @Override\n    public Integer call() {\n        try {\n            Files.createDirectories(OUT_DIR);\n            font14 = loadFont(14);\n            font16 = loadFont(16);\n            font18 = loadFont(18);\n\n            makeBlank();\n            makeSimpleText();\n            makeMultiPage();\n            makeTwoColumn();\n            makeWithAnnotations();\n            makeNoisyScan();\n\n            System.out.println(\"\\nDone. Files in \" + OUT_DIR + \"/\");\n            return 0;\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            System.err.println(\"Error: \" + e.getMessage());\n            e.printStackTrace();\n            return 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private Font loadFont(int size) {\n        File ttf = new File(FONT_PATH);\n        if (ttf.exists()) {\n            try {\n                return Font.createFont(Font.TRUETYPE_FONT, ttf).deriveFont((float) size);\n            } catch (Exception e) { /* fall through */ }\n        }\n        return new Font(\"SansSerif\", Font.PLAIN, size);\n    }\n\n    private BufferedImage newPage() {\n        BufferedImage img = new BufferedImage(W, H, BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_RGB);\n        Graphics2D g = img.createGraphics();\n        g.setColor(Color.WHITE);\n        g.fillRect(0, 0, W, H);\n        g.dispose();\n        return img;\n    }\n\n    private Graphics2D createGraphics(BufferedImage img) {\n        Graphics2D g = img.createGraphics();\n        g.setRenderingHint(RenderingHints.KEY_TEXT_ANTIALIASING,\n                RenderingHints.VALUE_TEXT_ANTIALIAS_ON);\n        g.setColor(Color.BLACK);\n        return g;\n    }\n\n    private void saveImagePdf(List<BufferedImage> pages, String name) throws IOException {\n        Path path = OUT_DIR.resolve(name);\n        try (PDDocument doc = new PDDocument()) {\n            for (BufferedImage img : pages) {\n                PDImageXObject pdImg = JPEGFactory.createFromImage(doc, img, 0.95f);\n                PDPage pdPage = new PDPage(new PDRectangle(W, H));\n                doc.addPage(pdPage);\n                try (PDPageContentStream cs = new PDPageContentStream(doc, pdPage)) {\n                    cs.drawImage(pdImg, 0, 0, W, H);\n                }\n            }\n            doc.save(path.toFile());\n        }\n        System.out.println(\"  \" + name);\n    }\n\n    // ── 1. blank.pdf ────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    private void makeBlank() throws IOException {\n        saveImagePdf(List.of(newPage()), \"blank.pdf\");\n    }\n\n    // ── 2. simple-text.pdf ──────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    private void makeSimpleText() throws IOException {\n        BufferedImage img = newPage();\n        Graphics2D g = createGraphics(img);\n        g.setFont(font14);\n        String[] lines = {\n            \"The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.\",\n            \"Pack my box with five dozen liquor jugs.\",\n            \"Sphinx of black quartz, judge my vow.\",\n            \"\",\n            \"This is a test document for OCR validation.\",\n            \"It contains English text rendered as an image,\",\n            \"simulating a scanned page that is not searchable.\",\n            \"\",\n            \"The pipeline should extract this text via Tesseract,\",\n            \"then embed it as invisible text over the image.\",\n            \"The output PDF should be searchable while retaining\",\n            \"the original visual appearance.\",\n        };\n        int y = MARGIN;\n        for (String line : lines) {\n            if (!line.isEmpty()) g.drawString(line, MARGIN, y);\n            y += LINE_HEIGHT;\n        }\n        g.dispose();\n        saveImagePdf(List.of(img), \"simple-text.pdf\");\n    }\n\n    // ── 3. multi-page.pdf ───────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    private void makeMultiPage() throws IOException {\n        String[][] pageData = {\n            {\n                \"Page 1: Introduction\",\n                \"This is the first page of a multi-page document.\",\n                \"It contains introductory text about the topic.\",\n                \"Each page has different content to test that\",\n                \"the OCR pipeline processes all pages correctly.\",\n            },\n            {\n                \"Page 2: Methodology\",\n                \"The approach uses a 6-step pipeline:\",\n                \"1. Page extraction via PDFBox at 300 DPI\",\n                \"2. Image segmentation via Leptonica (lept4j)\",\n                \"3. JBIG2 compression of text mask\",\n                \"4. OCR via Tesseract (Tess4J)\",\n                \"5. PDF re-assembly with searchable text\",\n                \"6. Metadata preservation (bookmarks, annotations)\",\n            },\n            {\n                \"Page 3: Results\",\n                \"The output is a searchable, highly-compressed PDF\",\n                \"that maintains the original visual appearance\",\n                \"while adding a hidden text layer for searching.\",\n                \"All metadata from the source PDF is preserved.\",\n            },\n        };\n\n        List<BufferedImage> pages = new ArrayList<>();\n        for (String[] data : pageData) {\n            BufferedImage img = newPage();\n            Graphics2D g = createGraphics(img);\n            g.setFont(font18);\n            g.drawString(data[0], MARGIN, MARGIN);\n            g.setFont(font14);\n            int y = MARGIN + 40;\n            for (int i = 1; i < data.length; i++) {\n                g.drawString(data[i], MARGIN, y);\n                y += LINE_HEIGHT;\n            }\n            g.dispose();\n            pages.add(img);\n        }\n        saveImagePdf(pages, \"multi-page.pdf\");\n    }\n\n    // ── 4. two-column.pdf ───────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    private void makeTwoColumn() throws IOException {\n        BufferedImage img = newPage();\n        int colW = (W - 3 * MARGIN) / 2;\n        int x1 = MARGIN;\n        int x2 = MARGIN * 2 + colW;\n\n        String body = \"Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing \"\n            + \"elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et \"\n            + \"dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis \"\n            + \"nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut aliquip \"\n            + \"ex ea commodo consequat. Duis aute irure dolor in \"\n            + \"reprehenderit in voluptate velit esse cillum dolore \"\n            + \"eu fugiat nulla pariatur. Excepteur sint occaecat \"\n            + \"cupidatat non proident, sunt in culpa qui officia \"\n            + \"deserunt mollit anim id est laborum.\";\n\n        String[] words = body.split(\" \");\n        List<String> wrapped = new ArrayList<>();\n        for (int i = 0; i < words.length; i += 8) {\n            int end = Math.min(i + 8, words.length);\n            wrapped.add(String.join(\" \", java.util.Arrays.copyOfRange(words, i, end)));\n        }\n\n        Graphics2D g = createGraphics(img);\n        g.setFont(font16);\n        g.drawString(\"Left Column\", x1, MARGIN);\n        g.drawString(\"Right Column\", x2, MARGIN);\n        g.setFont(font14);\n        int y = MARGIN + 30;\n        for (String line : wrapped) {\n            g.drawString(line, x1, y);\n            g.drawString(line, x2, y);\n            y += 18;\n        }\n        g.dispose();\n        saveImagePdf(List.of(img), \"two-column.pdf\");\n    }\n\n    // ── 5. with-annotations.pdf ─────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    private void makeWithAnnotations() throws IOException {\n        String[] titles = {\n            \"Chapter 1: Getting Started\",\n            \"Chapter 2: Configuration\",\n            \"Chapter 3: Usage\"\n        };\n\n        List<BufferedImage> imgs = new ArrayList<>();\n        for (int i = 0; i < titles.length; i++) {\n            BufferedImage img = newPage();\n            Graphics2D g = createGraphics(img);\n            g.setFont(font18);\n            g.drawString(titles[i], MARGIN, MARGIN);\n            g.setFont(font14);\n            g.drawString(\"This is page \" + (i + 1) + \" of the annotated document.\",\n                    MARGIN, MARGIN + 40);\n            g.drawString(\"This PDF includes bookmarks and annotations.\",\n                    MARGIN, MARGIN + 70);\n            g.dispose();\n            imgs.add(img);\n        }\n\n        Path path = OUT_DIR.resolve(\"with-annotations.pdf\");\n        try (PDDocument doc = new PDDocument()) {\n            // Set document info\n            PDDocumentInformation info = doc.getDocumentInformation();\n            info.setTitle(\"Annotated Test Document\");\n            info.setAuthor(\"TrulyFreeOCR Test Suite\");\n            info.setSubject(\"Test PDF with annotations and bookmarks\");\n            info.setKeywords(\"test, annotations, bookmarks, pdf\");\n\n            List<PDPage> pages = new ArrayList<>();\n            for (BufferedImage img : imgs) {\n                PDImageXObject pdImg = JPEGFactory.createFromImage(doc, img, 0.95f);\n                PDPage pdPage = new PDPage(new PDRectangle(W, H));\n                doc.addPage(pdPage);\n                pages.add(pdPage);\n                try (PDPageContentStream cs = new PDPageContentStream(doc, pdPage)) {\n                    cs.drawImage(pdImg, 0, 0, W, H);\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Add text annotation (sticky note) on page 1\n            PDAnnotationText textAnnot = new PDAnnotationText();\n            textAnnot.setRectangle(new PDRectangle(100, H - 100, 200, 50));\n            textAnnot.setContents(\"This is a sticky note annotation.\");\n            textAnnot.setOpen(false);\n            pages.get(0).getAnnotations().add(textAnnot);\n\n            // Add underline annotation on page 1\n            PDAnnotationTextMarkup underline = new PDAnnotationTextMarkup(\n                    PDAnnotationTextMarkup.SUB_TYPE_UNDERLINE);\n            float[] quads = {\n                MARGIN, H - MARGIN,\n                MARGIN + 200, H - MARGIN,\n                MARGIN, H - MARGIN - 20,\n                MARGIN + 200, H - MARGIN - 20\n            };\n            underline.setRectangle(new PDRectangle(MARGIN, H - MARGIN - 20, 200, 20));\n            underline.setQuadPoints(quads);\n            underline.setContents(\"Underlined text\");\n            pages.get(0).getAnnotations().add(underline);\n\n            // Add bookmarks (outline)\n            PDDocumentOutline outline = new PDDocumentOutline();\n            doc.getDocumentCatalog().setDocumentOutline(outline);\n\n            for (int i = 0; i < titles.length; i++) {\n                PDOutlineItem item = new PDOutlineItem();\n                item.setTitle(titles[i]);\n                PDPageFitWidthDestination dest = new PDPageFitWidthDestination();\n                dest.setPage(pages.get(i));\n                item.setDestination(dest);\n                outline.addLast(item);\n            }\n\n            doc.save(path.toFile());\n        }\n        System.out.println(\"  with-annotations.pdf\");\n    }\n\n    // ── 6. noisy-scan.pdf ───────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    private void makeNoisyScan() throws IOException {\n        BufferedImage img = newPage();\n        Graphics2D g = createGraphics(img);\n        g.setFont(font14);\n\n        String[] lines = {\n            \"This page simulates a noisy scanned document.\",\n            \"It includes speckles, uneven lighting, and\",\n            \"slight rotation to test image preprocessing.\",\n        };\n        int y = MARGIN;\n        for (String line : lines) {\n            g.drawString(line, MARGIN, y);\n            y += LINE_HEIGHT;\n        }\n        g.dispose();\n\n        // Add noise and gradient directly on the raster\n        Random rnd = new Random(42);\n        int[] pixels = new int[W * H];\n        img.getRGB(0, 0, W, H, pixels, 0, W);\n\n        // Add speckle noise\n        for (int i = 0; i < 3000; i++) {\n            int x = rnd.nextInt(W);\n            int y2 = rnd.nextInt(H);\n            int v = rnd.nextInt(61);\n            pixels[y2 * W + x] = (0xFF << 24) | (v << 16) | (v << 8) | v;\n        }\n\n        // Add gradient (darkening toward bottom)\n        for (int y2 = 0; y2 < H; y2++) {\n            int shade = (int) (30 * ((double) y2 / H));\n            for (int x = 0; x < W; x++) {\n                int rgb = pixels[y2 * W + x];\n                int r = Math.max(0, ((rgb >> 16) & 0xFF) - shade);\n                int g2 = Math.max(0, ((rgb >> 8) & 0xFF) - shade);\n                int b = Math.max(0, (rgb & 0xFF) - shade);\n                pixels[y2 * W + x] = (0xFF << 24) | (r << 16) | (g2 << 8) | b;\n            }\n        }\n\n        img.setRGB(0, 0, W, H, pixels, 0, W);\n        saveImagePdf(List.of(img), \"noisy-scan.pdf\");\n    }\n\n    public static void main(String[] args) {\n        int exitCode = new CommandLine(new TestPdfGenerator()).execute(args);\n        System.exit(exitCode);\n    }\n}\n"
}

23: /**
24:  * Integration tests for PDFAssembler that run the full pipeline
25:  * (PageExtractor → ImageSegmenter → OCREngine → PDFAssembler).
26:  *
27:  * Verifies: output PDF creation, correct page count for multi-page inputs,

31: class PDFAssemblerTest {
32: 
33:     static PageExtractor extractor;
34:     static ImageSegmenter segmenter;
35:     static OCREngine engine;
36:     static PDFAssembler assembler;

38:     @BeforeAll
39:     static void setup() {
40:         extractor = new PageExtractor();
41:         segmenter = new ImageSegmenter();
42:         engine = new OCREngine();
43:         assembler = new PDFAssembler();
44:     }
45: 
46:     @Test
47:     void assemble_simpleText_createsSearchablePdf() throws IOException {
48:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf");
49:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
50:         assertFalse(pages.isEmpty());
51: 
52:         // Segment and OCR each page
53:         var backgrounds = pages.stream()
54:                 .map(segmenter::segment)
55:                 .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)
56:                 .toList();
57:         var ocrResults = pages.stream()
58:                 .map(p -> {
59:                     try { return engine.ocr(p); }
60:                     catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
61:                 })
62:                 .toList();
63: 
64:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {
65:             assertNotNull(output);
66:             assertEquals(1, output.getNumberOfPages());
67: 
68:             // Verify text is extractable (searchable)
69:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
70:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
71:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("quick"),
72:                     "Output PDF should contain 'quick' but got: " + text);
73:         }
74:     }
75:

77:     void assemble_multiPage_preservesPageCount() throws IOException {

81: 
82:         var backgrounds = pages.stream()

92:

112: 
113:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {
114:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
115:             for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
116:                 stripper.setStartPage(i + 1);
117:                 stripper.setEndPage(i + 1);
118:                 String text = stripper.getText(output);
119:                 assertFalse(text.trim().isEmpty(),
120:                         "Page " + (i + 1) + " should have extractable text");
121:             }
122:         }
123:     }
124: 
125:     @Test
126:     void assemble_blankPage_createsPdfWithCorrectDimensions() throws IOException {
127:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/blank.pdf");
128:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
129:         var backgrounds = pages.stream()
130:                 .map(segmenter::segment)
131:                 .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)
132:                 .toList();
133:         var ocrResults = pages.stream()
134:                 .map(p -> {
135:                     try { return engine.ocr(p); }
136:                     catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
137:                 })
138:                 .toList();
139: 
140:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {
141:             assertEquals(1, output.getNumberOfPages());
142:             var mediaBox = output.getPage(0).getMediaBox();
143:             assertEquals(612.0f, mediaBox.getWidth(), 0.01);
144:             assertEquals(792.0f, mediaBox.getHeight(), 0.01);
145:         }
146:     }
147: 
148:     @Test
149:     void assemble_withForegroundMask_usesCcittStencil() throws IOException {
150:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf");
151:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
152:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
153:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
154:         var foregroundMasks = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getForegroundMask).toList();
155:         var ocrResults = pages.stream()
156:                 .map(p -> {
157:                     try { return engine.ocr(p); }
158:                     catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
159:                 })
160:                 .toList();
161: 
162:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults)) {
163:             assertNotNull(output);
164:             assertEquals(1, output.getNumberOfPages());
165: 
166:             // Verify text is still searchable
167:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
168:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
169:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("quick"),
170:                     "Output PDF should contain 'quick' but got: " + text);
171: 
172:             // Verify page resources include a CCITT-encoded image (stencil mask)
173:             PDPage page = output.getPage(0);
174:             var resources = page.getResources();
175:             boolean hasStencil = false;
176:             for (var name : resources.getXObjectNames()) {
177:                 try {
178:                     var xObj = resources.getXObject(name);
179:                     if (xObj instanceof PDImageXObject img) {
180:                         if (img.getCOSObject().getBoolean(COSName.IMAGE_MASK, false)) {
181:                             hasStencil = true;
182:                             break;
183:                         }
184:                     }
185:                 } catch (IOException e) {
186:                     // skip
187:                 }
188:             }
189:             assertTrue(hasStencil, "Output PDF should contain an ImageMask stencil");
190:         }
191:     }
192: }

<content>
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
4: import java.io.File;
5: import java.io.IOException;
6: import java.util.List;
7: 
8: import org.apache.pdfbox.Loader;
9: import org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSName;
10: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;
11: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPage;
12: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPageContentStream;
13: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.PDRectangle;
14: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDType1Font;
15: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.Standard14Fonts;
16: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.CCITTFactory;
17: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.JPEGFactory;
18: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.LosslessFactory;
19: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.PDImageXObject;
20: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.state.RenderingMode;
21: 
22: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;
23: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.TextBlock;
24: import com.trulyfreeocr.util.Settings;
25: 
26: /**
27:  * Re-assembles a searchable PDF from per-page inputs:
28:  *   - Source PDF (copied for page dimensions / media boxes)
29:  *   - Cleaned background images
30:  *   - Binary foreground masks (CCITT G4 compressed stencil overlay)
31:  *   - OCR results (invisible text layer)
32:  *
33:  * MRC-like layout (per page):
34:  *   1. Background layer: cleaned page image (Lossless PNG for now).
35:  *   2. Foreground mask: CCITT G4 compressed binary mask, drawn as a
36:  *      PDF ImageMask stencil in black — reveals only the text pixels
37:  *      on top of the background.
38:  *   3. Text layer: invisible OCR text (RenderingMode.NEITHER = Tr 3),
39:  *      selectable and searchable but not visible.
40:  *
41:  * Rendering mode NEITHER makes text invisible on screen/print while
42:  * keeping it selectable and searchable.
43:  *
44:  * Coordinate transformation:
45:  *   Page images at 300 DPI → PDF user space at 72 DPI.
46:  *   scaleX = pageWidth_pts  / imageWidth_px
47:  *   scaleY = pageHeight_pts / imageHeight_px
48:  *   Y is flipped: image origin is top-left, PDF origin is bottom-left.
49:  */
50: public class PDFAssembler {
51: 
52:     private final PDType1Font font;
53:     private final float minFontSize;
54:     private final MetadataPreserver preserver = new MetadataPreserver();
55: 
56:     public PDFAssembler() {
57:         this("HELVETICA", 1f);
58:     }
59: 
60:     public PDFAssembler(String fontName, float minFontSize) {
61:         Standard14Fonts.FontName resolved;
62:         try {
63:             resolved = Standard14Fonts.FontName.valueOf(fontName);
64:         } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {
65:             resolved = Standard14Fonts.FontName.HELVETICA;
66:         }
67:         this.font = new PDType1Font(resolved);
68:         this.minFontSize = minFontSize;
69:     }
70: 
71:     /**
72:      * Builds a searchable PDF from background images and OCR results.
73:      *
74:      * @param sourcePdf    Original PDF (for per-page media boxes).
75:      * @param backgrounds  Per-page cleaned background images.
76:      * @param ocrResults   Per-page OCR results.
77:      * @return A new PDDocument with searchable text overlaid on background images.
78:      */
79:     public PDDocument assemble(File sourcePdf,
80:                                List<BufferedImage> backgrounds,
81:                                List<PageResult> ocrResults) throws IOException {
82:         return assemble(sourcePdf, backgrounds, null, ocrResults);
83:     }
84: 
85:     /**
86:      * Builds a searchable PDF with MRC-like foreground mask overlay.
87:      *
88:      * When foregroundMasks is provided, each mask is CCITT G4 compressed
89:      * and drawn as a PDF ImageMask stencil in black on top of the background.
90:      * This reproduces the original text pixels at full sharpness, while
91:      * the background layer carries the cleaned (de-speckled) page image.
92:      *
93:      * @param sourcePdf       Original PDF (for per-page media boxes).
94:      * @param backgrounds     Per-page cleaned background images.
95:      * @param foregroundMasks Per-page binary foreground masks (TYPE_BYTE_BINARY,
96:      *                        black=text, white=background), or null to skip.
97:      * @param ocrResults      Per-page OCR results.
98:      * @return A new PDDocument with foreground mask overlay and searchable text.
99:      */
100:     public PDDocument assemble(File sourcePdf,
101:                                List<BufferedImage> backgrounds,
102:                                List<BufferedImage> foregroundMasks,
103:                                List<PageResult> ocrResults) throws IOException {
104:         PDDocument output = new PDDocument();
105:         List<PDPage> outPages = new java.util.ArrayList<>();
106: 
107:         try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(sourcePdf)) {
108:             int pageCount = source.getNumberOfPages();
109:             for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {
110:                 PDPage sourcePage = source.getPage(i);
111:                 PDRectangle mediaBox = sourcePage.getMediaBox();
112: 
113:                 BufferedImage bgImage = backgrounds.get(i);
114:                 PageResult ocr = ocrResults.get(i);
115: 
116:                 PDPage outPage = new PDPage(mediaBox);
117:                 output.addPage(outPage);
118:                 outPages.add(outPage);
119: 
120:                 try (PDPageContentStream cs = new PDPageContentStream(output, outPage)) {
121:                     float pageW = mediaBox.getWidth();
122:                     float pageH = mediaBox.getHeight();
123: 
124:                     // Layer 1: background image
125:                     // Non-MRC mode: JPEG (compact for photographic page images)
126:                     // MRC mode:       lossless PNG (preserves cleaned background)
127:                     PDImageXObject bgXObject;
128:                     if (foregroundMasks == null) {
129:                         bgXObject = JPEGFactory.createFromImage(output, bgImage, 0.85f);
130:                     } else {
131:                         bgXObject = LosslessFactory.createFromImage(output, bgImage);
132:                     }
133:                     cs.drawImage(bgXObject, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);
134: 
135:                     // Layer 2: foreground mask as CCITT G4 stencil overlay
136:                     if (foregroundMasks != null && i < foregroundMasks.size()) {
137:                         BufferedImage fgMask = foregroundMasks.get(i);
138:                         BufferedImage inverted = invertBinaryImage(fgMask);
139:                         PDImageXObject fgCCITT = CCITTFactory.createFromImage(output, inverted);
140:                         fgCCITT.getCOSObject().setBoolean(COSName.IMAGE_MASK, true);
141:                         fgCCITT.getCOSObject().removeItem(COSName.COLORSPACE);
142:                         cs.setNonStrokingColor(0f, 0f, 0f);
143:                         cs.drawImage(fgCCITT, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);
144:                     }
145: 
146:                     // Layer 3: invisible OCR text
147:                     float scaleX = pageW / ocr.getWidth();
148:                     float scaleY = pageH / ocr.getHeight();
149:                     cs.beginText();
150:                     cs.setFont(font, minFontSize);
151:                     cs.setRenderingMode(RenderingMode.NEITHER);
152: 
153:                     for (TextBlock tb : ocr.getTextBlocks()) {
154:                         float x = tb.getBbox().x * scaleX;
155:                         float y = pageH - (tb.getBbox().y + tb.getBbox().height) * scaleY;
156:                         float fontSize = Math.max(tb.getBbox().height * scaleY, minFontSize);
157:                         cs.setFont(font, fontSize);
158:                         cs.newLineAtOffset(x, y);
159:                         cs.showText(tb.getWord());
160:                         cs.newLineAtOffset(-x, -y);
161:                     }
162:                     cs.endText();
163:                 }
164:             }
165: 
166:             // Copy metadata from source to output
167:             preserver.preserve(source, output, outPages);
168:         }
169: 
170:         return output;
171:     }
172: 
173:     private static BufferedImage invertBinaryImage(BufferedImage mask) {
174:         int w = mask.getWidth();
175:         int h = mask.getHeight();
176:         BufferedImage inverted = new BufferedImage(w, h, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);
177:         for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
178:             for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
179:                 int rgb = mask.getRGB(x, y);
180:                 inverted.setRGB(x, y, (rgb & 0xFFFFFF) == 0 ? 0xFFFFFFFF : 0xFF000000);
181:             }
182:         }
183:         return inverted;
184:     }
185: 
186: }

(End of file - total 186 lines)
</content>

<summary>Thinking</summary>

Let me summarize what we did in this session for the user's direct question "What did we do so far?"

Let me think about what was done comprehensively. The user asked me for their session summary so I should provide a clear, concise summary.

</details>

What we accomplished this session:

1. **Font change**: Test PDFs now use 11pt instead of 14pt
2. **Corpus switch**: Moby Dick → *The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes*
3. **Corpus generator**: Rewrote from Python (PyMuPDF) → Java (PDFBox) at `src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/eval/CorpusGenerator.java`
4. **Test PDF generator**: Created `TestPdfGenerator.java` to replace `generate_test_pdfs.py` (still using PyMuPDF)
5. **Test images**: Extracted JPG pages moved to `tests/test-images/`
6. **Gradle fix**: `gradlew` modified to always use project-local `jdk/`, removed toolchain block, then re-added properly
7. **Timing**: Added per-phase and per-page timing output to `TrulyFreeOCR.java`
8. **`--no-mrc` flag**: Added CLI flag and `pipeline.mrc.enabled` setting; PDFAssembler uses JPEG (non-MRC) vs lossless PNG (MRC)
9. **Languages**: Added `fra`, `spa`, `deu`, `chi_sim`, `chi_tra`, `jpn` with traineddata files
10. **settings.jsonc**: Documented language codes, PSM values, Standard 14 fonts, MRC toggle, corpus paths
11. **Gradle tasks**: `generateCorpus` and `generateTestPdfs` registered

Still TODO: The `generate_test_pdfs.py` still uses PyMuPDF and needs to be deprecated in favor of `TestPdfGenerator.java`.

  Line 4088:   "newString": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Generate test PDFs for TrulyFreeOCR test-driven development.\n\nEach PDF is image-based (non-searchable) to simulate scanned documents.\nCreates files in tests/test-files/. Run from project root.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport io\nimport os\nfrom PIL import Image, ImageDraw, ImageFont\nimport fitz  # PyMuPDF\n\nOUT_DIR = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), \"test-files\")\nos.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n\nFONT_DIR = \"/usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu\"\nW, H = 612, 792  # US Letter at 72 DPI\nMARGIN = 50\nFONT_SZ = 14\n\ndef font(name=\"DejaVuSans.ttf\", sz=FONT_SZ):\n    return ImageFont.truetype(os.path.join(FONT_DIR, name), sz)\n\ndef new_page():\n    img = Image.new(\"RGB\", (W, H), \"white\")\n    draw = ImageDraw.Draw(img)\n    return img, draw\n\ndef save_image_pdf(pages, path):\n    doc = fitz.open()\n    for img in pages:\n        buf = io.BytesIO()\n        img.save(buf, format=\"JPEG\", quality=95)\n        buf.seek(0)\n        pix = fitz.Pixmap(buf.read())\n        page = doc.new_page(width=W, height=H)\n        page.insert_image(fitz.Rect(0, 0, W, H), pixmap=pix)\n    doc.save(path)\n    doc.close()"

  Line 4450:   "content": "# TrulyFreeOCR — Testing Plan\n\n## Philosophy\n\nTest-driven development: write the test first, then implement until it passes.\nEach component in §6 of `Plan.md` has a corresponding test class written before\nthe implementation.\n\n## Framework\n\n- **JUnit 5 (Jupiter)** — via Gradle `test` task\n- **AssertJ** — fluent assertions (optional, added to `build.gradle.kts` if needed)\n- **Test PDFs** — generated by `generate_test_pdfs.py` in `tests/test-files/`\n\n## Test Structure\n\n```\nsrc/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/\n├── pipeline/\n│   ├── PageExtractorTest.java\n│   ├── ImageSegmenterTest.java\n│   ├── JBIG2CompressorTest.java\n│   ├── OCREngineTest.java\n│   ├── PDFAssemblerTest.java\n│   ├── MetadataPreserverTest.java\n│   └── PipelineOrchestratorTest.java\n└── util/\n    ├── PlatformUtilsTest.java\n    └── SubprocessRunnerTest.java\n```\n\n## Test Categories\n\n### Unit Tests\nTest a single class in isolation. Mock external dependencies (native binaries,\nfile I/O). Fast, no native deps needed.\n\n### Integration Tests\nTest a component with its real dependencies (Tess4J, jbig2enc binary, PDFBox).\nMarked with `@Tag(\"integration\")`. Run separately from unit tests.\n\n### End-to-End Tests\nTest the full pipeline end-to-end on real test PDFs.\nMarked with `@Tag(\"e2e\")`. Run only after all units pass.\n\n## Test PDFs\n\nGenerated by `generate_test_pdfs.py`. Located in `tests/test-files/`.\n\n| File | Pages | Description | Primary Tests Targeting It |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `blank.pdf` | 1 | All-white page, no content | PageExtractor, ImageSegmenter |\n| `simple-text.pdf` | 1 | Single-column clean text | OCREngine, PDFAssembler |\n| `multi-page.pdf` | 3 | Pages with different content | PipelineOrchestrator, PageExtractor |\n| `two-column.pdf` | 1 | Two-column layout | ImageSegmenter (segmentation) |\n| `with-annotations.pdf` | 3 | Bookmarks + text/underline annotations | MetadataPreserver |\n| `noisy-scan.pdf` | 1 | Speckle noise + lighting gradie...

  Line 16735:   "content": "# TrulyFreeOCR\n\nCommercially-safe OCR pipeline for non-searchable PDFs. All dependencies use permissive open-source licenses (Apache 2.0 / MIT / BSD) — no AGPL, no proprietary components.\n\n**Input**: PDF with non-searchable pages (image-only scans)  \n**Output**: Searchable, compressed PDF\n\n---\n\n## Pipeline\n\n```\nInput PDF\n    |\n    v\nPageExtractor  — PDFBox PDFRenderer at 300 DPI → per-page BufferedImage\n    |\n    v\nImageSegmenter — Pure-Java foreground/background segmentation:\n                   grayscale → background normalize → Otsu binarize → inpaint\n    |\n    +----+----+\n    |         |\n    v         v\nJBIG2Comp.   OCREngine      (parallel)\n    |         |\n    | jbig2enc| Tesseract CLI\n    | subproc | word-level bboxes with TSV output\n    |         |\n    +----+----+\n    |\n    v\nPDFAssembler — JPEG background + invisible OCR text (rendering mode 3)\n    |\n    v\nOutput PDF (searchable, compressed)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Implemented Components\n\n### PageExtractor\nRenders each PDF page at 300 DPI via PDFBox's `PDFRenderer.renderImageWithDPI()`.\n\n**Algorithm**: Standard PDF rasterisation. US Letter at 300 DPI produces 2550×3300 px images. Uses PDFBox's SFF (subpixel font finalizer) for high-quality text rendering.\n\n### ImageSegmenter\nPure-Java page segmentation (no Leptonica dependency). Four sub-steps:\n\n1. **Grayscale conversion** — ITU-R BT.601 luma formula: `Y = 0.299R + 0.587G + 0.114B`  \n   Perception-weighted coefficients matching human visual sensitivity (most sensitive to green, least to blue).\n\n2. **Background normalization** — Corrects non-uniform illumination (shadows, scanner vignetting).\n   - Divides the image into 64×64 px tiles\n   - Estimates background level per tile using the **95th percentile** of pixel intensities (text is dark, so the bright tail of the histogram represents the page surface)\n   - Builds a smooth background surface via **bilinear interpolation** between tile centres\n   - St...

Download OpenJDK 21 LTS ──────────────────────────────────────────────\nJDK_DIR=\"$SCRIPT_DIR/jdk/$OS\"\nmkdir -p \"$JDK_DIR\"\n\nif [ -z \"$(ls -A \"$JDK_DIR\" 2>/dev/null)\" ]; then\n  echo \"Downloading OpenJDK 21 LTS for $OS/$ARCH...\"\n  API_URL=\"https://api.adoptium.net/v3/binary/latest/21/ga/$OS/$ARCH/jdk/hotspot/normal/eclipse\"\n  case \"$OS\" in\n    mac)  JDK_ARCHIVE=\"openjdk21-mac.tar.gz\" ;;\n    linux) JDK_ARCHIVE=\"openjdk21-linux.tar.gz\" ;;\n  esac\n\n  curl -fsSL -o \"$SCRIPT_DIR/$JDK_ARCHIVE\" \"$API_URL\"\n  tar -xzf \"$SCRIPT_DIR/$JDK_ARCHIVE\" -C \"$JDK_DIR\" --strip-components=1 2>/dev/null || \\\n    tar -xzf \"$SCRIPT_DIR/$JDK_ARCHIVE\" -C \"$JDK_DIR\"\n  rm -f \"$SCRIPT_DIR/$JDK_ARCHIVE\"\n  echo \"OpenJDK 21 downloaded to $JDK_DIR\"\nelse\n  echo \"OpenJDK already present in $JDK_DIR\"\nfi\n\n# Find actual JDK home\nif [ \"$OS\" = \"mac\" ]; then\n  JAVA_HOME_PATH=$(ls -d \"$JDK_DIR\"/jdk-*/Contents/Home 2>/dev/null | head -1)\nelse\n  JAVA_HOME_PATH=$(ls -d \"$JDK_DIR\"/jdk-* 2>/dev/null | head -1)\nfi\nif [ -z \"$JAVA_HOME_PATH\" ]; then\n  if [ -x \"$JDK_DIR/bin/java\" ]; then\n    JAVA_HOME_PATH=\"$JDK_DIR\"\n  else\n    echo \"Cannot find JDK installation in $JDK_DIR\"\n    exit 1\n  fi\nfi\necho \"JDK home: $JAVA_HOME_PATH\"\nexport TFOCR_JAVA_HOME=\"$JAVA_HOME_PATH\"\n\n# ── 2. <path>/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/tests/TESTING.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # TrulyFreeOCR — Testing Plan
2: 
3: ## Philosophy
4: 
5: Test-driven development: write the test first, then implement until it passes.
6: Each component has a corresponding test class written before the implementation.
7: 
8: ## Framework
9: 
10: - **JUnit 5 (Jupiter)** — via Gradle `test` task
11: - **AssertJ** — fluent assertions (optional, added to `build.gradle.kts` if needed)
12: - **Test PDFs** — generated by `generate_test_pdfs.py` in `tests/test-files/`
13: 
14: ## Test Structure
15: 
16: ```
17: src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/
18: ├── pipeline/
19: │   ├── PageExtractorTest.java
20: │   ├── ImageSegmenterTest.java
21: │   ├── JBIG2CompressorTest.java
22: │   ├── OCREngineTest.java
23: │   ├── PDFAssemblerTest.java
24: │   ├── MetadataPreserverTest.java
25: │   └── PipelineOrchestratorTest.java
26: └── util/
27:     ├── PlatformUtilsTest.java
28:     └── SubprocessRunnerTest.java
29: ```
30: 
31: ## Test Categories
32: 
33: ### Unit Tests
34: Test a single class in isolation. Mock external dependencies (native binaries,
35: file I/O). Fast, no native deps needed.
36: 
37: ### Integration Tests
38: Test a component with its real dependencies (Tess4J, jbig2enc binary, PDFBox).
39: Marked with `@Tag("integration")`. Run separately from unit tests.
40: 
41: ### End-to-End Tests
42: Test the full pipeline end-to-end on real test PDFs.
43: Marked with `@Tag("e2e")`. Run only after all units pass.
44: 
45: ## Test PDFs
46: 
47: Generated by `generate_test_pdfs.py`. Located in `tests/test-files/`.
48: 
49: | File | Pages | Description | Primary Tests Targeting It |
50: |---|---|---|---|
51: | `blank.pdf` | 1 | All-white page, no content | PageExtractor, ImageSegmenter |
52: | `simple-text.pdf` | 1 | Single-column clean text | OCREngine, PDFAssembler |
53: | `multi-page.pdf` | 3 | Pages with different content | PipelineOrchestrator, PageExtractor |
54: | `two-column.pdf` | 1 | Two-column layout | ImageSegmenter (segmentation) |
55: | `with-annotations.pdf` | 3 | Bookmarks + text/underline annotations | MetadataPreserver |
56: | `noisy-scan.pdf` | 1 | Speckle noise + lighting gradient | ImageSegmenter (preprocessing) |
57: 
58: ## TDD Workflow Per Phase
59: 
60: ### Phase 1 — Foundation (CLI + scaffold)
61: 1. `TrulyFreeOCRTest` — parse `--help`, `--version`, basic arg parsing via picocli
62: 2. `PlatformUtilsTest` — OS detection returns expected values
63: 3. `SubprocessRunnerTest` — runs a known command, checks stdout/stderr/exit code
64: 
65: ### Phase 2 — PageExtractor
66: 1. **Write**: `PageExtractorTest`
67:    - `blank.pdf` → 1 page, dimensions match
68:    - `multi-page.pdf` → 3 pages, each returns a non-null `BufferedImage`
69:    - Invalid path → throws descriptive exception
70: 2. **Implement**: `PageExtractor.java`
71: 
72: ### Phase 3 — OCREngine
73: 1. **Write**: `OCREngineTest`
74:    - `simple-text.pdf` → returns `PageResult` with word-level `TextBlock` list
75:    - Known word "quick" appears in results
76:    - `blank.pdf` → empty result
77: 2. **Implement**: `OCREngine.java`
78: 
79: ### Phase 4 — ImageSegmenter
80: 1. **Write**: `ImageSegmenterTest`
81:    - Takes `BufferedImage` → returns `SegmentedImage` with mask + bg
82:    - `two-column.pdf` → mask separates both columns
83:    - `noisy-scan.pdf` → denoised output
84: 2. **Implement**: `ImageSegmenter.java`
85: 
86: ### Phase 5 — PDFAssembler (basic)
87: 1. **Write**: `PDFAssemblerTest`
88:    - Reconstructs PDF from components
89:    - Output PDF is searchable (text can be extracted)
90:    - Output PDF has same dimensions as input
91: 2. **Implement**: `PDFAssembler.java`
92: 
93: ### Phase 6 — JBIG2Compressor
94: 1. **Write**: `JBIG2CompressorTest`
95:    - Falls back gracefully when jbig2enc is not on PATH
96:    - Compresses a known binary mask
97: 2. **Implement**: `JBIG2Compressor.java`
98: 
99: ### Phase 7 — PDFAssembler (JBIG2)
100: 1. **Extend**: `PDFAssemblerTest`
101:    - Output with JBIG2 is smaller than uncompressed version
102:    - Visual fidelity preserved
103: 
104: ### Phase 8 — MetadataPreserver
105: 1. **Write**: `MetadataPreserverTest`
106:    - `with-annotations.pdf` → bookmarks preserved in output
107:    - Annotations count matches
108: 2. **Implement**: `MetadataPreserver.java`
109: 
110: ### Phase 9 — PipelineOrchestrator
111: 1. **Write**: `PipelineOrchestratorTest` (integration)
112:    - End-to-end on `simple-text.pdf` → produces searchable output
113:    - Output file exists and is valid PDF
114: 2. **Implement**: `PipelineOrchestrator.java`
115: 
116: ## Running Tests
117: 
118: ```bash
119: # All unit tests (fast)
120: $TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar test
121: 
122: # Integration tests only
123: $TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar test --tests "*IT"
124: 
125: # A single test class
126: $TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar test --tests "*PageExtractorTest"
127: ```
128: 
129: ## Adding New Test PDFs
130: 
131: 1. Add a new function in `tests/generate_test_pdfs.py`
132: 2. Run it to regenerate
133: 3. Reference the file from your test class by path:
134:    ```java
135:    var pdf = new File("tests/test-files/my-new-file.pdf");
136:    ```

(End of file - total 136 lines)
</content>
```
